baseInformation.loreShort: Achilles, invincible warrior of the Greeks, led a thousand ships to Troy to avenge the theft of Helen by Prince Paris. Bathed as a child in the River Styx, his skin was made impervious, save for his heel. Achilles fought bravely until a feud with King Agamemnon led him to withdraw from battle. Hector, prince of Troy, seized the moment, but Achilles returned after his beloved Patroclus was killed in his place. Achilles killed Hector, but Paris, with divine guidance, struck Achilles in his heel, ending his life. Now drawn from Hades, Achilles seeks vengeance on the gods who stole his glory.
baseInformation.loreLong: King Agamemnon brought his fury to bear against gilded Troy, for Prince Paris had stolen his Helen, his wife, whose beauty rivaled that of Athena and Aphrodite. To famed Achilles, invincible warrior, the king gave command of a thousand ships. Across stormy seas and salted beach, soldiers sieged the city. Arrow and stone, blade and barb bounced from Achilles? skin. Bathed as a babe in the River Styx by his Nereid mother, his hide was hardened, imperviously made. Through every charge, every death-defying battle, Achilles was at the fore. Troy hung poised to crumble. Until Agamemnon gave slight to the mighty myrmidon. In grave offense, Achilles pulled his forces from the field. Hector, boldest, bravest, eldest of the Trojan princes seized the chance to push the Greeks to the sea. Water?s reflection mirrored scorching sails as Hector fired their ships. All seemed lost until Achilles rose to meet him. Fierce and fast the two titans fought, but Hector?s spear felled Achilles fair. Though Patroclus, it was, in the armor of Achilles, not Achilles who lay dead. Wrathful at the loss of his faithful lover, Achilles donned armor newly-made and challenged Hector alone. Spear and blade and amor rang, but Achilles could not be harmed. Hector, prince of Troy, died in battle that day. Paris, brother lost, tearful-eyed, let arrow loose, guided by divine envy. For there were Gods that could not suffer Achilles to survive. Straight and true the arrow flew and harpooned Achilles? heel, where his mother held him when submerged. The wound was deep, his weakness found, Achilles met his end. A decade thence, from Hades? depths, Achilles has been drawn. Armored now, upon the heel, revenge his only aim. For envious Gods stole from him his glory and his life. Now they tremble at the wrath of the man who cannot be harmed.
baseInformation.shortRole: Adapts With Power or Armor, Executes Weakened Enemies

basic.name: Achilles Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal <Damage>{Damage_Formula} Physical Damage</> to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Gift of the Gods
passive.shortDesc:
Choose to wear armor or forgo it. Wearing armor grants bonus Health and Protections, while forgoing it grants bonus Strength and Movement Speed.

 • Swap between armor states by using Basic Attacks while the Passive targeter is active inside the Fountain.

A01.name: Shield of Achilles
A01.shortDesc:
Punch forward with your shield, dealing [80% STR] Physical Damage and Stunning enemies in a short cone. The force of the shield radiates further, dealing reduced damage.
 • The radiated force deals 80% Damage.
 • Non-God targets take 115% Damage.
 • .2 seconds increased Stun duration when Armored, 15% increased Scaling when forgoing your Armor.

A02.name: Radiant Glory
A02.shortDesc:
You are blessed by the Gods, gaining bonus Strength, Protections, and 20-30% CCR for a short duration. Damaging enemies with abilities during this time Heals you.
• When Armored, the Gods' blessings also grant you a [75 HP + 20 Per Level] Physical Shield.
      • When forgoing your Armor, hitting an enemy god with your attacks from behind reduces their Protections.
A02.shortDescAspect:
You are blessed by the Gods, gaining bonus Strength for a short duration. Damaging enemies with abilities during this time Heals you.
 • When Armored, the Gods’ blessings also grant you a [50 HP + 10% STR] Physical Shield.
      • When forgoing your Armor, hitting an enemy god with your attacks from behind reduces their Protections.

A03.name: Combat Dodge
A03.shortDesc:
Dodge in your current direction and ready your spear to strike enemies for [45% STR] Physical Damage. Hitting an enemy god with the strike allows you to recast the ability once before it goes on cooldown.

 • When Armored, striking an enemy god with your spear Slows them, stacking up to 2 times.
      • When forgoing your Armor, the speed of your dodge is doubled.

A04.name: Fatal Strike
A04.shortDesc:
Prepare briefly, then Dash forward, dealing [100% STR] Physical Damage to enemies and Executing gods that are below 35% health. Killing a god allows you to recast this ability up to five times.

 • Each kill exposes your heel, increasing your damage taken by 5%.

tips.title: Tips
tips.value: Go no armour in the early game to allow for better clearing/trading potential. Around level 15 you can equip your armour, or whenever you're starting to rotate to fights.

aspect.description: Combat Dodge can now be recast after striking a Jungle Monster or Boss, with Spear Strike gaining increased Scaling when forgoing your Armor. Radiant Glory no longer grants increased Protections or Crowd Control Reduction.

baseInformation.loreShort: In all realms, fire is both feared and cherished. Agni, twin of thunderous Indra, is both purifier and destroyer, the bearer of divine messages through sacred flames. His fires can warm and enlighten, yet they burn with ferocity, cleansing the unworthy. Beloved by mortals, feared by gods, he strides forward, flames licking away corruption, shielding those who stand beside him. Beware, for Agni's dual nature is unyielding—whether to preserve or consume, only he decides.
baseInformation.loreLong: There are few elements as destructive or as purifying as fire. Agni, God of Fire, is the embodiment of both of these qualities, with a head for each. Though the source of his origin warrants debate - for there are many tales of his parentage ranging from two simple sticks rubbed together, to the cosmic energy that made all things at the beginning of time - Agni is a pivotal and important God with many duties to the Pantheon. He is the twin brother to Indra, God of the Heavens and Rains and chief among warriors. Conversely, Agni is chief among priests, acting as messenger between mortals and Gods. Every Hindu ritual and prayer is performed in front of a fire of some kind, so Agni carries the words and sacrifices, traveling between the Earth and the Heavens. He is welcome in every home and every hearth and much beloved by the Faithful. Through his flames, Agni provides heat and light, but also cleanses impurities. Smoke from his pyres create the air and hold the Heavens aloft. The sun, a source of fire itself, brings life-giving energy to the world, and his lightning streaks the sky during storms. For all his kindness and service, Agni has two faces. One is the face of kindness and purity, turned towards the people and Gods. His other face, grim and resolute, guides the God of Fire, to play his role in the cosmic cycle of creation and destruction, to burn and blacken all the atrocities of the world to ash.
baseInformation.shortRole: Summons Fire Waves, Rains Meteors On Enemies

basic.name: Anubis Basic Attack

passive.name: Combustion
passive.shortDesc: Hitting an enemy with a Basic Attack provides 1 stack of Combustion. Hitting an enemy god provides 2. At 4 stacks Agni's next Flame Wave or Rain Fire will ignite all enemies hit, dealing [6% INT] Magical Damage every 0.5s for 3s. When Agni kills an enemy god, he goes on a Hot Streak. When on a Hot Streak, Combustion is fully charged and using Flame Wave or Rain Fire does not consume Combustion.

Flame Wave and Rain of Fire consume ignites dealing a burst of [30 + 70% STR] damage. Rain of Fire has 45% reduced INT Scaling. [65% INT -> 20% INT] For every stack of Ignite, Combustion deals 25% more damage.

A01.name: Noxious Fumes
A01.shortDesc:
Summon a cloud of noxious fumes which deals [5% INT] Magical Damage every second. Hitting the fumes with any of your other abilities detonates the gas, Stunning and dealing [20% INT] Magical Damage to enemies in the area.

  • Fumes last for 10 seconds. Applies 25% Healing Reduction.

A02.name: Flame Wave
A02.shortDesc:
Summon a wave of fire, dealing [65% INT] Magical Damage. 

  • Ignites Noxious Fumes

A03.name: Path of Flames
A03.shortDesc:
Dash forward and leave a trail of fire behind you. Enemies that enter the area take [15% INT] Magical Damage every .5s for 2s. 

  • Flame trail lasts for 3s 

 • Ignites Noxious Fumes

A04.name: Rain Fire
A04.shortDesc:
Summon a meteor, dealing [60% INT] Magical Damage. You can have up to 3 charges of this ability. 

  • Ignites Noxious Fumes

baseInformation.loreShort: From a resourceful street urchin scraping by with his mother, to a Sultan guided by powerful Genies, Aladdin's journey spans poverty, deception, magic, and unexpected fortune.
baseInformation.loreLong:
From a resourceful street urchin scraping by with his mother, to a Sultan guided by powerful Genies, Aladdin's journey spans poverty, deception, magic, and unexpected fortune. Once an aimless youth known more for his misdeeds than his ambition, Aladdin found himself caught in the web of a cunning sorcerer. Disguised as a caring uncle, this sorcerer promised Aladdin wealth if he would retrieve a mysterious Lamp from a dangerous, trap-filled cave. Expecting the young man to meet his end, the sorcerer underestimated Aladdin's resilience – and the power of a lesser Genie hidden within a simple ring he provided.

Through this Genie's aid, Aladdin not only survived but secured the Lamp and the powerful Genie within it. That moment set him on a path from impoverished mischief-maker to a leader of his people, gaining wealth, influence, and ultimately earning his place as the Sultan.

In Smite 2, Aladdin has reached this pinnacle. Surrounded by loyal followers drawn to his optimism and charisma, he rules with the Lamp's Genie at his side. Yet uncertainty looms. Whether it's the sorcerer's machinations or the watchful eyes of the gods, Aladdin will face these trials head-on. His past has prepared him to make the most of any opportunity, and the Genies ensure he won't face the struggle alone.
baseInformation.shortRole: Fights Alongside His Genie, Traps Enemies in his Lamp.

basic.name: Aladdin Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal Physical Damage</> to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Three Wishes
passive.shortDesc:
Make 3 wishes at any time in the match. Gain up to 1 charge of Genie's Strength, every 10s, -1s CD for each enemy slain, or 5s for gods slain. 
			• Wish: Wealth. God Kills and Assists grant 33 more gold and gain 7.5% Bonus Gold from all sources for 180s. 
			• Wish Immortality. While dead, wish to be instantly revived. Gain 75% Movement Speed for 10s when leaving the fountain 
			• Wish Power. Reset ability and item cooldowns. Gain 1 charge of Genie's Strength.

A01.name: Kufic Invocation
A01.shortDesc:
Fire 5 Kufic symbols that deal [45% INT and 35% STR] Magical Damage to enemies in a cone.
 • When fully charged the symbols merge dealing [72% INT and 63% STR] Magical Damage. (Calculated here fully charged)
 • When fully charged the symbol travels further and hits a larger area 
 • Use a charge of Genie's Strength to send symbols back to Aladdin, dealing [40% INT and 35% STR] Magical Damage when small or [72% INT and 63% STR] Magical Damage when large (Calculated here fully charged)

A02.name: Sultan's Grace
A02.shortDesc:
Dash forward dealing [65% INT and 65% STR] Magical Damage to enemies you pass through. 
• Deals [0.5% + [0.13% INT]] increased damage to enemies for each % Health below 50% 
 • Use a charge of Genie's Strength to summon the Genie to unleash a flurry of 5 punches that deal [20% INT and 35% STR] Magical Damage to enemies in front of Aladdin

A03.name: Agile Run
A03.shortDesc: Dash forward. Dashing into a wall causes you to run along it before leaping to a location, dealing [70% INT and 50% STR] Magical Damage to enemies in the landing area.  • Use a charge of Genie's Strength to call the Genie to slam down at the landing location. Enemies take [20% INT and 40% STR] Magical Damage and are knocked straight up  • Genie Knockback now knocks enemies away from the landing location.

A04.name: Into The Lamp
A04.shortDesc:
Throw the Lamp forward, stopping on first enemy god hit and dealing [90% INT + 65% STR] Magical Damage. If successful, Aladdin and the enemy god are pulled into the lamp to challenge each other. 
 • Aladdin's non-ultimate cooldowns are reset. The target's non-ultimate cooldowns are halved 
 • The Lamp is left behind, allowing any god to enter the lamp and join the ongoing challenge.
 • The fight lasts for 16s, after which everyone is kicked out of the lamp. An escape lamp appears at 8s into a fight.
 • The escape can open early when either the main Aladdin and fight target (hereby referred to as combatants) die, or if only 1 team remains in the lamp.
 • Entering the lamp or leaving the lamp requires interacting with the lamp. This interaction takes 0.5s and is interrupted by taking damage.
 • Non-combatants are now ephemeral and deal reduced damage but take reduced damage.
 • Being kicked out of the lamp interrupts any currently firing ability.
 • Gods will be returned to the original locations and camera rotations when leaving the lamp.
 • Recalling and Aladdin's A04 are blocked from being used while inside the lamp.
 • Gain 35% of Aladdin's Max Health as a shield. Enemies also gain a shield equal to 35% of Aladdin's Max Health.**
 • Cooldown is increased by 30s upon a successful transfer to the lamp.

builds.author: Mytharria
builds.notes: STR-oriented jungle build. (sell lamp after all wishes are used/when you're full build for Timelock-Aegis)

tips.title: Wish Tips
tips.value: Do NOT waste your wish of immortality early game. Use your genie's strength mid-late game to empower your 3 to give you a knockup to confirm your poly into your 1 - 2 combo.

tips.title: Double Genie Punch
tips.value: To maximize damage output, use your 2nd ability, just the dash portion, Lamp your target resetting your cooldowns, and then dash immediately in the lamp. This will allow you to press your Genie's Strength for your 2 TWICE, giving you double genie punches. The damage output that comes from this technique is ABSURD.

baseInformation.loreShort: Radiant Amaterasu, goddess of the Sun, beloved beacon of hope and light. Even the sun knows shadows, for her brother Susano's envy cast her into despair. Hidden away, her light faded until, with laughter of friends, she returned, vowing never to dim again. Her people once suffered her absence, but now she rises to defend them. As war threatens to swallow creation in darkness, Amaterasu shines like the dawn, ready to cast brilliance upon the unworthy and guide the faithful through the storm—even if it leads to her brother's end.
baseInformation.loreLong: As the first rays of dawn cross the horizon, before all else they touch the land of the rising sun. A realm of islands painted with the careful brush strokes of its deities. That morning light, which brings warmth and life is Amaterasu, the benevolent Goddess of the sun. Universally, she is adored. Less so are her brothers, namely Susano-o, bringer of storms, whose jealousy of his sister?s popularity drove him to burn her golden rice fields and shatter the sacred looms of her people. Grief stricken at the destruction, Amaterasu sealed herself inside a deep cavern. Her people suffered and the realm fell into crushing darkness. For an unknown time, she stayed there, hidden away from the atrocities of the world. Until, one day, came a thumping from outside. At first, she tried to ignore it, but curiosity called. Moving aside the great stone sealing the cavern, she peered cautiously out, only to stare into a polished bronze mirror. Blinded by her own reflection, Amaterasu was forced to creep further out to find the source of the sound. Hands grabbed her from either side and pulled her free of the cave. Gathered around were hundreds of friends and loved ones. Atop a wide tub danced Usume, Goddess of Mirth, creating the erratic drumming to the joy of all. Amaterasu smiled and light came back to the world. Seeing all those that depended on her, all those that loved her, Amaterasu vowed never again to hide away from calamity. She would, instead, face it, just as the dawn rises each new day.
baseInformation.shortRole: Empowers Allies With Auras, Slices and Stuns Enemies

basic.name: Amaterasu Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal  Physical Damage</> to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Illuminating Strike
passive.shortDesc:
Basic attack or hit an enemy with Dazzling offensive 3 times to illuminate their weaknesses, causing them and other nearby enemies to take more damage from all sources. 
• Damage increase: 10%

A01.name: Divine Presence
A01.shortDesc:
Heal yourself for [26% INT] over 4s, and alternate between the Gold and Red buff effects. 
			
	  • Gold buff provides a Health Shield for 6s on activation, and a persistent Movement Speed Buff 
	  • Red buff provides an Attack Speed buff for 6s on activation, and a persistent [5% INT] Strength and [3% INT] Intelligence Buff

A02.name: Heavenly Reflection
A02.shortDesc:
Charge your Mirror for 5s and decrease all damage you take while charging by [5% INT]. Reactivate the ability to fire a projectile that deals [60% STR + 60% INT] Physical Damage to enemies, increased by the Mirror's charge amount. 
		

  • Deal and take damage to charge the Mirror 

  • Base Damage is doubled when Mirror is fully charged (Automatically calculated as fully charged)

A03.name: Glorious Charge
A03.shortDesc:
Silence enemies in front of you for 1s, then dash forward and deal [60% STR] Physical Damage.  
		

  • Pierces through and damages minions but stops on first god hit

A04.name: Dazzling Offensive
A04.shortDesc:
Become CC Immune and then attack 3 times to deal [45% STR + 45% INT] Physical Damage to enemies in front of you each time. 

 • Attacks deal more damage and have bonus effects on each subsequent successful hit 

 • 2nd hit deals 1.2x damage and Slows by 30% for 2s 

  • 3rd hit deals 1.4x damage and Stuns for 1.5s

tips.title: Tips
tips.value: Stay in your red stance while fighting/clearing. Be sure to auto one minion three times to apply the increased damage aura before dashing through wave to 2 to ensure your 2 full clears.

aspect.description: Divine Presence now defaults to Red Buff, and when activated you gain Movement Speed, Haste, Lifesteal, and Basic Attack Power in addition to the original effects. It no longer alternates to Gold Buff, or Heals you when activated.
aspect.name: **__Aspect Of Valor__**

baseInformation.loreShort: Fierce Anhur, lion-headed God of War, wields cunning and strength, both swift as a spear's strike. With his sister Bastet, he defends Ra's journey against the serpent Apep. Yet even gods may love, and Anhur's heart led him to Menhet, whom he captured and claimed as his bride. Now, armed with both skill and valor, he leads the charge against tyranny, a weapon to the oppressed, a terror to the unjust.
baseInformation.loreLong: Anhur, the lion-headed Egyptian God of war, slays his enemies with spear and guile. Weapons equally as sharp!As a son of Ra, Anhur, and his sister Bastet, rode in the golden barge their father sailed across the sky each day. They protected the ship at dawn and dusk from the world serpent, Apep, who lurked at the horizon. Courageous, formidable, and above all, intelligent, Anhur defended the vessel with his spear and wits, outsmarting and distracting the serpent until feral Bastet delivered a killing blow. With Apep gone, Ra became Pharaoh, Bastet, the Goddess of Cats and Women, and Anhur donned the mantle of the God of War. This is how he became known as the Slayer of Enemies. As time went on, another feline Goddess, Menhet, ran away from the Pantheon, spurning Ra. Anhur vowed to bring her back from the distant lands of Nubia to appease his father, but when he finally caught up with Menhet, he was enraptured and fell in love. He tied a rope to her neck and led her home, where he requested Menhet become his wife, a boon Ra happily granted. For Egyptians, warfare is more than bloody battle, it's a means to break the chains of tyranny. Anhur can be found at the head of any charge, crushing corruption and leading the people to freedom. While his spear is deadly, Anhur is cunning and clever, able to outmaneuver opponents in ways they least expect.
baseInformation.shortRole: Hurls Spears, Pinning Enemies Against Walls

basic.name: Anhur Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals  Physical Damage</> to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Enfeeble
passive.shortDesc: Dealing Damage to enemies reduces their Physical Protection.

A01.name: Shifting Sands
A01.shortDesc:
Summon an obelisk, surrounded by shifting sands.

  • Enemies in the sands are Slowed and take additional damage from your basic attacks
  • The obelisk acts as a Wall

A02.name: Impale
A02.shortDesc:
Throw a large spear, dealing [90% STR] Physical Damage in a line.

  • The first God hit is Pushed
  • If the God hits a Wall, they are Stunned

A03.name: Disperse
A03.shortDesc:
Leap forward, dealing [75% STR] Physical Damage where you land.

  • Knock Back all enemies from your landing point

A04.name: Desert Fury
A04.shortDesc:
Hurl a series of empowered spears, each dealing [45% STR] Physical Damage.

  • The final spear deals a burst of [90% STR] Physical Damage
  • These spears pass through everything
  • You are CC Immune while using this ability

tips.title: Tips
tips.value: The pillar + impale combo looks sick, but that's about it. It's better to just place it for the slow/basic damage increase than for the stun combo as most times you'll end up just pushing them out of auto range.

aspect.description: Enfeeble no longer Debuffs enemies but dealing damage to the same enemy god with Basic Attacks provides you up to 100% bonus Attack Speed. Dealing basic attack damage to another enemy god immediately resets all stacks.

baseInformation.loreShort: Eternal judge of souls, Anubis, whose jackal's gaze sees all truths. Born of shadow and light, Anubis honored his father Osiris through the sacred rites of mummification, a gift he granted to mortals. Weighing each heart against Ma'at, Anubis casts the unworthy into oblivion. As gods vie and the realm of the dead trembles, Anubis steps forth, ready to render judgment upon all—even the gods—to see who remains worthy of existence.
baseInformation.loreLong: Jackal-headed Anubis holds the ultimate judgment over the dead, measuring every heart against the weight of Truth. Nephthys, wife of Set - the God of Darkness, desired a child, yet her husband was infertile, so Nephthys disguised herself as Eset, the wife of Set's brother Osiris, and seduced him. From that union, Anubis was born. Osiris cared for Anubis as his own, but when Osiris was murdered by his evil brother, Set, Anubis embalmed his adopted father and mummified the corpse so he would not rot. This preservation passed down to the Faithful, for if it was good enough for the Gods, it was good enough for man. None enter the abyss of the Underworld without first being tested by Anubis. When corpses are preserved, he is the embalmer. When time for judgment comes, he is the final arbiter. In his realm, he keeps legendary weighing scales. On one side, the massive weight of Ma'at - truth and order. On the other side, he places the heart of the deceased. Should the weight of Ma'at prove infinitely heavier than that of the heart, Anubis casts the soul deep into the darkness of the Underworld, where it is forgotten, never to be reborn.
baseInformation.shortRole: Mummifies Enemies and Channels Death Magic

basic.name: Anubis Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals  Magical Damage to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: The Scales
passive.shortDesc: Anubis starts the game with 2% Lifesteal and 10 Protections. Gain additional Bonus Lifesteal and Protections scaled from missing health. Caps at +6% Lifesteal and 20 Protections at 20% Health.

A01.name: Plague Of Locusts
A01.shortDesc:
Locusts fly from your mouth, repeatedly dealing [18-22% INT] Magical Damage in an area in front of you.

  • This is a Channeled ability
  • You are Displacement Immune and move at 35% Movement Speed while Channeling

A02.name: Mummify
A02.shortDesc:
Fire a bandage projectile, mummifying and Stunningthe first god hit.

  • This ability deals [55% INT] Magical Damage, only to gods

A03.name: Grasping Hands
A03.shortDesc:
Summon allies from the underworld, dealing [35% INT] Magical Damage repeatedly to enemies in an area.

   • This also Slows enemies in the area

A04.name: Death Gaze
A04.shortDesc:
Your piercing gaze burns enemies in a line.

  • This deals an initial burst of [90% INT] Magical Damage
  • Then Channels to repeatedly deal [20% INT] Magical Damage (24 times)
  • You are CC Immune while using this ability
  • The Scales is always at full strength during this Ultimate

builds.author: Mytharria
builds.notes: INT-oriented burst mage build with lifesteal

tips.title: Tips
tips.value: Similar to Ra, in late game teamfights/objectives try to hide behind walls or bushes. Especially if your teammates are setting up this can lead to situations where unaware enemies can be quickly bursted by your 3 + 4 combo over a wall. (no, your stun doesn't go over walls)

baseInformation.loreShort: Aphrodite, the Goddess of Beauty, is as breathtaking as she is lonely, seeking solace where none remains. Her allure sparked the Trojan War, yet now she hopes to quell conflict rather than fan its flames. Adorned with grace, she steps onto the battlefield not to bring destruction, but to seek a beauty far deeper than appearance—a peace woven from within, born of her truest self. She knows all too well how to bend the wills of men, but now her heart is set on controlling the hearts of the gods as well.
baseInformation.loreLong: There are too few words to describe the radiance, the magnificence, the inequitable glory of Aphrodite, Goddess of Beauty. So wondrous is she to behold that one look can inspire tears, desire, jealousy, and love. Yet, beneath her flawless exterior resides a heart burdened with insecurity. Inattentive and hideous to look upon, her husband, Hephaestus, unknowingly leaves Aphrodite feeling lonely and worthless. She finds consolation in the arms of other men such as Ares, or validation by competing in any contest of beauty she encounters. At a wedding attended by all the Gods, a golden apple appeared, inscribed with the words to the fairest.  Immediately Aphrodite, her mother Hera, and sister Athena quarreled over it. Zeus elected Paris, Prince of Troy, to choose a victor. In exchange for being named the fairest, Aphrodite offered any woman in the world as his wife, so Paris agreed. However, he chose Helen as his wife, and their love began the catastrophic Trojan War. Now, a new war brews, but this one between Gods, not men. While the battlefield is no place for the fairest of all, Aphrodite recalls the destruction between the Greeks and Trojans. Perhaps, this time, her beauty can be used to stop a war instead of start one. Perhaps, this time, she will discover the beauty within.
baseInformation.shortRole: Links to Allies, Providing Healing and Temporary Invulnerability

basic.name: Aphrodite Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals  Magical Damage</> to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Center of Attention
passive.shortDesc: Gain Physical Protection and Magical Protection for each friendly or enemy god nearby.

A01.name: Kiss
A01.shortDesc:
Fire a kiss projectile at an allied god to link a Soul Mate, giving you both increased movement speed, and reduce Kiss's cooldown. If the kiss hits an enemy god, they take [25% INT] Magical Damage, are Stunned and your Soul Mate becomes Jealous, increasing their damage dealt. 

   • The Soul Mate gains 50% of your MP5 and 11% of your Protections.

A02.name: Back Off!
A02.shortDesc:
Deal [90% INT] Magical Damage, Slow, and Push away enemies around you. 

 • If you have a Soul Mate, Deal [60% INT] Magical Damage and slow enemies around them as well. If an enemy is hit by both areas, they take [85%] reduced damage from the 2nd area.

A03.name: Love Birds
A03.shortDesc:
Fire a doves projectile that deals [17.5% INT] Magical Damage repeatedly over time to enemies hit. (7 ticks) 

   • On fire, Aphrodite and her Soul Mate start Healing repeatedly for [+3.5% INT Self, +2.5% INT Ally] over time, and have their active cooldowns decreased per tick.

A04.name: Undying Love
A04.shortDesc:
You and your Soul Mate become Damage Immune, CC Immune, and gain the Jealousy effect for a short duration. 

   • Reactivate to Leap to your Soul Mates location.

tips.title: Kissing/Linking Tips
tips.value: Try to switch partners every few seconds during fights/objectives to provide Jealousy to your allies. This will give your team a massive DPS increase. Also try to make sure you're casting your 3 BEFORE ulting to get value of out the CD reset.

tips.title: Ulting Tips
tips.value: Before fights/before the game starts, try to identify what abilities/ultimates you'll be immuning with your ultimate. This will give you a lot more value out of your immunity.

baseInformation.loreShort: apollo, invincible warrior of the Greeks, led a thousand ships to Troy to avenge the theft of Helen by Prince Paris. Bathed as a child in the River Styx, his skin was made impervious, save for his heel. apollo fought bravely until a feud with King Agamemnon led him to withdraw from battle. Hector, prince of Troy, seized the moment, but apollo returned after his beloved Patroclus was killed in his place. apollo killed Hector, but Paris, with divine guidance, struck apollo in his heel, ending his life. Now drawn from Hades, apollo seeks vengeance on the gods who stole his glory.
baseInformation.loreLong: King Agamemnon brought his fury to bear against gilded Troy, for Prince Paris had stolen his Helen, his wife, whose beauty rivaled that of Athena and Aphrodite. To famed apollo, invincible warrior, the king gave command of a thousand ships. Across stormy seas and salted beach, soldiers sieged the city. Arrow and stone, blade and barb bounced from apollo? skin. Bathed as a babe in the River Styx by his Nereid mother, his hide was hardened, imperviously made. Through every charge, every death-defying battle, apollo was at the fore. Troy hung poised to crumble. Until Agamemnon gave slight to the mighty myrmidon. In grave offense, apollo pulled his forces from the field. Hector, boldest, bravest, eldest of the Trojan princes seized the chance to push the Greeks to the sea. Water?s reflection mirrored scorching sails as Hector fired their ships. All seemed lost until apollo rose to meet him. Fierce and fast the two titans fought, but Hector?s spear felled apollo fair. Though Patroclus, it was, in the armor of apollo, not apollo who lay dead. Wrathful at the loss of his faithful lover, apollo donned armor newly-made and challenged Hector alone. Spear and blade and amor rang, but apollo could not be harmed. Hector, prince of Troy, died in battle that day. Paris, brother lost, tearful-eyed, let arrow loose, guided by divine envy. For there were Gods that could not suffer apollo to survive. Straight and true the arrow flew and harpooned apollo? heel, where his mother held him when submerged. The wound was deep, his weakness found, apollo met his end. A decade thence, from Hades? depths, apollo has been drawn. Armored now, upon the heel, revenge his only aim. For envious Gods stole from him his glory and his life. Now they tremble at the wrath of the man who cannot be harmed.
baseInformation.shortRole: Harness Melodic Might, Take to the Skies in your Chariot

baseInformation.loreShort: War incarnate, Ares strides into battle with a brutal grin, the chaos of war raging in his wake. Scorned by his father Zeus, shunned by his sister Athena, yet adored by those who revere strength above all. His worshipers know his strength and the firey spirit that rests within his soul. The Spartans bound his statues in chains, seeking to contain his fury within their city's walls. But now, unleashed, his wrath flows unchecked, a force feared by gods and mortals alike.
baseInformation.loreLong: Feared by his enemies, reviled by his father, adored by the Goddess of Beauty, and worshipped by warriors, Ares, a tortured and lonely soul, is the bloodthirsty Greek God of War. Ares is not loved by the people. He represents everything they fear and despise: war, strife, chaos, and murder. It?s said that the sky darkens when Ares takes the field. That he rides to battle on a chariot drawn by fire-breathing steeds and delights only in the clash of battle and smell of bloodshed. Ares is not loved by his father. Zeus has many sons, but only two with his wife Hera. However, the thunder God has long suspected Hera of having an affair that led to the birth of Ares, perhaps as revenge for all of Zeus? infidelity. For this, Zeus is disgusted by him. Ares is not loved by his sister. Athena and Ares are both Gods of War. Where Ares represents violence, force, and slaughter, Athena is the embodiment of intelligence, strategy, and leadership. Ares is quick to act and leave a bloody trail. Athena is thoughtful and careful with lives. Often they have been on the opposite sides, most notably at the Battle of Troy, where Athena viciously wounded him and forced Ares to retreat. Ares is the lover of the Goddess of Beauty. Though Aphrodite is married to Ares? brother, Hephaestus, Ares takes what he wants, and Aphrodite gives in willingly. Their union created offspring almost as terrible as Ares himself: Phobos and Deimos, Fear and Terror. Ares is loved only by the Spartans, who revere the God for his masculinity, his merciless warrior spirit, and tenacity against all odds. The Spartans, however, know the unpredictable fury of their patron, and bind his statues in chains. This is, in part, to prevent Ares? spirit of victory from escaping Sparta, and a vain effort to keep the God of War contained.
baseInformation.shortRole: Chains Foes and Drags Them to Their Death

basic.name: Ares Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal  Magical Damage to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Blessed Armaments
passive.shortDesc:
Provide Strength and Intelligence in an Aura.

  • Build Protections to increase Strength Aura [8%] 
  • Build Cooldown Rate to increase Intelligence Aura [80%]
  • Both Auras increase with Level [1 Per Level]

A01.name: Shackles
A01.shortDesc:
Fire a Shackle projectile that deals [25% STR] Magical Damage and Slows.

  • You can refire after hitting an enemy god
  • Three Shackles may be out at one time
  • Gain Movement Speed for each Shackled god
  • Shackled gods take repeating [15% INT] Magical Damage and are Crippled 
 (Calculated as all 3 chains on 1 god, at max duration, so 8 ticks.)

A02.name: Call To Arms
A02.shortDesc:
You and nearby allied gods gain Magical and Physical Protections and Basic Attack Damage. You also gain HP/s while this buff is active.

  • Damaging enemy gods increases the duration and the effects of this buff 
  • The buff can be extended by 4 seconds upon damaging an enemy god three times. The buff effect is doubled when duration is extended.

A03.name: Searing Flesh
A03.shortDesc:
Flames pour from your shield, dealing [7% INT] Magical Damage repeatedly to enemies in front of you.

  • This is a Channeled ability, but you can fire Shackles
  • Deals bonus damage based on the enemies' maximum Health
  • You are Displacement Immune while Channeling
  • Applies 25% Healing Reduction

A04.name: No Escape
A04.shortDesc:
Attach a chain to nearby gods, then Pull the gods toward you, and finally Stun enemies around you.

  • Deal [30% STR] Magical Damage when Chains attach
  • Deal [55% STR] Magical Damage to nearby enemies while Stunning
  • You are CC Immune and gain Damage Mitigation while using this ability 
  • If a chained enemy uses any kinda of CC immunity/cleanse after having a chain attached to them, they immediately break the chain. You also cannot apply a chain to currently CC immune enemies. 
  • Any displacement immunity (Loki 3, Bacchus 3, etc.) will stop the pull from this ultimate.

tips.title: Tips
tips.value: Try to use your ultimate to pull targets that don't have easy access to CC immunity, like a Kukulkan or a Jungler with their beads down. Also remember that your ultimate CRIPPLES, so you can use it as a way to stop enemies from jumping out of your teammate's Vulcan ult, even it'll be useful even if they beads the actual pull of the ult.

aspect.description: Call to Arms no longer grants Protections or affects allies. Instead, Ares empowers his Shield for his next Basic Attack, stunning the target and spreading damage to nearby enemies.

baseInformation.loreShort: Artemis, twin of Apollo, Goddess of the Hunt and protector of the wild.
baseInformation.loreLong: Artemis, Goddess of the Hunt, is a force of precision and vengeance. Born alongside her twin Apollo under Hera's curse, she claimed her destiny as a protector of the wilds and a relentless pursuer of justice. She has humbled arrogant hunters, dispatched demigods with cunning traps, and punished all who dared dishonor her. Worshipper by hunters, midwives, and warriors, Artemis is both protector and punisher, a goddess whose arrows always find their mark. On the battlefield, her aim is clear, and her wrath, unstoppable.
baseInformation.shortRole: Lays Traps, Rains Arrows, and Controls the Hunt

basic.name: Anhur Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals  Physical Damage</> to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Still Target
passive.shortDesc: Artemis deals 15% increased Basic Attack Damage to gods that are affected by Crowd Control, and 30% increased Basic Attack Damage to non-gods affected by Crowd Control. She is also able to detect nearby enemy gods hidden in stealth grass.

A01.name: Transgressor's Fate
A01.shortDesc:
Place a trap on the ground. Enemy gods that come in contact with it activate it, Rooting, Crippling, and repeatedly dealing [30% STR] Physical damage per second for 3 seconds.

  • A max of 4 traps can be placed at the same time.

A02.name: Vengeful Assault
A02.shortDesc:
Greatly increase your Movement Speed and Attack Speed.

  • On activation, you are cleansed of Slows and become Slow Immune for 0.6s.
  • While the buff is active, gain a charge each time you hit an enemy god. At 4 charges, these charges are consumed to extend your buff duration by 3s. (This can occur infinite times)

A03.name: Suppress the Insolent
A03.shortDesc: Fire a volley at the target area, dealing [65% STR] Physical Damage and Slowing enemies.

A04.name: Calydonian Boar
A04.shortDesc:
Summon the great Calydonian Boar, charging at the closest enemy god, dealing [90% STR] Physical Damage and Stunning them.

  
  • The boar is immune until it hits its first god, then charges at remaining enemy gods. If none are left, it will begin to basic attack minions.
  • You are CC immune for 1.5s after using this ability.

tips.title: Lock-down Tips
tips.value: If you're non-aspect, try to immediately throw down a trap on enemies stunned by your ultimate, by the time the stun wears off, your trap will be armed, rooting them, giving you extra damage from your passive since they're CC'ed, and free time to auto away at them.

baseInformation.loreShort: Ice melts in cascading waterfalls from jagged mountain peaks, pouring into rivers roaring white to the cold sea.
baseInformation.loreLong:
Ice melts in cascading waterfalls from jagged mountain peaks, pouring into rivers roaring white to the cold sea. Straightening slowly, like aged men, trees and fauna of the underbrush, now free of snow-weight, reach for the sun. It is spring; the world awakens from darkness and death to grow green again. Her hibernation ends. Coat wet with fresh rain, Artio, Goddess-Bear, roars into the chill morning air.

She is guardian of the cycle. Not the passage of time, but the balance of things. There is no spring without winter, no death without life, no darkness without light, no goodness without evil. Nature declares these opposites into law and Artio is the enforcer.

Among the ursine she runs, sometimes in the shape of a woman, lithe and wild, sometimes as a bear, brown and fierce. Nowhere in the forest do there stand shrines in her name, for Artio is less worshipped and more respected. Perhaps, instead, she looms overhead, a constant presence in the twinkle of the stars, a connection of glistening light; the constellation Ursa Major.

And now there is war. None could know peace without war, victory without defeat, glory without failure. Artio must join those that fight if only to enforce the cycle of things. Nothing and no one defies the laws of nature like a God.

baseInformation.loreShort: Wise and vigilant Athena, daughter of Zeus, embodies honor and valor. While her brother Ares revels in carnage, Athena's hand steadies warriors who fight only when needed, seeking justice and wisdom over bloodshed. Her followers are measured, her teachings founded upon balance and reason. She defended Troy's siege with intellect, defying even family to maintain order. Now, as gods clash, mortals turn to Athena, praying for strength to survive with honor intact.
baseInformation.loreLong: In war, there are two kinds of soldiers: those that fight for blood, and those that fight for honor. The first crave the clash of steel and smell of slaughter, the latter know the wisdom of justice, the kindness of mercy, and that life should never be taken wantonly. These different warriors seek different Gods. Ares, God of War revels in the bloodletting, but his sister, Athena, Goddess of Wisdom, is patron to those who fight with dignity, and only when there is no other alternative. Favored daughter of Zeus, wise as ten thousand scholars, just as the scales themselves; Athena is a paragon of incorruptible virtue, and worshipped with righteous fervor. So revered is she that Athens, greatest city in all the world, is named for her, and regal temples adorn the countryside in her honor, each tended by loyal priestesses. To Athena, a life is a precious thing that need not be wasted in frivolous battle. Bloodthirsty Ares, on the other hand, is reviled by their father and chained by his ravenous subjects.At the Siege of Troy, Ares disobeyed Zeus and joined with the battle, fighting for the mortal Trojans. Athena rose to stop him, indirectly championing the Greeks, but directly keeping the order of divine law. Their fierce battle clashed in unspeakable proportions, ending only when Ares limped from the battle field, cowed by his sister and rival. With Ares diminished, the tide of the war shifted and the heroes of the Greek Army toppled Troy. In times of war, prayers are sent to both Gods. Ares, for bloodstained victory, Athena for honor, valor, and the wisdom to fight with dignity. Make no mistake, these are times of war, and Ares has already answered the call to arms. Athena, ever cautious with lives, goes to battle without eagerness, but solemn necessity, for the Goddess of Wisdom knows honor is for the living. The dead have no need of it.

baseInformation.loreShort: Awilix, the Goddess of the Moon, commands the night with a silent authority, her Panther Suku always by her side.
baseInformation.loreLong: Awilix, the Goddess of the Moon, commands the night with a silent authority, her Panther Suku always by her side. Her light cuts through shadows, revealing paths for those who revere her and setting traps for those who oppose her. While the sun burns with unchecked intensity, Awilix's radiance soothes and guides, her power reflected in the prowling strength of her loyal companion. As war threatens to plunge even the night into chaos, Awilix and Suku emerge to defend their domain, proving that the moonlight belongs to them alone.
baseInformation.shortRole: Rides her Panther and Pulls Airborne Enemies to her Spear

basic.name: Achilles Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal <Damage>{Damage_Formula} Physical Damage</> to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Initiative
passive.shortDesc:
If you have not taken or dealt damage in the last 5s, gain 25% increased Basic Attack Power and Strength for 4s if you strike an enemy first. 
• The buff is extended by 2s if triggered by the leap from Summon Suku

A01.name: Summon Suku
A01.shortDesc: Mount your Panther Suku, gaining movement speed but decreased strafe speed. You may Leap off of Suku to deal [70% STR] Physical Damage in the target area.

A02.name: Feather Step
A02.shortDesc:
Flip over the last enemy you hit within 1.5s, Rooting, Crippling and dealing [45% STR] Physical Damage to them. The damage dealt is increased by 50% based on the next attack in your Basic Attack progression. (1/1.35/1.9) 
• If the next attack is your third attack, you also hit all enemies in the area around the target

A03.name: Moonlight Charge
A03.shortDesc:
Suku comes to Awilix's aid, rushing forward dealing [80% STR] Physical Damage to all enemies. If hit from the side or behind, enemies are also Knocked Up. 
• This ability can be used while riding Suku

A04.name: Gravity Surge
A04.shortDesc: Pull the closest enemy god to you that is either Knocked Up or Leaping. The target god takes [65% STR] Physical Damage and is Stunned when they land. You are then bathed in moonlight, gaining bonus Attack Speed, Basic Attack Power, and Strength, as well as protection against Roots, Slows, and Displacement for 6s.

tips.title: Featherstep Tip
tips.value: Remember that ANY of your abilities can mark a target for Featherstep's activation. So you can use Summon Suku's jump on an enemy, then immediately flip over them with Featherstep without Basic Attacking.

tips.title: Ultimate Tip
tips.value: If an enemy either doesn't have a jump or they're holding it, just use your ultimate as a buff since it's a LOT of strength and attack speed. Waiting for a jump that may never come is a sure way to either lose a trade or drop a kill.

baseInformation.loreShort: Ah, Bacchus! The god of revelry, who taught mortals to seek freedom in a wine-filled cup. Taught by his mentor Solinus, he spread the 'Mysteries of the Vine,' transforming the mundane into ecstatic liberation. Yet joy is fickle; Bacchus himself saw it turn dark. Take heed from King Midas, who learned that even the sweetest gift can curdle into despair. In war, Bacchus is no less potent—he brings a heady chaos, a jubilant anarchy, with every sip shared and every step danced, a god of both joy and its consequences.
baseInformation.loreLong: Eat, drink, and be merry, for tomorrow you may die. None adhere more vigorously to this creed than Bacchus, the Roman God of Wine, lewd behavior, and madness. After spending much of his formative years with his impressively intoxicated mentor Solinus, whom Bacchus loves like a father, the God of Wine is now a wanderer and teacher. He?s journeyed across all of Asia, through India, and finally back to Rome, spreading the ?Mysteries of the Vine,? a ritual involving incredible amounts of wine to lower inhibitions, freeing one from social constraints, oppression of gender, sexual taboos, and the stresses of life. Quite the successful ritual, if ever there was one. Much of this ritual seems to have come from Solinus, who is widely known to have vast, secret knowledge and the ability to predict the future, though only while completely drunk. The king Midas, covetous of this knowledge, captured Solinus, endeavoring to extract it by providing the scholar the finest food, drink, and entertainment he could muster. Bacchus finally tracked down his mentor in the court of Midas and discovered him well fed and cared for. Quickly, Midas lied, claiming Solinus had been lost and the king had taken him in. Seeing through the ruse, Bacchus offered the monarch a boon. Midas asked that anything he touched be turned to gold. Without hesitation, Bacchus granted this. He tested his new gift on a twig and stone and found them turned completely to gold. Elated, Midas called a feast, but found his food turned to gold in his hands and his daughter solidified at his touch. Starving and crazed, Midas begged Bacchus to remove the ?gift. ? Bacchus acquiesced, allowing the greedy king to wash his hands in the Pactolus river. As the golden touch left his hands, the river sands transformed to glittering gold. Of course, the product of continuous overindulgence in alcohol and deviant behavior, Bacchus could simply be insane.
baseInformation.shortRole: Gets Drunk and Burps Crowd Control

basic.name: Bacchus Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal  Magical Damage to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Drunk-O-Meter
passive.shortDesc:
As you become more Drunk, gain Strength, Intelligence, and Protections.

  • You become Tipsy at 30% Drunk
  • You become Smashed at 60%, doubling the buffs

A01.name: Chug
A01.shortDesc:
Take a drink, Healing, and becoming more Drunk.

  • You also gain increased Strength and Intelligence
  • Your next basic attack deals an additional [0-35% of Protections] Magical Damage

A02.name: Belly Flop
A02.shortDesc:
Leap forward to and slam into the ground belly first, Knocking Up enemies.

  • Deals [75% STR + 30% Protections] Magical Damage
  • If you are Tipsy, enemies are also Slowed

A03.name: Belch Of The Gods
A03.shortDesc:
Channel a big ol' burp to deal [17.5% INT] Magical Damage repeatedly to enemies in front of you. 

  • If you are Tipsy, the final hit will Stun
  • Reduces enemy Healing
  • You are Displacement Immune while Channeling

A04.name: Intoxicate
A04.shortDesc:
Smash your jug of wine, dealing [65% INT] Magical Damage and Intoxicating enemies around you.

  • If you are Smashed, gain Strength and Intelligence in anger over your lost wine

tips.title: CC-Chaining Tips
tips.value: If you can, or if you're fighting a god that can't interrupt your 3, try to stun with your burp BEFORE Belly Flopping as knockups aren't affected by DR (diminishing returns) which allows for maximized CC duration.

aspect.description: After using Chug, your inhand attacks cleave and deal bonus damage scaling with Strength [50% STR] instead of Protections, for a short duration. (This extra basic damage is BONUS damage, meaning it's not affected by any basic attack damage enchancers.)

baseInformation.loreShort: Death is not the end but a doorway to the liveliest of afterparties with Baron Samedi as your host. Laughter trails his every step, a jest always on his lips, yet he commands death with an authority few dare question. With top hat and cane, he invites mortals to dance their final dance, for only he knows what lies beyond. So, pour a drink and raise a glass—for in the Baron's world, death is the greatest adventure of all.
baseInformation.loreLong:
Death. If you think that it means the end, then allow me to introduce you to someone. You thought it was all over, but Baron Samedi is going to change your mind. The only fire here is in the water, so share a drink with him. Let the sights and sounds entrance you. Join the celebration, feel your pulse pounding so hard you?ve forgotten your heart stopped beating. Forget what you think you know. There is a life after death, and Baron Samedi will be your guide. So come along and let Baron Samedi show you the wonders of a whole new world, and that even a god of death can be the life of the party. After all, what have you got to lose?
God.Unlisted,Standing upon the edge of the wooded realm of Cernunnos, one can feel the invitation and the threat. ?Come and feast,? it calls. ?Nature provides an abundance of crops and wild game. ? Yet, the same voice turns dark. ?Treat everything with due respect,? it warns. ?This is not your home. ? And, should one peer intently enough through the shadowed branches, a figure can be seen, horned as a stag, clothed in moss and leaves, eyes burning like untended embers. Cernunnos is master here, and although he has much to offer, he will not tolerate greed.
baseInformation.shortRole: Spreads Hysteria and Puts Foes in a Coffin

basic.name: Baron Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals  Magical Damage to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Hysteria
passive.shortDesc:
Damage applies Hysteria, granting your abilities bonus effects when enough is applied. Your team can also purchase Baron's Brew from the item store.

  • Gods at Max Hysteria [70] deal 20% Reduced Damage to Baron Samedi

 • Baron's Brew can be thrown at enemies to apply Magical Damage and Hysteria

A01.name: Vivid Gaze
A01.shortDesc:
Summon skulls that deal [70% INT] Magical Damage in an X shape.

   • Deals a bonus 35% Magical Damage to enemies hit by the overlap (automatically applied here)

  • Hitting a god above 30 Hysteria applies a 20% + [5% Protections] Strength, Intelligence, and Attack Speed Reduction

A02.name: Consign Spirits
A02.shortDesc:
Call spirits that deal [80% INT] Magical Damage to enemies in an area.

   • Hitting a god Heals nearby allies for  3% + [5% CDR] Missing Health

  • Hitting a god above 30 Hysteria makes the heal apply a 25% Speed Buff and Slow Immunity

A03.name: Wrap It Up
A03.shortDesc:
Throw a snake that constricts enemy gods, Slowing them to a Root.

   • Constricted gods take [50% INT] Magical Damage over 1.75s

  • Minions take a tick of Magical Damage when the snake passes through them

  • Hitting a god above 30 Hysteria causes the snake to explode when it Roots, applying a Mesmerize to nearby enemies

A04.name: Life of the Party
A04.shortDesc:
Open a Coffin that Vortexes enemies towards Baron Samedi. Gods Vortexed into the Coffin take [70% INT] + (10% + [2.5% of protections]) of their Max Health as Magical Damage and are Stunned.

   • The Vortex deals [7% INT] Magical Damage every 0.5s

  • Gods above 30 Hysteria are Vortexed with more intensity
	  • This is being calculated as 10 Vortex ticks + the coffin slam damage.

tips.title: Tips
tips.value: Try to maximize hysteria on enemies before ulting to ensure they can't simply walk out or beads at the last second of the vortex. Also try to turn away at the last second to bait the enemy's aegis/talisman of purification if they have it before quickly turning back after these effects wear off.

aspect.description: Hysteria applied over max deals [1.5% INT] Magical Damage. Max Hysteria no longer reduces damage. Consign Spirits only heals Baron. Vivid Gaze bonus now grants Hysteria over time, but no debuff. Wrap It Up explosion and DoT apply Bonus Hysteria, but the explosion no longer Mesmerizes. Your personal Brew can be thrown every 20s.

baseInformation.loreShort: Bellona, fierce Goddess of War, as relentless as steel on stone. She fought alongside Rome's legions, urging heroes to rise, yet as peace grew, Bellona faded from memory. But war is eternal, and Bellona answers its call once more, her gaze now fixed upon the gods' battlefield. Armed with sword, whip, or hammer, she rises to rally those beside her and strike terror into those who dare oppose her. Her fury is never dormant—it merely awaits the call.
baseInformation.loreLong: Rome has forgotten her. Bellona, the Goddess of War, took no time to build temples or gather worshipers. She favors only those for whom war is life; for that is where she thrives - not on the steps of hallowed basilicas or in whispered prayers, but in the blood-soaked mud, among the breathless armored troops, in the roar of victory. When Rome was young, Bellona ran with her armies, conquered her enemies, made her strong. As Rome aged and began to crumble, she fought only with her strongest and most cunning of worshipers, Lucius Cornelius Sulla. Sulla rose through the ranks by accomplishing impossible deeds of heroism and ruthless prowess. His enemies feared him, his soldiers loved him, and everywhere he went, Bellona rode with him. Together they quelled the Germanic hordes, they broke the Social War, they sacked Athens. Sulla was utterly undefeatable, and it was the Goddess of War that made him so. But the Roman Senate moved to displace Sulla and end his rise to glory. March on Rome, Bellona quietly urged, and you shall rise as no other. Emboldened, Sulla commanded his legions and took the city streets, Bellona at the fore, slaughtering the gladiator-slaves that stood against them. The Senate buckled. They cast the vote. Sulla became the first life-long dictator of Rome. During Sulla's reign, Bellona was worshipped for the Goddess she was. But Sulla had grown old. His wars were over. Bellona moved on, and Rome forgot. But the forgotten are not gone. Gods clash in titanic conflict. It is in war she thrives. No one will forget Bellona the Goddess of War this time.
baseInformation.shortRole: Leaps Into Battle With Three Powerful Weapons

basic.name: Bellona Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc:
Deal  Physical Damage to an enemy in front of you. 

You have 4 different weapons to attack with, depending on the most recent ability used.

passive.name: Master Of War
passive.shortDesc:
When you hit or are hit by basic attacks, you gain stacks of Movement Speed.

  • If using Sword and Shield, you also get Protection stacks
  • If using Scourge, you also get Attack Speed stacks
  • If using Hammer, you also get Strength stacks

A01.name: Shield Bash
A01.shortDesc:
Dash forward with your shield to deal [50% STR + 30% Protections] Physical Damage and Slow enemies in front of you.

  • Gain 1 Block stack for each enemy god hit
  • Change to Sword and Shield basic attacks
  • Every third successful basic attack grants a Block stack, reflecting damage

A02.name: Bludgeon
A02.shortDesc:
Spin your hammer to deal [25% STR] Physical Damage to enemies around you, then slam to deal [60-70% STR] Physical Damage to enemies in a line.

  • Change to Hammer basic attacks
  • Your basic attacks now hit nearby enemies

A03.name: Scourge
A03.shortDesc:
Lash out with your Scourge to deal [50% STR] Physical Damage and Disarm enemies.

  • Change to Scourge basic attacks
  • Your basic attacks Heal you [10 + 6% Physical Protections] 
  • This is a healing ability.

A04.name: Eagle's Rally
A04.shortDesc:
Leap forward and plant your flag to deal [60% STR] Physical Damage and Stun enemies.

  • The flag empowers you and allied gods in the area, granting Strength, Intelligence, Physical Protection, and Magical Protection
  • You are CC Immune while Leaping

tips.title: Boxing Tips
tips.value: When starting a fight, especially vs another auto attacker, be sure to disarm, THEN use your 1 and box in your 1 stance since it gives block stacks you can use your hammer to finish off targets if needed.

aspect.description: Bludgeon no longer deals bonus Final Slam Damage but whenever you gain Block stacks with Sword and Shield or gain Healing from Scourge, nearby allied gods also gain those benefits.

baseInformation.loreShort: Cabrakan, Destroyer of Mountains, whose boastful pride thunders like a storm. A being born of fury, Cabrakan's every footstep shatters rock and rends mountains, his power as fearsome as the earth itself. Worshipers of Cabrakan are those drawn to raw power—the ambitious, the indomitable, those who see themselves as forces of nature. Warriors and builders alike revere him, hoping to channel his strength or, perhaps, his terrifying resilience. Now, awoken and ravenous for revenge, Cabrakan's footsteps quake with wrath, ready to tear down not only mountains but the gods who dare cross him.
baseInformation.loreLong: Feel the earth trembling beneath your feet? Only a titan of tremendous power could cause such fear. He is the Render of Stone, the Earthsunder. He is Cabrakan, Destroyer of Mountains. Proud, boastful, confident to the point of arrogance, Cabrakan revels in his strength, tearing down what the earth has labored to rise. These traits ? and worse - he inherited from his sire, the demon bird Seven Macaw. Seeking to end Seven Macaw's entire lineage, the Hero Twins tracked Cabrakan by his quaking footfalls. They found him in a valley, surrounded by great peaks, shattering rock into dust. How mighty you are, they told him, playing to his pride. I am, he boasted. Can you shatter that distant mountain? they asked. Of course, Cabrakan roared. You must be hungry, the Hero Twins offered. Always!And so they shot a bird from the sky for the giant to feast upon, yet as they turned the carcass over the flame, Hun-apu coated it with poisonous mud. Without a thought, Cabrakan devoured the feast and rose, but already the poison had taken him. Teetering, losing sight, the Earthsunder collapsed. Mountains crumbled a final time. The valley became his tomb. Yet vengeance is a cycle. Blood for blood, and a Titan is not so easily slain. The destroyer of mountains cannot be buried beneath stone, and Cabrakan's pained slumber is at an end. Bursting free, he now thunders across the field of war, seeking the Twins that played him the fool. Why stop at mountains when Cabrakan can destroy Gods.
baseInformation.shortRole: Creates Tremors and Fissures to Disrupt Enemies

basic.name: Cabrakan Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal <DamageMagical>{Damage_Formula} Magical Damage</> to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Shadow Zone
passive.shortDesc: You and nearby allies take reduced damage. Deal increased damage to enemies that damage you or your nearby allies. You also gain 1% Damage Mitigation per 100 Item Protections. (Not granted to allies)

A01.name: Seismic Crush
A01.shortDesc:
Become enraged, increasing your Movement Speed and Attack Speed. While active, your next successful Basic Attack will Stun and deal bonus [45% INT + 80% STR] Magical Damage.

 • Gain Haste and Immunity to Slows and Roots while this effect is active.

A02.name: Refraction Shield
A02.shortDesc:
Clash your shields, unleashing a concussive blast that deals [50% INT] Magical Damage. Taking damage charges your shields, granting Protections.

 • At Max Stacks, gain access to Enhanced Refraction Shield on a separate cooldown.
      • Enhanced Refraction Shield's concussive blast deals [70% STR] Magical Damage and Stuns enemies.
      • Restores Mana per enemy hit by Refraction Shield, or [0.75% Max Health] Health per enemy hit by Enhanced Refraction Shield.

A03.name: Tremors
A03.shortDesc:
Repeatedly slam the ground, Trembling and dealing [35% INT] Magical Damage to enemies every 0.5 seconds while pulling them toward you in a Vortex. (Calculated as 6 ticks for the calculator)

• Refire for a stronger quake that deals [50% STR] Magical Damage and Cripples enemies, and incurs an increased cooldown.

A04.name: Tectonic Shift
A04.shortDesc:
Stomp the ground to create a fissure that deals [60% INT  + 80% STR] Magical Damage to enemies in its path. From the fissure, five pieces of earth erupt to form a wall.

 • Refire the ability to destroy all pieces at once.

tips.title: Tips
tips.value: Since your first ability's stun isn't affected by any DR (Diminishing Returns), using it AFTER your 2nd ability's stun provides the highest duration of CC.

aspect.description: Seismic Crush Stealths you and Slows enemies instead of Stunning. Refraction Shield stacks energy by dealing damage and deals bonus damage instead of a Stun. Tremors spawns a vision dealing [30% INT + 15% STR] Magical Damage 8 times and then Slowing on the final hit, dealing [40% INT + 50% STR] Magical Damage. You teleport to a location before casting Tectonic Shift, each Wall having less Health.

baseInformation.loreShort: Cernunnos, the Horned God, lord of the untamed wild, is both hunter and guardian, benevolent and fierce. His realm calls to those who respect the balance of nature: farmers, hunters, and those who seek harmony over dominion. His worshipers find wisdom in the cycles of life and death, honoring the Sacred Wheel of seasons. Though he welcomes the humble to feast upon nature's gifts, those who take greedily face his wrath. To his followers, Cernunnos is both a blessing and a warning—he walks with them in the shadows of the forest, reminding them that they are visitors in his ancient domain.
baseInformation.loreLong:
Standing upon the edge of the wooded realm of Cernunnos, one can feel the invitation and the threat. ?Come and feast,? it calls. ?Nature provides an abundance of crops and wild game. ? Yet, the same voice turns dark. ?Treat everything with due respect,? it warns. ?This is not your home. ? And, should one peer intently enough through the shadowed branches, a figure can be seen, horned as a stag, clothed in moss and leaves, eyes burning like untended embers. Cernunnos is master here, and although he has much to offer, he will not tolerate greed. Time turns on the Sacred Wheel, Spring to Summer, Summer to Autumn and Autumn at last to Winter. 
Cernunnos was there at the shaping of the wheel, born of the womb of the All Mother before Gods were Gods, when the land was shapeless. This wheel is not of his making. He is both master of it and slave to it, transforming with the seasons like the world beneath his feet. He bears a heavy metal torc around his neck as a permanent pledge of loyalty to this cycle of life and death that only he can drive. Once, it was said, there was no break between his realm and this world. They were the same. Perhaps a time will come when the world shall once again be his. Then, all can walk among the whispering leaves. So long as they remain humble. For the arrogant, Cernunnos hunts. What are these other Gods? Guest or prey?
baseInformation.shortRole: Throws Glaives, Transforms Enemies Into Pigs

Smite2F2pOb1CrimsonstalkerSocial1920x108.name: Smite 2 F 2 P Ob 1 Crimsonstalker Social 1920 X 1080 Notag

basic.name: Cernunnos Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals  Physical Damage to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Heavy Glaive
passive.shortDesc: Basic attacks deal [8 + 1 Per Level] + [30% STR + 5% INT] bonus Physical Damage to enemies in melee range.

A01.name: Shifter Of Seasons
A01.shortDesc:
Activate to toggle between Spring, Summer, Fall, or Winter basic attack bonus effects.

  • Spring: Self Healing [10% INT] 
  • Summer: Bonus Damage [12%-24% INT or STR, whichever is higher]
  • Fall Protection Reduction [5-25%] + [5% INT] as flat reduction. 
  • Winter: Slow [2.5% INT] 
 (Change season stance then add Shifter of Seasons to switch stances)

A02.name: Bramble Blast
A02.shortDesc:
Fire a bramble that explodes on hit or upon reaching max range, dealing [75% STR + 50% INT] Physical Damage in an area.

  • This Roots a directly hit target

tips.value: Cernunnos' first ability is called Shifter of Seasons for a reason. They mimic the seasons of the real world, so press your stance switch ONCE while in Summer stance to switch to Fall, and THRICE to swap to Summer.

baseInformation.loreShort: One coin is all that it costs to enter the Elysian Fields.
baseInformation.loreLong: One coin is all that it costs to enter the Elysian Fields. One coin, no more, no less. That is the toll Charon expects. The Ferryman of Hades, is a simple being at heart. He has but one task, and does it well. Neither judge nor jury, he is a fixed point in the cosmology of the Underworld. A deliverer of souls to their final rest...so long as the proper rites have been performed and payment made. But attempting to board his craft without permission or payment is sure to arouse his wrath, which is never far from the surface. This is not to say that Charon is as irascible as some stories claim. Rather, he is a professional; a being of boundaries and permissions. To cross the one, you must have the other. But those who defy the ancient laws which govern the threshold between the living and dead, find that Charon is the first to confront them – and often the last. Yet, while he is a bellicose defender of the underworld's sanctity, he has also been known to console the souls of the grieving or the frightened. Though he is fearsome, Charon is not frightening. The souls of the dead have nothing to fear from him. He is both guardian and escort in one. He has guided his boat along the shadowed lengths of the Styx and the Acheron for millennia, and souls who book passage upon his craft are thenceforth under his protection against the myriad dangers of the underworld. To ride with Charon is to be ensured a safe journey to one's ultimate and final destination. But the time is coming where Charon must sail on unfamiliar waters. The pantheons are in upheaval, and the souls of the fallen are at the mercy of unfriendly forces. So the Ferryman must set forth and bring these lost souls home, whatever the cost.
baseInformation.shortRole: Collects Tolls to Ferry Allies and Control Enemies

basic.name: Charon Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals Magical Damage to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Ferryman of Souls
passive.shortDesc:
When any friendly lane minion or jungle monster dies, they drop a coin for you to collect that persists for 8 seconds. Coins grant you 1 gold and a Soul Stack. You lose all soul stacks when returning to base and gain 2 max health per stack lost. Allies can pay you 1 gold to ferry with you. This grants you a Movement Speed boost while they ride. 

  • While being ferried, allies take 50% of the damage you take as true damage. 
  • Any Stun, Displacement, Teleport, Banish or Grab will immediately eject all allies.

A01.shortDesc:
Fire a soul in a line that deals [45% INT] Magical Damage and Slows all enemies, stopping on the first god hit. If the soul hits an enemy god, it explodes, dealing [45% INT] Magical Damage and Silencing all enemies hit. 

  • Hit enemies leave a trail behind them for 3 seconds that deals [5% INT] Magical Damage every 0.5 seconds.

A02.name: Damnation
A02.shortDesc:
After a short delay, a burst of water deals [40% INT] Magical Damage to enemies. Enemy gods hit are revealed and have reduced Attack Speed. Allies hitting marked gods gain a Shield. Subsequent hits grant 25% shield value. 

  • Shield health can reach up to 15% of your maximum health.

A03.name: Swift Voyage
A03.shortDesc: Charge forward with increased Movement Speed and remain Slow and Displacement Immune. Enemies you collide with, or that hit the wake of your boat, take [40% INT] Magical Damage and are Rooted.

A04.name: Summon Styx
A04.shortDesc: Gain CC Immunity and summon a massive wave across the battlefield. Enemy gods hit take [50% INT] Magical Damage and are Feared. Allies gain Protections and Movement Speed.

aspect.description: Passive coins are pulled to you while in assist range. Damnation no longer applies an attack speed slow or Shield. Instead, it summons a soul on enemy gods hit that deals damage when you hit them. Once for attacks, three times for abilities. Spectral Surge refreshes the duration of the soul but does not Silence.

baseInformation.loreShort: Chiron is a being of conflicted duality. Two opposites in one form. For all appearances, he is a centaur, bearing the torso of a man, and the body of a horse. Brutish, crude, and violent, the centaurs are not civilized creatures. Yet, Chiron is a scholar, a poet, an astrologer, and a teacher. And for all the wonder and glory of the world he tries to impart upon his illustrious students, Chiron, it seems, must constantly teach them the art of war.
baseInformation.loreLong:
Chiron is a being of conflicted duality. Two opposites in one form. For all appearances, he is a centaur, bearing the torso of a man, and the body of a horse. Brutish, crude, and violent, the centaurs are not civilized creatures. Yet, Chiron is a scholar, a poet, an astrologer, and a teacher. And for all the wonder and glory of the world he tries to impart upon his illustrious students, Chiron, it seems, must constantly teach them the art of war.

Fame and legend are the hallmarks of his pupils. Perseus, who beheaded Medusa, Ajax, who battled bravely against Troy, and Achilles, who Chiron practically raised from infancy, all are figures of renown. But it is for their acts of violence, not art or science, for which songs are sung of these heroes. Chiron would prefer the world a more contemplative place where mankind adored nature and kindled bonds of friendship over scholarly discussions.

Though, to his wizened eyes, the brink has been reached and the world he finds so fascinating tilts toward annihilation. Set aside are his books and scrolls, the easel and brush, and taken up are his bow and armor. On thunderous hooves Chiron gallops to the field. To save all that he holds dear, Chiron, the Great Teacher, will go to war.

basic.name: Chiron Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals Physical Damage to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Herbal Medicine
passive.shortDesc:
Gain a charge every 12 seconds. Using an ability consumes a charge to throw a poultice to an injured ally, applying a Heal over Time.

  • Healed allies also gain Strength and Intelligence for a duration
  • Poultices prioritize the ally with the lowest Health in range

A01.name: Training Exercise
A01.shortDesc: Deploy a constellation that grants CC Immune to allies in the area. After a 1 second delay, it explodes dealing [80% STR] Physical Damage to all enemies in the area.

A02.name: Masterful Shot
A02.shortDesc:
Applies a Target Mark to enemies when they are damaged by Chiron's basic attacks or abilities. Reduces the Physical Protections of marked enemies by 10%. Activating the ability fires seeking arrows towards all marked targets, dealing [80% STR] Physical Damage and Slowing affected enemies.

  • Once Chiron has 250 Strength, a second larger arrow can be fired in a targeted area, dealing 100% of the initial damage and providing an additional 15% bonus damage per target hit by Masterful Shot, up to 4 targets
  • Once Chiron has 2.5 Attack Speed, Masterful Shot transforms into Masterful Flurry, firing 3 arrows with lower scaling quickly at each target, increasing Chiron's attack damage and procs basic attack item effects

TickCount:
Masterful Shot: 1
Masterful Shot (Enhanced): 1
Masterful Flurry: 3

A03.name: Giddyup!
A03.shortDesc:
Charge forward, dealing [50% STR] Physical Damage to enemies, Knocking Up minions, and Kicking enemy gods behind you.

  • You are Knockup Immune while charging
  • You may fire Basic Attacks and Masterful Shot while charging

A04.name: Centaurus
A04.shortDesc:
Transform into a constellation, reducing Movement Speed but gaining the ability to fire three long distance arrows that pass through walls, dealing [65% STR] Physical Damage to all enemies they hit.

  • If you would be killed during this ability, you do not die until you run out of time or shots
  • If you would have died but killed an enemy with this ability, you are Healed for health equal to the damage dealt instead
  • You can fire Masterful Shot during this ability

aspect.description: Target Mark now only applies to one target, and you passively copy 10% of damage done to that target, dealing it again as Physical Damage. Target Mark can no longer apply to lane minions. Training Exercise boosts Allies' Strength and Intelligence when it hits or cleanses them. Centaurus has less scaling, but slows enemies, and when it hits allies, grants them Attack Speed, Shield and applies Herbal Medicine's buff.

baseInformation.loreShort: Chaac, the Rainbringer, whose mighty axe cleaves the heavens, unleashing life-giving waters that feed the land. In ages past, he gifted maize to the Mayans, yet pride led him astray when his heart sought his brother's wife, casting him into a tempest of regret. His tears became his rain—a gift to the earth and a constant lament. Now, as Chaac cleaves the sky, each thunderous crack is a reminder of life's fleeting strength. His worshipers, attuned to his deep emotions, channel the strength of his passion to summon rain, knowing both the beauty and sorrow in each storm. Amidst the gods' chaos, Chaac strikes to sustain the world he once failed, vowing never to falter again.
baseInformation.loreLong: Rain is life. Showers from above soak the earth, feeding seeds which grow into precious fruits and grains. Man and beast alike are sustained by this cycle. Without rain, this world would be barren and all living things would turn to dust. Thus is the power of Chaac, God of Rain. But, like all great power, it did not come without cost. In mankind?s earliest days there were no farmers, so they starved beneath the sun. For days, Chaac sought a way to feed them. Deep in the mountains he discovered a golden boulder and cleft it with his mighty axe. Within was Maize, the crop that would change the Maya people forever, and Chaac was worshipped and adored for it. Drunk with pride, Chaac sought the object of his deepest desire, the wife of his brother. For a time, their affair remained secret, but that was not to last. Chaac?s brother, the Sun God, discovered them. Without a word, she dutifully returned to her husband and Chaac, realizing what he had done, let her go, but a rift formed between the two brothers that would never heal. In his grief, Chaac drove his axe into the sky, and from the wound tears of regret fell. Since then, Chaac has chosen a life free of pride and selfishness, devoting himself only to mankind. As a reminder of his greatest failing, he continues to cleave the heavens, washing the land in sustaining waters. Thus do crops grow. Thus do the people survive. Thus is the power of Chaac, God of Rain.

baseInformation.loreShort: Cute underworld doggo protects Hades. :3
baseInformation.loreLong:
Do not pass the black expanse of the river Styx if blood still flows in your veins. For there, upon the opposing shore, stands guard a beast of impossible animosity. Three fanged wolf heads will be your welcome, hackles raised, eyes like embers, burning green with ghostly flame. A sinuous tail whips with a mind all its own, barbed with the head of a serpent, forked tongue tasting the air. Should your body still be warm with life's glow when you dare set foot upon the shore, Cerberus, Hound of Hell, will feast upon your flesh, consuming both body and soul.
No mercy resides in this creature. He is a monster, brought into this world by the Mother of Monsters, Echidna, for no other purpose than to guard the gates of hell. There he remains, eternally chained to the shrouded passage. None of the living may enter. None of the dead may leave.
Hades, master to Cerberus, has unbound his servant to the battlefield of the Gods. With his poisonous breath, his acidic spittle, his gaze that can turn flesh to stone, Cerberus now hunts freely in the world of the living, eager to drag everyone back down to hell.

targets. The stun is 75% less effective within 2.5 meters
