baseInformation.loreShort: Cupid, God of Love, carries arrows tipped with desire, making hearts fall wherever he flies. He is both son of Mars and Venus, bound to passion yet knowing the chaos it can bring. Once he fell for Psyche, a mortal of radiant beauty, yet their love ignited divine jealousy. Now, Cupid readies his arrows, seeking not to incite mere whimsy but to remind all gods that even amidst the fury of battle, love remains—fierce and unyielding.
baseInformation.loreLong: An arrow from the bow of Cupid, chubby little God of Love, will make anyone give in to passion and desire. Cupid, as he was called by the Romans, is the son of Venus and Mars, or Aphrodite and Ares as they were known by the Greeks. He is the God of love and desire, flying about on feathery wings, shooting passion-tipped arrows at all the creatures of the earth. When struck with one of his arrows, man and beast alike fall in love with the next thing they see. Without Cupid?s steady aim, there would be no love in this world, which is precisely what happened when Cupid fell in love himself. Psyche was a mortal of astounding beauty, which made Venus venomously jealous. Venus ordered Cupid to sting her with an arrow, then arranged for Psyche to fall in love with a spider. Invisible, Cupid reluctantly went to Psyche as she slept, yet, she woke suddenly and saw right through Cupid?s invisibility. Surprised, Cupid cut himself on his own arrow and fell immediately in love with Psyche. Furious, Venus would not let Cupid marry Psyche, so Cupid stopped shooting arrows, people stopped falling in love and stopped worshipping Venus. Panicked, the Goddess of beauty relented and Cupid went back to work. Psyche was whisked to a secret location. Cupid visited her only at night so she could not see who he was. Fearing him to be a terrible monster, Psyche waited for Cupid to fall asleep and lit a lantern. Surprised at what she found, she accidentally scraped one of Cupid?s arrows and fell in love with him. Venus was displeased and forbade Psyche to see her son, forcing her to complete a series of impossible challenges that Psyche overcame only with mystical help. Finally, Cupid begged Jupiter (Zeus) to intervene. Casually, he declared Psyche and Cupid could be together. Thus, Cupid fed Psyche the drink of ambrosia that made her immortal and the God of love got to live happily ever after.
baseInformation.shortRole: Makes Enemies' Hearts Explode and Shares the Love with Allies

basic.name: Cupid Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals Physical Damage</> to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Lovestruck
passive.shortDesc:
Hitting a Basic Attack grants a stack, or 2 stacks if the target is an enemy god.

 • Heart Bomb, Share the Love, and Fields of Love always consume up to 8 stacks on use, granting them additional Damage, Healing, and bonus effects

A01.name: Heart Bomb
A01.shortDesc:
Fire an arrow of love, dealing [80% STR + 55% INT] Physical Damage to the first enemy hit and Slowing them.

 • After 3s, the enemy's heart explodes, dealing [80% STR + 55% INT] Physical Damage to all enemies within the radius.

 • If you have 8 stacks of Lovestruck, all enemies in the explosion area are Stunned.

A02.name: Share the Love
A02.shortDesc:
Lob an explosive bomb at a location that detonates after 0.75s, dealing [65% STR + 35% INT] Physical Damage to all enemies hit.

 • When the bomb explodes, 3 hearts spawn around the detonation location, Healing [3.5% INT] and restoring [5% INT] Mana to any ally that picks them up. You gain 60% of the healing if an ally picks these up.

 • If you have 8 stacks of Lovestruck, the hearts also provide a temporary [10% STR] Strength and [5% INT] Intelligence buff

A03.name: Flutter
A03.shortDesc: Dash forward quickly, leaving behind a trail that increases all allies' Attack and Movement Speed.

A04.name: Fields of Love
A04.shortDesc:
Fire a volley of arrows in an area, Slowing and Crippling all enemies inside.

 • The arrows grow for 2s before exploding, dealing [100% STR + 75% INT] Physical Damage and Mesmerizing all enemies inside the area, while Healing all allies [12.5% INT]

 • If you have 8 stacks of Lovestruck, enemies are Stunned instead of Mesmerized

tips.title: Lovestruck Management Tips (Passive)
tips.value: Try to get enough stacks of Lovestruck to apply the stun to your ultimate as this makes it MUCH better than having a Mesmerize and can very often instantly win/turn fights.

tips.title: Healing Tips
tips.value: If you aim your 2 directly below yourself or an ally you can quickly burst heal with all 3 hearts, which can be VERY useful in a pinch.

aspect.description: Heart Bomb's damage is decreased from [80% STR + 55% INT] to [15% STR + 15% INT] but the enemy hit has reduced Attack Speed and the explosion area heals allies. At 8 stacks of Lovestruck, allies also gain a Health Shield.
aspect.name: **__Aspect of Love__**

basic.name: Cupid Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals Physical Damage</> to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Lovestruck
passive.shortDesc:
Hitting a Basic Attack grants a stack, or 2 stacks if the target is an enemy god.

 • Heart Bomb, Share the Love, and Fields of Love always consume up to 8 stacks on use, granting them additional Damage, Healing, and bonus effects

A01.name: Heart Bomb
A01.shortDesc:
Fire an arrow of love, dealing [80% STR + 55% INT] Physical Damage to the first enemy hit and Slowing them.

 • After 3s, the enemy's heart explodes, dealing [80% STR + 55% INT] Physical Damage to all enemies within the radius.

 • If you have 8 stacks of Lovestruck, all enemies in the explosion area are Stunned.

A02.name: Share the Love
A02.shortDesc:
Lob an explosive bomb at a location that detonates after 0.75s, dealing [65% STR + 35% INT] Physical Damage to all enemies hit.

 • When the bomb explodes, 3 hearts spawn around the detonation location, Healing [3.5% INT] and restoring [5% INT] Mana to any ally that picks them up. You gain 60% of the healing if an ally picks these up.

 • If you have 8 stacks of Lovestruck, the hearts also provide a temporary [10% STR] Strength and [5% INT] Intelligence buff

A03.name: Flutter
A03.shortDesc: Dash forward quickly, leaving behind a trail that increases all allies' Attack and Movement Speed.

A04.name: Fields of Love
A04.shortDesc:
Fire a volley of arrows in an area, Slowing and Crippling all enemies inside.

 • The arrows grow for 2s before exploding, dealing [100% STR + 75% INT] Physical Damage and Mesmerizing all enemies inside the area, while Healing all allies [12.5% INT]

 • If you have 8 stacks of Lovestruck, enemies are Stunned instead of Mesmerized

baseInformation.loreShort: Emperor Zhou's desire for the Goddess Nu Wa was considered an obsession, perhaps even madness. She sent signs and missives to reject him, but Zhou would not be dissuaded. At night, he slipped into her temple and scrawled poetry across the walls espousing his undying love.
baseInformation.loreLong:
Emperor Zhou's desire for the Goddess Nu Wa was considered an obsession, perhaps even madness. She sent signs and missives to reject him, but Zhou would not be dissuaded. At night, he slipped into her temple and scrawled poetry across the walls espousing his undying love. This defilement could not go unanswered. Nu Wa summoned the most conniving and wicked Fox Spirit she could and thrust it upon the Emperor with intent to spiral his life into misery. Little did Nu Wa know what she had just unleashed.

For thousands of years the nine-tailed Fox spirit had lived, biding its time, honing its malicious appetites. Finally in human form, it assumed the irresistible guise of Da Ji, and to Emperor Zhou, she could do no wrong.

With free reign of the Empire and its people, Da Ji reveled in torture and malice. She took delight in their screams of pain as she danced to playful music composed at her command, the musicians trembling for every note.

So grievous did these atrocities grow that the people rose up and overthrew their ruler. They stormed the gates, toppled the throne, and pursued Zhou to his bed chamber, where the crazed emperor finally took his own life.

Yet, Da Ji managed to escape and conceal herself. Now she joins the fray, not to save the universe, nor empower her pantheon, but instead to soak up every last cry of anguish from her enemies.
baseInformation.shortRole: Claw Enemies with Bleeds, Chain Them to Your Paolao

basic.name: Da Ji Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal <Damage>{Damage_Formula} Physical Damage</> to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Torture Blades
passive.shortDesc:
Basic Attacks and Non-Ultimate Abilites bleed enemies, dealing [5% STR] Physical Damage every 1s for 2s. 
 • Stacks infinitely
 • Does not proc item effects.

A01.name: Horrible Burns
A01.shortDesc:
While active, your next successful Attack will Slow, deals bonus [20% STR] Physical Damage, and deals additional [10% STR] Physical Damage every second.
 • The nearest enemy god below a health threshold is marked, reducing this ability's cooldown rapidly.
 • Hitting a marked enemy god with this ability permanently increases its damage scaling by [1% STR].
 • While active, you gain Haste.

A02.name: One Thousand Cuts
A02.shortDesc:
Strike enemies ahead of you 4 times, dealing [40% STR] Physical Damage.
 • While channeling you gain Movement Speed and Mitigate Damage from gods behind you.
 • You are Slow Immune and immune to strafe/backpedal penalties.

A03.name: Trickster Spirit
A03.shortDesc:
Teleport to a target after a delay, dealing [70% STR] Physical Damage in an area.
 • Your rotation before teleport determines your facing after arrival.

A04.name: Paolao
A04.shortDesc:
Rise onto your Paolao for up to 7s and fire ensnaring chains that Cripple and pull enemies, dealing [60% STR] Physical Damage.
 • After 3 shots or canceling, you can Leap.
 • Hitting an enemy twice increases pull strength.
 • If an enemy reaches the Paolao, they are Stunned and take the Chain Damage again.
 • Enemies near the Paolao take periodic [5% STR] Physical Damage.
 • You are CC Immune while using this ability.

tips.title: Tips
tips.value: Try to use your ultimate to pull targets that don't have easy access to CC immunity, like a Kukulkan or a Jungler with their beads down. Also remember that your ultimate CRIPPLES, so you can use it as a way to stop enemies from jumping out of your teammate's Vulcan ult, this means it'll be useful even if they beads the actual pull of the ult.

aspect.description:
Horrible Burns no longer burns but provides Lifesteal and lasts for a short duration, causing any subsequent attacks to deal [15% STR] bonus Physical Damage. One Thousand Cuts has a shorter cooldown, fires 2 swings, increasing at Attack Speed thresholds and deals Basic Attack damage. Trickster Spirit deals [70% -> 60% STR] damage. 
 • Procs Basic Attack Item effects for 70% of their value. 
 • Strikes become faster when reaching the attack speed thresholds.
aspect.name: Aspect of Ferocity

baseInformation.loreShort: Danzaburou, the cunning tanuki of Sado Island, delights in trickery and mirth. A master shapeshifter, he revels in outwitting mortals and gods alike, his laugh as infectious as his pranks. Often mistaken for a friend, his gifts of sake and sly deals soon lead to surprises—often at someone else's expense. Yet behind his grin lies a sharp mind, and Danzaburou weaves through chaos, always ready with a fresh trick, a friend one moment, a foe the next, and a mystery always.
baseInformation.loreLong: Among the tanuki of Sado Island, one name is known above all others ? that of Danzaburou. The supreme commander of all tanuki is infamous for his antics and his tricks are justly feared by mortal and immortal alike. A shape-shifter and trickster without equal, Danzaburou has never met a prank he would not pull. While these are mostly for his own amusement, he has been known to act on behalf of others, so long as he finds it entertaining. Even so, like all tanuki, he lives to cause trouble, and relishes every opportunity to show off his cleverness, as well as his capacity to cause havoc. Nowhere is this more evident than on the battlefield, where Danzaburou surrounds himself in chaos and confusion. He fights with great cunning. When not dodging his opponent?s attacks, or leaving a statue in his place to absorb the brunt of their wrath, he?s hiding in plain sight, waiting for his moment to strike. To say that Danzaburou is a frustrating opponent is an understatement. But Danzaburou brings more than just frustration ? he is also a purveyor of good fortune. Jug of sake in one hand, and a promissory note in the other, the tanuki offers his opponents a drink and payment even as he plans his next trick or three. With his sly grin and big belly, the cheerful tanuki can turn even the staunchest of his foes into a friend ? at least for as long as it takes to get to the bottom of a jug of sake. It is said that Danzaburou has been outwitted only once, by a human peasant. Ever since, the tanuki has refrained from playing tricks on hapless humans. Instead, he reserves his best pranks for what he considers to be a less perceptive audience. . . Namely, the gods themselves.
baseInformation.shortRole: Baits Enemies With Tricks, Fires Explosive Shots

basic.name: Danzaburou Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals  Physical Damage</> to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Dishonest Duplication
passive.shortDesc: You have a 5% + 0.5% per level + 0.05% per Intelligence chance of spawning a clone of yourself with each basic attack hitting an enemy. These clones deal (10 + (1 x level)) + [10% of your Basic Attack Damage]

A01.name: Fool's Gold
A01.shortDesc:
Roll out 3 money bags that deal [90% STR + 65% INT] Physical Damage to enemies hit and then explode dealing [20% STR + 40% INT] Physical Damage.

 • They stop on enemy gods, Trembling them and dropping a coin

   • Coins can be picked up by yourself or allied gods to provide you 10 gold

 • This is being calculated as all 3 initial hits of the money bags, with the built in DR on the 2nd and 3rd hits.

A02.name: Alluring Spirits
A02.shortDesc:
Drink some sake, Healing for [3% INT] Health every .5s for 1.5s before throwing the bottle, dealing [70% STR + 40%] Physical Damage to enemies hit.

   • On landing, the bottle applies a stacking Slow to enemy gods

   • On reaching 3 stacks, they are Taunted towards the bottle

A03.name: Tanuki Trickery
A03.shortDesc:
Create a field where you gain Movement Speed, Slow Immunity, your Basic Attack Movement Penalty is removed, and your Dishonest Duplication chance is doubled. When you leave the area, it disappears and you transform into a leaf while decoys mimic your movement.

   • Enemies in the field are Slowed

   • As a leaf you gain increased Movement Speed, Slow Immunity and a buff that causes your next Basic Attack to deal [100% INT + 55% STR] Physical Damage

A04.name: Uproarious Rocket
A04.shortDesc: Fire a rocket that locks onto enemy gods or reactivate this ability to become the rocket! The rocket deals [100% STR + 100% INT] Physical Damage, Stuns the first enemy god hit and explodes dealing [80% STR + 50% INT] Physical Damage.

tips.title: Boxing/Shredding Tips
tips.value: Be sure to press your 3 while shredding objectives/boxing enemy gods as this gives you damage mitigation and an increased chance to spawn clones from your passive, Dishonest Duplication, increasing DPS and making you harder to kill.

aspect.description: Alluring Spirits no longer throws out a sake bottle but instead spawns 2 Clones. All of your Clones deal more damage and take less damage.
aspect.name: **__Aspect of Fellowship__**

baseInformation.loreShort: It is said Rome wasn't built in a day. Whether the sweeping arches of the Colosseum, the long stretch of the aqueducts, or the power the Servian Wall, none can truly claim they were the origin that built Rome. For as mighty as she has become, Rome might have never been were it not for the mischief of the Goddess Discordia.
baseInformation.loreLong:
It is said Rome wasn't built in a day. Whether the sweeping arches of the Colosseum, the long stretch of the aqueducts, or the power the Servian Wall, none can truly claim they were the origin that built Rome. For as mighty as she has become, Rome might have never been were it not for the mischief of the Goddess Discordia.

Once known among the Greeks as Eris, the maligned Goddess of Strife, Discordia craved to unleash her full potential, to unravel the foundations of the mortals and deities that hated her. She found that opportunity through the vanity of others.

Conjuring a golden apple, Eris cast it forth. Upon it read the words for the fairest. A phrase that would set Athena, Hera, and Aphrodite against one another. A phrase that would cause Paris to kidnap Menelaus' wife, Helen. A phrase that would ignite the Trojan War.

As chaos swirled, as cities crumbled, as heroes both Greek and Trojan fell to each other's blades and Gods grew to loathe one another, no one seemed to wonder from whence the golden apple came. It was masterful! Eventually, the Trojan War reached its peak, and Eris attached herself to the mortal Aeneus. She guided him safely from Troy, across the sea and wilderness, to the place where a new nation would be built. Here, Aeneus founded Rome.

Free of the judgement she wore under the Greeks, bereft of the belittling glares of the other Gods, Eris was remade as Discordia. A Roman Goddess of importance and prominence. Indeed, Rome was not built in a day. It was the culmination of a plan for one Goddess to reinvent herself as more than she ever was.

And all with one golden apple.
baseInformation.shortRole: Imbue enemies with Chaos and drive them Mad.

basic.name: Discordia Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals  Magical Damage to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Contest of Gods
passive.shortDesc: The god on your team with the highest damage dealt to enemy gods gains a buff of Strength and Intelligence. Gaining a level or a god kill/assist provides a random temporary buff of Intelligence, Lifesteal or Healing. Healing ticks every 0.5s.

A01.name: Unruly Magic
A01.shortDesc: Fire out an orb of unruly magic dealing [75% INT] Magical Damage. After reaching the target location, it explodes in a small area dealing [60% INT] Magical Damage before breaking into bouncing minor projectiles that each deal [10% INT] Magical Damage.

A02.name: Strife
A02.shortDesc: Create an area that deals [60% INT] Magical Damage. Hitting one enemy causes them to be Rooted, hitting multiple causes them to attack their nearest ally for [10% INT] Magical Damage

A03.name: Erratic Behavior
A03.shortDesc: Leap and create a field in an area. While within the field, you gain Stealth, Movement Speed and your ability cooldowns are decreased. Dealing damage to enemy gods while within the field increases its duration. Damaging enemy gods increases the field duration by 0.25s up to a max of an additional 3s. Ability cooldown reduction ticks every 0.5s and does not apply to this ability.

A04.name: Golden Apple of Discord
A04.shortDesc: Throw the Golden Apple of Discord which deals [50% INT] Magical Damage before exploding in an area dealing [60% INT] Magical Damage and spreading the effects of the Apple. Enemies afflicted by the Apple effect are Crippled and Trembled. Enemies caught in the explosion take initial damage and a burst of damage at the end of the effect, but do not explode in an area. Projectile bounces, passes through and damages enemies but stops on the first enemy god hit.

aspect.description: Contest of Gods only provides you extra Attack Damage from Intelligence. Unruly Magic does not explode but returns back, dealing damage and providing a stack causing your next Attacks to explode. Enemies can only be damaged by one minor projectile from Unruly Magic. Erratic Behavior no longer creates a field or Stealth but Attack Speed and Attack Damage. Golden Apple of Discord is faster and does not spread.

baseInformation.loreShort: Eset, Goddess of Magic and Motherhood, is the devoted protector of Egypt. Beloved for her wisdom and compassion, she is worshipped by those seeking healing, guidance, and the strength to endure hardship.
baseInformation.loreLong: Eset, Goddess of Magic and Motherhood, is the devoted protector of Egypt. Beloved for her wisdom and compassion, she is worshipped by those seeking healing, guidance, and the strength to endure hardship. When her husband Osiris was betrayed and slain by Set, Eset scoured the earth to gather his scattered remains, restoring him through her unmatched sorcery. From this devotion came Horus, destined to challenge Set and reclaim Egypt's throne. As a goddess, Eset embodies resilience and hope, shielding the vulnerable and empowering the faithful. Those drawn to her command the battlefield through protection and support, turning the tide with both strength and sacrifice.
baseInformation.shortRole: Channels Divine Magic and Releases its Power for Protection and Ruin

basic.name: Eset Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals  Magical Damage</> to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Funeral Rites
passive.shortDesc:
Whenever a God dies within 12m of Eset, she gains a stack of Funeral Rites, granting Health Regen and Mana Regen for her and nearby allies. Additionally, every third Basic Attack from Eset is empowered, allowing it to hit in a wider radius and applying bonus Damage based on her stacks.
  
  Notes:
  • At 4 stacks, Eset's Invocation buff can stack up to 4 times.
  • At 5 stacks, Eset's Invocation buff can stack up to 5 times.
  • At 10 stacks, Eset's Invocation buff can stack up to 6 times, and Eset gains 10% Cooldown Rate.

A01.name: Wing Gust
A01.shortDesc:
Fire 4 projectiles in a line, each dealing [65% INT] Magical Damage to all targets in their path.
    
    Notes:
    • While channeling, Eset gains Movement Speed, reduced backpedal and strafing penalties, and Knockback and Root immunity.
    • After casting, Eset's next Basic Attack will be empowered.
    • Each projectile grants a stack of Invocation the first time it hits an enemy.
    • This ability passes through and damages all enemies, and passes through walls.

A02.name: Spirit Ball
A02.shortDesc:
Send forth a ball of spirit energy. While it travels, Eset can re-activate this ability to detonate the ball, dealing [70% INT] Magical Damage and Stunning enemies.
    
    Notes:
    • The further the ball travels, the higher its damage and Stun duration. Up to 1.5x the normal damage and Stun duration at max distance.
    • When the ball deals damage to enemies, grants a stack of Invocation for each enemy hit.
    • Deals 105/172.5/240/307.5/375 + 105% Intelligence Magical Damage, and Stuns for 1.5 seconds at max range.
    • This ability passes through all enemies, and passes through walls.

A03.name: Dispel Magic
A03.shortDesc:
Eset Silences and Slows enemy Gods at her target location. Enemy Gods hit also have reduced Magical Protections.
    
    Notes:
    • If Eset successfully hits an enemy God, allies near the Dispel Magic or Eset gain a Health Shield.
    • If Eset hits multiple enemy Gods, the Health Shield is increased by 30% for each enemy after the first up to a maximum of 60%.
    • Applies up to a 80/160/240/320/400 Health Shield when hitting 3 enemy Gods.

A04.name: Circle of Protection
A04.shortDesc:
Call down your staff and create a Circle of Protective Magic for 5 seconds before it explodes, dealing [50-151% INT] Magical Damage to all enemies and Healing all allies in the area.
    
    Notes:
    • Eset can re-activate this ability at any time to detonate it, dealing the Magical Damage and Healing early.
    • Any damage dealt in the area while it is active charges the circle, increasing its Magical Damage and Healing.
    • While standing in the area, allies take reduced damage.
    • Deals 364/435/505/574/645 + 100/115/125/137/151% Intelligence Magical Damage and 150/247/340/452/540/645 Healing at full charge.

aspect.description: Dispel Magic no longer requires an enemy god hit to shield allies and now applies Transference to all allies it shields. Wing Gust no longer has damage scaling, but applies a movement speed aura to allies, and when its projectiles hit enemies, all allies with Transference are healed.
aspect.name: Aspect of Lifewarden

baseInformation.loreShort: Fenrir, the wolf-son of Loki, defies death and the gods themselves. Foretold to bring Ragnarök, the end of all things, he was bound by trickery, yet Fenrir's hunger remained, growing more ravenous with each chain. Thrice he shattered his bonds, and on the third, Tyr paid the price, his hand sacrificed to secure the beast. Now unbound, Fenrir hunts his captors, driven not by hunger but vengeance, the first hunter among gods, eager to claim his dark, destined prize.
baseInformation.loreLong: Mortals must rely on the wisdom of the Gods, but never forget they are fallible. They can be petty, churlish, and worst of all, wrong. What are the consequences for their mistakes? They should have slain the wolf-beast Fenrir, but they chose instead to bind him. Despite the warnings and prophecies he would tear Odin apart at Ragnarok and devour the earth and sky, they did not end Loki's son. Now, terror roams free. It is unknown how, but Fenrir is Unbound. Thrice they tried to bind him, each time convincing Fenrir that the chains were nothing more than a challenge for his strength, a test to see if he could shatter them. The first two God-forged fetters were easily broken beneath the beast?s terrible power, but the third was a ribbon, dwarf made, and Fenrir grew suspicious of the God?s intentions. As a show of faith, he demanded one of them place a hand in his mouth, and if the ribbon proved magical in nature, he would exact punishment. Brave, if foolish, Tyr put his fist in Fenrir?s maw as the ribbon was secured to the wolf?s leg. Fenrir strained and screamed, but could not break free, and in rage, he devoured Tyr?s hand. For a time, Fenrir was forgotten, the dire prophecy just a memory. But they were all warned and the Gods did nothing. Fenrir is hungry now, the first hunter, an alpha among sheep, but no flesh will satisfy his need. It is revenge he craves, and now he is Unbound.

baseInformation.loreShort: Guan Yu, the Saint of War and embodiment of loyalty and honor, stands as a symbol of justice for the downtrodden and the righteous. His worshipers- warriors, merchants, and villagers-seek his protection, trusting his unwavering integrity. Once a mortal general who swore a sacred oath to create a just world, Guan Yu became a god revered for his valor and virtue. In combat, his massive guan dao cleaves through evil, and his red- faced likeness wards against dishonesty. On the battlefield, Guan Yu is a steadfast guardian of righteousness, leading his allies to victory with unshakable resolve and peerless might.
baseInformation.loreLong: Guan Yu, the Saint of War and embodiment of loyalty and honor, stands as a symbol of justice for the downtrodden and the righteous. On the battlefield, Guan Yu is a steadfast guardian of righteousness, leading his allies to victory with unshakable resolve and peerless might.

baseInformation.loreShort: After rigorous celebration, Parvati, Goddess of fertility and love, washed the dirt and sweat from her body. It floated upon the surface of her pool, formless and purposeless. With a wave of her hand, it was transformed into a boy, her son, and set him to guard the door as she finished washing. Shiva, the supreme being, returned and found this child doggedly blocking his path. Fearing the safety of his wife, Shiva drew his sword and cut him down.
baseInformation.loreLong:
After rigorous celebration, Parvati, Goddess of fertility and love, washed the dirt and sweat from her body. It floated upon the surface of her pool, formless and purposeless. With a wave of her hand, it was transformed into a boy, her son, and set him to guard the door as she finished washing. Shiva, the supreme being, returned and found this child doggedly blocking his path. Fearing the safety of his wife, Shiva drew his sword and cut him down.

This is your son! Parvati cried. You must save him!

Hurriedly, Shiva replaced the head of the child with the wisest creature in the world, that of an elephant. Thus, was Ganesha truly born.

Generous, warm-hearted, and optimistic, Ganesha has assumed authority over the very thing his mother tasked of him - obstacles. For those whose hearts are filled with boastful arrogance, Ganesha challenges progress. Financial difficulties, faulty materials, and unhappiness plague their every action. But, for the honest, the hard working, Ganesha clears the way. A life of good fortune, bright skies, and joyous laughter.

Obstacles can be either placed or removed. So, to Ganesha all mortals pray when starting a new endeavor, that their path will be open and their future bright. That he may grant them success.

Only with reluctance has Ganesha joined the Battle of the Gods. Violence is the last obstacle the elephant-God would ever place. But these are not mortals that threaten existence. They are Gods, and it is time that Ganesha determined who should succeed, and who should fail.

passive.name: Good Fortune
passive.shortDesc: Any time you deal a killing blow, the nearest allied god receives the credit and you receive the rewards for an assist instead plus a bonus amount of gold. In addition, your assist range and the time before you lose assist credit is increased.

A01.name: Turn of Fate
A01.shortDesc:
Send a curse forward dealing [55% INT] Magical Damage to enemies it passes through.

 • For each enemy hit, all nearby allied gods gain Bonus Damage.
 • Enemy gods are marked for 5s. Any Hard Crowd Control effect consumes the mark to deal bonus Magical Damage.

A02.name: Ohm
A02.shortDesc:
Rise into the lotus position Silencing enemies in front of you and increasing Protections of nearby allied gods.

• Ganesha gains an additional 50% of the Protections he provides
• You are Displacement Immune while channeling
• You are slowed by 15% while channeling.

A03.name: Remove Obstacles
A03.shortDesc:
Dash forward dealing [45% INT] Magical Damage to enemy minions you pass through and stopping on enemy gods, holding them in place before Knocking them Up.

• While dashing you pass through ultimate player-made walls and destroy non-ultimate player-made walls.
• Damage dealt to enemy gods is split between 3 hits.
• You pass through but do not destroy ally walls.
• You cannot cancel while Dashing but you can on contact with an enemy god.

A04.name: Dharmic Pillars
A04.shortDesc:
Summon 4 Great Pillars to imprison your enemies. Enemies that pass through the field take [64% INT] Magical Damage, are Slowed, and have their Protections reduced.

• Enemies take [40% INT] Magical Damage per tick if they remain in the field.
• Minions take 40% reduced damage from this ability.

Dharmic Pillars now no longer slows targets hit.

baseInformation.loreShort: Geb, God of Earth, whose flesh forms the land and whose patience is as ancient as stone. In the judgment halls of Ma'at, he claims guilty souls, dragging them into the depths of his domain. Once bound to the sky goddess Nut, his longing still echoes through the mountains, his love eternal yet forever thwarted by Ra's decree. Awakened by divine strife upon his surface, Geb rises not merely to defend his realm but perhaps to reclaim that which was torn from him—whatever it takes.
baseInformation.loreLong: Behold the rumble from the deep, for it is the bellow of rock and stone. A titan of immortal ages has awoken from fitful slumber to crush all who tread upon his realm. A lumbering giant, patient, deliberate, unmovable; he is Geb, God of Earth. When a mortal?s heart is weighed in the halls of Ma?at, Geb sits among the Gods in judgment. Those burdened with guilt and regret are claimed by Geb and dragged through the earthen crust to the underworld. Hearts free of such heaviness are taught words of power and ascend to the sky. Ah, the sky. It is but the one thing Geb longs for. In his eye, she is glorious sapphire, an unreachable object. But it was not always so. Once, the earth and sky were as one, Geb and Nut, Goddess of the Sky, locked in a lover?s embrace. Ra deemed their union unfit and sent Shu, God of Wind, between them. In his rage, Geb thundered and broke the earth, but for naught, Nut was now forever out of reach. Though his desire has not weathered with the ages, Geb has rested peacefully for time immemorial, nurturing Mankind through bountiful harvest and stable ground for pyramids and palaces. It is the clash of Gods upon his surface that stirs him. He has risen to war, though his motives are unclear. Does he fight to prevent the destruction of his realm, or will he seize the chance to shatter the wind and reunite the earth and sky? Whatever the outcome, the earth will always remain.

Stone Shield is self-only and buffs attack damage.
Cataclysm causes Knockback rather than a Stun.

baseInformation.loreShort: Gilgamesh, the two-thirds divine King of Uruk, is both hero and tyrant, his story a blend of arrogance and redemption.
baseInformation.loreLong: Gilgamesh, the two-thirds divine King of Uruk, is both hero and tyrant, his story a blend of arrogance and redemption. Worshiped by adventurers and rulers alike, his name is synonymous with strength and relentless ambition. From his friendship with Enkidu to his quest for immortality, Gilgamesh has battled monsters and toppled kings, carving his legend across the sands of time. Now, as Tiamat rises, Gilgamesh seeks to unite the gods against her, hoping that victory might finally grant him the divine recognition he craves. On the battefield, he combines raw power with indomitable determination, a relentless force driven by destiny.
baseInformation.shortRole: Warrior with strong displacement and zone control.

basic.name: Gilgamesh Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal 0 Physical Damage to an enemy in front of you. NOTE 1: If multiple enemies are in the area, the enemy closest to the center of the area will be hit. Scales with [100% STR] and [20% INT].

passive.name: Epic of Gilgamesh
passive.shortDesc: You wield artifacts that demand quests to unlock their true potential. Sun-forged Scimitar: Extend buff to max by hitting only gods 3 times. Drop Kick: Drop Kick a target into a god 3 times. Hero's Advance: Kill or Assist 3 gods affected by Hero's Advance damage bonus. Winds of Shamash: Knockback a target off the wall with Drop Kick 5 times. • Each quest: 100 gold + upgrade

A01.name: Sun-forged Scimitar
A01.shortDesc: Ignite your sword for 3s. Enemies around the ignition take [50% STR] Physical Damage and are Slowed for 2s. Lane minions take increased damage. Your Attacks deal bonus [3% Max Health] Physical Damage and extend the effect per hit. You gain Health Regen per rank. Quest Upgrade: Initial ignition extends buff on enemy hits; max duration +3s.

A02.name: Drop Kick
A02.shortDesc: Drop kick an area, dealing [30% STR] Physical Damage. The closest enemy is Knocked Forward, taking [20% STR] bonus Physical Damage when hitting a minion or [30% STR] burst Physical Damage and being Stunned when hitting a god or wall. Launched enemies hitting Winds of Shamash are Knocked Back towards center. Quest Upgrade: When Stun fades, targets are Intoxicated.

A03.name: Hero's Advance
A03.shortDesc: Leap to a target location, dealing [40% STR] Physical Damage on impact. Create a beacon for 5s. Center sweet spot applies increased Damage to enemies. Allies moving toward the beacon gain Movement Speed. Quest Upgrade: The beacon now travels with you.

A04.name: Winds of Shamash
A04.shortDesc: Call the winds for 6s. Enemies inside take [40% STR] Physical Damage and are Slowed. Enemies in the ring take [5% STR] Physical Damage every 0.5s. Outer wall heavily Slows; your damage Pulls them to center. At 6s, wind becomes thread, dealing [50% STR] Physical Damage and Rooting enemies. Quest Upgrade: Ticks every 0.375s and the outer wall Slow increases to 70%.

baseInformation.loreShort: Hades, lord of the Underworld, as constant as the dark itself. Eldest son of Cronus, he rules with solemn duty, seldom leaving his shadowed kingdom. His capture of Persephone filled him with purpose and tempered his rule with the cycle of seasons. Yet Hades' power lies not only in his domain but in the inevitability he represents. He watches the gods' warfare from below, his patience vast, knowing all paths eventually lead to his realm—no soul, no god, can evade his reach forever.
baseInformation.loreLong: Inevitably, death sends everyone to Hades, God of the Underworld. In the end, he always gets his way. Eldest son of the Titans Cronus and Rhea, Hades was swallowed by his father as an infant along with five other siblings. Zeus, the youngest brother, escaped this fate and freed them once he?d grown strong enough to battle Cronus. Together, the six siblings defeated the Titans and banished them to the dark realm of Tartarus. Now the reigning Gods, Zeus, Poseidon, and Hades drew lots for dominion. Hades drew the Underworld. Of the few that have entered the Underworld and come back, all speak dismally of the place, never wishing to return. Yet Hades, himself, rarely leaves the subterranean realm, constantly seeking ways to grow the numbers of the dead. When he desired a wife, Hades captured the radiant Persephone, daughter of the Goddess Demeter. Concealed in the Underworld, none could reach her, so Demeter cursed the land with a great famine. The other Gods pleaded with her to end it lest she destroy mankind, but she would not relent until her daughter was returned. Hades finally let Persephone leave, though not before feeding her pomegranate seeds. Having consumed the fruit, she was bound forever to Hades and required to return to her husband once per year to eat of the seeds again. This, it is said, is a time of mourning and the cause of winter. While many fear death and view it as evil, in truth, Hades endeavors only to keep balance. He is stern but fair. As God of the Dead and the Underworld, he views his authority on this matter absolute, forbidding anyone to leave his realm and growing wrathful when disobeyed or when someone tries to cheat death or steal from him. For everyone, death is unavoidable and service to Hades inevitable. Even the Gods.

  • Your abilities have extra effects on Blighted enemies

tips.title: Ultimate Tips
tips.value: You can extend your ultimate by a few seconds if you use the free ability cast during your ultimate to dash right as your ultimate is ending. This can be incredibly useful for chasing enemies or applying the Vortex/Debuff is a greater area.

tips.title: Teamfight Tips
tips.value: Use your Helm of Darkness to find a good angle to engage in a fight or look for a pick with a 2 poly 3 poly combo.If you have blink, you can blink in, ult, use your 3 in ult, then dash out.

baseInformation.loreShort: Hecate, Goddess of Sorcery and the keeper of all crossroads, stands as a guide between worlds, where shadows loom and reality blurs. She is the patron of scholars, mystics, and seekers of hidden knowledge—those brave enough to peer beyond the veil. Hecate's followers are drawn to her mastery of magic and the occult, and many are adept in rites and rituals that connect them to realms unseen. She guards those who face uncertainty, as they pray for her light in their darkest moments. As divine conflict grows, Hecate holds her torch high, ready to use her powers as guide, protector, or fearsome avenger.
baseInformation.loreLong: Between Olympus, Underworld, and all realms exists a liminal space very few can wander. The entrances to this ethereal plane are difficult to find, but those who do know to look for the shadowed figure lit by a mythical torch. Hecate, Goddess of Sorcery and Witchcraft, holder of the keys between worlds. From guiding the wayward Persephone home to alerting man of incoming conflict between Gods, she watches over the crossroads that connect all realities. The daughter of the Titan Perses and Asteria, her ability to traverse these spheres is only matched by her skills in magic. Bestowed the right by Zeus himself, she took to showing those worthy the ways of harnessing powers through unnatural means. She would become worshiped by scholars and cultists alike, all who seek the witch?s ways to distort the lines between life and death. While other Gods held them as their own, Hecate uses these domains as an expression of her will, and any who follow would enjoy such power. As she now breaches the seams between not only her pantheon but those beyond, she?s now faced with more potential than ever before. Now that deities war amongst themselves once more, Hecate finds herself a mediator once more. However, when reason and persuasion fail, there is no threat her sorcery cannot subvert.
baseInformation.shortRole: Eats Spells, Repels Magic, Creates Portals

basic.name: Hecate Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals  Magical Damage to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Mythic Ritual
passive.shortDesc:
You may interact with things to empower them.

  • Interacting with an allied Structure creates a zone which regenerates health and mana
  • Interacting with an ally who has recently killed a god will increase both your and that ally's Strength and Intelligence

A01.name: Triplicate Form
A01.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals [75% INT] Magical Damage to enemies. This projectile will linger for 1 second and deal the same damage to enemies in this lingering area. In Hex Form, this ability Mesmerizes enemies hit. In Power Form, this ability applies a mark that causes the enemy to take an additional [3.5% INT] Magical damage when hit by god abilities or basic attacks.

A02.name: Spell Eater
A02.shortDesc:
Create a Sigil that charges up when gods activate abilities in a large area.

  • Reactivate to call down the Sigil and deal a minimum of [50% INT] Magical Damage to enemies in the targeted area
  • Damage can increase to  190% Base Damage/Scaling when fully charged [Calculated as fully charged here]

A03.name: Repel Magic
A03.shortDesc:
Grant yourself or an ally a Magical Shield, absorbing Magical damage.

  • Deals [85% INT] Magical Damage near whomever is shielded
  • Activating this increases your Movement Speed, and gives Spell Eater 3 Charges

A04.name: Open the Gates
A04.shortDesc:
Open two portals between realms, Stunning, then Displacing enemy gods to the opposite portal.

  • Deals [80% INT] Magical Damage to enemies
  • You and allied gods can Interact with the portals to Teleport between them

tips.title: Charge Management Tips
tips.value: When leaving base, try to place your 2 and spam your kit while heading back to lane to ensure you have a few stacks. You can also get teammates who are leaving base to use abilities to quickly charge it. This can give you a big burst of damage as you arrive back in lane.

tips.title: Ultimate Tips
tips.value: When ulting, remember that you teleport enemies in the opposing circle TOWARDS you, and enemies in the circle surrounding you AWAY from you. So if you don't want that fed Bellona solo to be on top of you, don't place her in the  opposing circle, and vice versa.

aspect.description: Spell Eater can no longer be refired but instead reduces enemy ability damage in the area. Additionally, each time an enemy ability is cast, a projectile lands and explodes at their location. Dealing [20% INT] Magical damage to enemies caught in the explosion and proccing ability item effects with each projectile. Spell Eater now has a longer cooldown.

baseInformation.loreShort: Hercules, son of Jupiter, whose strength is as legendary as his trials. Cursed to madness by Juno, his journey to redemption began with twelve labors, his courage proven in each impossible feat. With every task, Hercules reclaimed his honor, but his glory came at the price of peace, his deeds marred by the tragedy from which they sprang. Though his triumphs are celebrated, Hercules remains haunted, forever a hero yet forever scarred, his strength a double-edged gift he bears alone.
baseInformation.loreLong: Brave Hercules, a demigod son of Jupiter, born with impossible strength and force of will; for whom no task is insurmountable, no heroic deed unachievable. Yet, Like all of Jupiter?s illegitimate children, Hercules once drew the ire of Juno, and though he is now known as a hero, his greatest achievements might never have been done without Juno?s initial tragic involvement. She struck Hercules mad, and he slew his own children. Once recovered, grief stricken, Hercules sought penance for his crimes. An oracle of Jupiter advised he should pledge his service to the king Eurystheus, and so Hercules reluctantly did so. Jealous of the demigod, the king set him to twelve impossible tasks, expecting him to fail at the first, to slay the Nemean Lion and return with its hide. The Nemean Lion captured women and devoured soldiers. Its fur was so thick no weapon could pierce it, and its claws so sharp, no armor could guard against them. Hercules tracked the lion to its mountain caves and drew it out, firing arrows that shattered against the beast?s impenetrable coat. Agilely, he dodged as the lion leapt to maul him. In the end, Hercules aimed a powerful shot straight into the lion?s roaring maw, a place the powerful fur did not protect, and slew it. Then, using the beasts own claws, he skinned the hide, wearing the mantle proudly as his new armor. Eurystheus was aghast upon the hero?s return. Immediately the legend of Hercules began to grow. Eleven more labors the confounded king set against him, but time after time, year after year, Hercules performed what could not be done, earning fame, glory, and the peace he so desperately sought.
baseInformation.shortRole: Throws Boulders, Pulls and Pushes Enemies Around

basic.name: Hercules Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal Physical Damage</> to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Apotheosis
passive.shortDesc:
Hercules gains Strength when enemy gods damage him. This stacks up to 3 times and lasts 5s. Additionally,  hit a target with an ability to gain a stack toward achieving Apotheosis, or 2 stacks from his Ultimate. 

 At 4 stacks, enter an empowered state that immediately subtracts 2s from active cooldowns and provides an additional Strength, Protections, and Cleaving basic attacks for the duration.

A01.name: Driving Strike
A01.shortDesc: Dash forward, dealing [85% STR] Physical Damage, Stunning and Pushing enemies hit.

A02.name: Earthbreaker
A02.shortDesc: Create a rift that deals [65% STR] Physical Damage to enemies in front of you. Enemies hit by the end area are Pulled to you.

A03.name: Mitigate Wounds
A03.shortDesc: Immediately Heal yourself, gaining Attack Speed and an additional 35% of your Protections from items for 4s. At the end of the duration Heal yourself again for a percentage of damage taken during the effect.

A04.name: Excavate
A04.shortDesc: Hurl a boulder at a location, Displacing enemies straight up at the landing spot. The boulder then rolls and bounces off walls, dealing [100% STR] Physical Damage to enemies hit.

baseInformation.loreShort: Hou Yi, the celestial archer, is the savior of the Earth, famed for felling nine of the ten suns that scorched the land. Worshipped by hunters, defenders, and those seeking courage, Hou Yi wields his mystical red bow with precision and unwavering resolve. His arrows carry the power of the slain sun-ravens, capable of striking fearsome monsters and gods alike. On the battlefield, Hou Yi's pinpoint accuracy and fiery attacks make him a defender of unparalleled skill. His worshipers revere his bravery and determination to protect the world from catastrophe.

His arrows carry the power of the slain sun-ravens, capable of striking fearsome monsters and gods alike. On the battlefield, Hou Yi's pinpoint accuracy and fiery attacks make him a defender of unparalleled skill. His worshipers revere his bravery and determination to protect the world from catastrophe.
baseInformation.shortRole: Constant Damage

basic.name: Izanami Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Throw a piercing projectile that deals  Physical Damage to all enemies hit. The projectile returns to you after reaching its max range or hitting a wall, dealing Physical Damage to any enemies hit on its way back.

passive.name: Suntouched
passive.shortDesc: When critically hit or taking more than 25% of your Health in a single blow, become Suntouched, reducing active cooldowns by 2s and Marking the enemy who triggered this effect. While Suntouched, you cannot be critically hit. While Suntouched, you cannot trigger Suntouched.

A01.name: Ricochet
A01.shortDesc:
Fire an arrow that ricochets off walls, dealing [70% STR + 55% INT] Physical Damage to enemies is passes through. Deals increasing Damage with each ricochet.

• Marked enemies who are hit are Stunned
• Ricochet bounces an additional time if you have 400 or more Intelligence

A02.name: Mark of the Golden Crow
A02.shortDesc:
Mark an enemy causing your other abilities to apply additional effects.

While STR is higher than INT, Marked enemies take bonus Physical Damage based on their missing health. While INT is higher than STR, Marked additional effects are amplified.

• Ricochet Stuns Marked enemies
• Divebomb Knocks Away Marked enemies
• Sunbreaker Slows Marked enemies more

A03.name: Divebomb
A03.shortDesc:
Leap into the air before selecting a location to dive onto, dealing [60% STR + 40% INT] Physical Damage to enemies where you land.

• Marked enemies who are hit are Knocked Away

A04.name: Sunbreaker
A04.shortDesc:
Shoot down 9 suns every 0.5s onto a target area, dealing [25% STR + 15% INT] Physical Damage and Slowed enemies hit. The first sun stays, acting as a wall that Ricochet can bounce off of.

• Marked enemies who are more heavily Slowed

baseInformation.loreShort: Hua Mulan, warrior of heart and honor, who took her father's place in the army and rose through courage alone. For years, she served in disguise, defending her family's honor and bringing victory to her people. Her worshipers are those who value duty, sacrifice, and the courage to defy tradition for a noble cause. For them, Hua Mulan stands as a paragon of virtue, one who redefines strength through loyalty and selflessness. Though her story should have ended at home, new threats call her back to the battlefield, her spirit a rallying cry for all who fight to protect what they love.
baseInformation.loreLong: There are many stories about the girl who became a hero. Most are embellished, the events polished to a legendary sheen. They say that Hua Mulan was blessed, that her bow and blade were gifts of the gods. That she was wise beyond measure, and stronger than a hundred warriors, for her heart was pure. That she became the sworn sister of the king?s daughter. That she returned home and married a prince, or perhaps a farmer. That she died. But every story, no matter how outlandish, has a grain of truth to it. And this is the truth of Hua Mulan. China was invaded and a man from every family was called to serve in the army. But her father was old and her brother a child, and so for love?s sake, she set aside her loom and went to war in their place. She disguised herself as a young man, and no one was the wiser, for her father had trained her in the ways of the bow and the blade, making her the equal of any man in the army. For twelve years, she maintained the pretence. She fought well against the enemies of China, attaining rank and the loyalty of her fellow soldiers. She led men into battle more than once, setting herself where the fighting was thickest so that those who followed her might see her and take heart. At the end of that twelve years, she was offered a high rank, but turned it down. Instead, having grown tired of war, she asked only that she might return home to her family. And so she did, her duty complete. But some stories do not end when the hero returns home. For some stories, that is only the beginning. For now, a new danger stirs, and Hua Mulan must once more take up her weapons in defense of her homeland...
baseInformation.shortRole: Wields a Sword, Spear, and Bow Gaining Power While Fighting

basic.name: Hua Mulan Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal <Damage>{Damage_Formula} Physical Damage</> to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Training Arc
passive.shortDesc:
Utilize your Sword, Spear, and Bow with your abilities to gain Skill towards them by damaging enemies. Your expertise in each weapon enhances their respective abilities.
    
    Additionally, gain two unique benefits:
      • Training Arc reduces the cooldown of your next non-ultimate ability.
      • Training Ground, a consumable which provides team-wide utility.

A01.name: Cross Strike
A01.shortDesc:
Strike twice with your sword, dealing [30% STR] Physical Damage to all enemies hit.

 • Skilled: Gain an Attack Speed increase after using this ability.
      • Adept: Perform a third strike in an extended cone in front of you, dealing [30% STR] Physical Damage.
      • Mastered: Permanently gain Attack Speed.

A02.name: Spear Thrust
A02.shortDesc:
Thrust your spear forward, striking enemies in front of you, Slowing them and dealing [35% STR] Physical Damage.

 • Skilled: Heal for up to 3 enemies hit.
 • Adept: Strike a second time with increased range, dealing bonus [10% STR] Physical Damage if the same enemy is hit twice.
      • Mastered: Permanently gain Strength.

A03.name: Grapple
A03.shortDesc:
Fire an arrow with a rope attached. If it hits an enemy god, Stun them, deal [50% STR] Physical Damage, and pull them toward you while dashing at them and following up with a slash for [20% STR] Physical Damage.

 • Skilled: Gain a Protections buff after hitting an enemy god.
      • Adept: Dash can now target walls.
      • Mastered: Permanently gain Protections.

A04.name: Divine Mastery
A04.shortDesc:
Unleash your entire arsenal, dashing forward with sword slashes that deal [60% STR] Physical Damage and Root enemies.

 • Follow up by using divine energy to Knock Up enemies with your spear, dealing [40% STR] Physical Damage.
      • Finish with a powerful projectile attack from your bow, dealing [60% STR] Physical Damage. You gain Protections while casting.

builds.notes: Bruiser active item oriented solo build.

tips.title: Teamfight Tips
tips.value: Try to look for good Grapples on targets to get them out of position and use your ultimate to chase enemies after they burn their mobility post-Grapple to setup for teammates.

baseInformation.loreShort: Hun Batz, the transformed trickster, once an artist and musician, but envy soured his talents. A grudge with the Hero Twins led him to treachery, and he was outwitted, transformed into a monkey—a god still, but forever bearing the memory of rivalry and loss. Worshipped as a deity of art, Hun Batz's craft now hides a thirst for revenge, his rivalry with the Twins festering, his grudge an ember ready to ignite once more.
baseInformation.loreLong: A grudge, like any wound, left untended will fester. Hun Batz and his twin brother Hun Chouen, led a contented life with their grandmother. Artists and musicians, everything they created was beautiful and enchanting, but they lacked their father?s talent for the ball game, Pitz. Summoned to the underworld, their father, Hun Hunapu never returned. Instead his new offspring, half-brothers to Hun Batz and Hun Chauen, appeared. The Hero Twins were everything the original twins were not, strong, athletic, and clever. And they were extremely gifted at playing Pitz. A jealous rivalry formed between the two sets of twins. The more they played ball together, the more Hun Batz and his brother were defeated. Eventually, the original twins resorted to foul tricks, aggressive tactics, and out-right cheating. Frustrated with these antics, the Hero Twins kicked the ball into a tree, knowing Hun Batz and his brother considered themselves excellent climbers and would go after it. Yet, the higher they climbed, the taller the tree grew, until they could not get back down. Pleased with their magic thus far, The Hero Twins encouraged Hun Batz and Hun Chouen to use their belts like tails in order to escape the terrible height. However, upon doing so, they were mysteriously and humiliatingly transformed into monkeys. Though the Monkey Twins, as they are now called, came to be worshipped as Gods of Art and Music, the animosity they hold for the Hero Twins remains. Time has only sharpened their combined cunning, and they eagerly await a chance to strike back. Like any wound, their grudge has festered, and what was once a brotherly rivalry has grown into a far more deadly game.

baseInformation.loreShort: Izanami, once a creator of worlds, now Queen of Yomi, the underworld. She and her husband, Izanagi, forged all things, yet in giving birth to the god of fire, she was torn from life. In grief, Izanagi sought her in Yomi, but seeing her decayed form, he fled, leaving her bound to death. Enraged by his betrayal, she vowed to take a thousand lives each day. And now, broken free from her prison in Yomi, Izanami returns—cold, relentless, and intent on vengeance, her promise a chilling whisper upon the living.
baseInformation.loreLong: As Izanami gave birth to the god of Fire, his searing skin scorched her flesh. Fearful she would die, her husband, Izanagi, the man with whom she had created the world, held her close and wept. But it was too late. So grievous were the wounds, Izanami succumbed to death. To rescue her soul, Izanagi traveled to Yomi, the realm of darkness and death. Through the black maze he searched, until finally he came upon her, hidden amongst the shadows. He implored her to return with him. She could not, for she had already eaten the fruit that grew in Yomi. Izanagi insisted. He would not leave her in this place. He swore it. Izanami agreed to take her husband to see the Gods of Yomi, to implore them to free her. Meanwhile, remain in darkness, she cautioned him, for the realm of the dead was not meant for the living to see. Taken by foreboding, Izanagi lit a torch and laid eyes upon his wife. No longer the graceful, elegant beauty she once was, Izanami now appeared a rotting corpse, hollow and decayed, maggot ridden and foul. Frightened and disgusted, Izanagi broke his vow and fled. Through the bowels of Yomi he ran, pursued by the fiend that was once his wife. Escaping the cave entrance, Izanagi rolled a boulder in place to block it. Sealed within, betrayed and cursed, consumed with wrathful anger, Izanami swore, one thousand lives would she take each day. One thousand to pay for the broken promise he made. Since then, Izanami has kept her vow, each day reaching beyond the grave to pull souls to Yomi. But war has broken the boulder that once kept her sealed away, and Izanami has emerged, cold and lifeless, but burning with a vengeance against all that lives. One thousand souls will not be enough.
baseInformation.shortRole: Throws Spectral Boomerangs, Fades Into the Shadows

basic.name: Izanami Basic Attack

Gain Physical Penetration and Intelligence for each 1% of your Max Health taken as damage.

   • Each stack fades 10 seconds after the damage was taken.

  • Stack count cannot drop below the percentage of your current missing health.

A01.shortDesc:
Significantly increases your Attack Speed for 6 seconds, while your Basic Attacks deal [100% STR + 20% INT] Physical Damage but no longer pierce enemies or return to you.

   • Basic Attacks deal Bonus Damage to Structures.

  • Applies a stacking Bleed effect to enemies hit.
	  • This bleed deals [30% INT] over 2 seconds. (calculated as the full 2 second DOT per auto)

A02.name: Spectral Projection
A02.shortDesc: Send forth a spectral projection of yourself, dealing [80% STR + 75% INT] Physical Damage and Slowing all Enemies in a line. Applies 25% Healing Reduction.

A03.name: Fade Away
A03.shortDesc:
Descend into the underworld, becoming Stealthed, and leap away from your current position.

   • Gain Movement Speed while in Stealth.

A04.name: Dark Portal
A04.shortDesc:
Summon a dark portal which deals instant [100% STR + 30% INT] Physical Damage and [70% INT] Physical Damage over time.

   • Damaged enemies are also Silenced and Slowed.

tips.title: Shred Tips
tips.value: If you're hitting a single target, be sure to use your 1 as it greatly increases DPS. You can also toggle it off on command by pressing your 'cancel' keybind. (right-click for KBM)

baseInformation.loreShort: Janus, God of Portals and Transitions, stands at every threshold, watching both past and future unfold.
baseInformation.loreLong: Janus, God of Portals and Transitions, stands at every threshold, watching both past and future unfold. With two faces ever turned to what was and what will be, Janus is the keeper of change itself. Doorways, seasons, and the shifting tides of fate all bend to his will. His followers seek him in times of uncertainty, knowing he alone can open the way forward. In battle, Janus is a master of space, bending reality with portals and passages unseen. For him, life is not a straight path but an endless series of doors - and he alone holds the keys.
baseInformation.shortRole: Portal in and Out of Combat, Decimating Your Foes

basic.name: Janus Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals  Physical Damage to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Passages
passive.shortDesc: When you pass through a Portal or Threshold, your next ability deals [20% INT] bonus Magical Damage. Stacks up to 2 times.

A01.name: Portal
A01.shortDesc:
Open a portal on a surface.
• Wall portals create a passage to the other side for yourself
• Ground portals Banishes you and enemies upwards, dealing [70% INT] Magical Damage to enemies on landing

A02.name: Unstable Vortex
A02.shortDesc:
Launch two linked portals, dealing [95% INT] Magical Damage to enemies in their path
• Hitting an enemy with both portals deals [20% INT] bonus Magical Damage
Projectile passes through and damages all enemies, and passes through walls.

A03.name: Threshold
A03.shortDesc:
Unleash a rift that Slows enemies and grants Movement Speed to allies who cross it.
• Slowed enemies are Phased, causing your abilities to deal [25-45% INT] bonus Magical Damage and Magical Damage you deal ignores 15% of their Magical Protection from your abilities
• Passive: You and allies also gain stacking Movement Speed when crossing the rift or passing through a portal

A04.name: Through Space and Time
A04.shortDesc:
Fire a global projectile that deals up to [90% INT] + [110% INT] Magical Damage and opens portals for allies on any surface it passes.

● You are CC Immune while firing
● Damage increases with distance traveled
○ Projectile passes through and damages all enemies, and passes through walls.
○ Cannot deal damage to enemies in the fountain

tips.title: Threshold
tips.value: Place your threshold on enemies to hit your 2 easier and deal bonus damage.

baseInformation.loreShort: Jing Wei, the fierce-winged spirit, whose defiant vow resounds as she tirelessly drops stones into the sea that claimed her life. Once a beloved princess, daughter of the Flame Emperor Yan Di, she drowned pursuing the dawn across vast waters. Reborn as a bird, she now labors endlessly to conquer the ocean, each pebble cast a testament to her unyielding resolve. Her worshipers—those who have faced impossible tasks, the resilient, and the determined—see in her the power of unbreakable oaths. Jing Wei inspires those who defy fate, each stone a symbol of her steadfast defiance against forces that once overpowered her.
baseInformation.loreLong: Against the red rising sun, a winged form sweeps out to sea, claw clutched around a pebble. With focused grit, she hurls the small stone into the watery maw, circling above as it sinks into darkness. Resolved, she turns back to collect another. Jing Wei will not cease until the ocean is filled. That is her oath. And she will not break an oath. Once, she was a young girl, a princess, enamored by the glory of morning light. She gazed in wonder as her father, Yan Di, the Flame Emperor, shepherded the sun from the eastern sea. Desperately, she longed to reach the place where it emerged, but her father refused take her on the waters. One night, the princess sailed out alone, secretly hoping for a clear view come the dawn. But the waters were churning in a storm. Mountainous waves crashed into her tiny ship. The sea swallowed her whole. Yan Di woke and coaxed the sun from the horizon, only to find the shattered remains of his daughter's boat upon the shore. Yet, even as he grieved, a bright goddess burst from the ocean and soared overhead. Jingwei! she cried, and Yan Di knew his daughter was reborn. Though given a second chance that day, Jing Wei swore an oath against the seas that took her life. She would fill them with stone until they were gone. An impossible task. But Jing Wei swore an oath. And Jing Wei will not break an oath.
baseInformation.shortRole: Flies Through Battle With Her Crossbow

basic.name: Jing Wei Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals  Physical Damage to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Rapid Reincarnation
passive.shortDesc:
When exiting the fountain, fly into the sky and soar over obstacles.

 • Select a landing location early or descend when the duration expires

A01.name: Persistent Gust
A01.shortDesc:
Create a Gust of Wind that deals [85% STR] Physical Damage and Knocks Up enemy gods and yourself.

  • The Gust area continually deals [4% STR] Physical Damage
  • You gain increased Attack Speed while in the outer area of the Gust

A02.name: Explosive Bolts
A02.shortDesc:
Your next 3 basic attacks are wider, have increased damage scaling, and explode on the first enemy hit.

  • You gain increased Critical Strike Chance while this ability is active
  • This ability can gain multiple charges

A03.name: Agility
A03.shortDesc:
Dash forward and gain increased Strength.

  • This ability can be used while Displaced, and you will Dash further

A04.name: Air Strike
A04.shortDesc:
Dash forward and fly into the sky, dropping explosives and dealing [115% STR] Physical Damage to enemies beneath you.

  • Select a landing location, or wait to Dash backwards, dealing  Physical Damage to enemies again before landing

tips.title: Explosive Bolt Management
tips.value: Be sure to press your 2 before flying out of base, as you can store up to 3 charges of your 2nd ability at max rank, but this isn't including the 3 bolts that are already loaded. Doing this will net you an additional THREE explosive bolts.

baseInformation.loreShort: Armageddon. Apocalypse. The end times. All myths and legends contain forewarnings of a great, singular catastrophe, when the stars fall from the sky and the very earth breaks apart into nothingness.
baseInformation.loreLong:
Armageddon. Apocalypse. The end times. All myths and legends contain forewarnings of a great, singular catastrophe, when the stars fall from the sky and the very earth breaks apart into nothingness. Among these days of judgement, none are eclipsed in their scale of horror and destruction foretold of more than Ragnarok, the prophesied disaster of the Norse pantheon.

There is a single being who acts as the herald of Ragnarok, the god known as Jormungandr, the World Serpent. A son of Loki, he is an immense, serpentine dragon, massive enough to encircle the entire world and blot the light from the sun. Dark omens are whispered of when the World Serpent will rise, casting his noxious poisons out to engulf the whole of the earth.

The time Jormungandr bestrides the earth is one of unmatched calamity, the days of Ragnarok where the oceans boil and the land quakes. They are days of incredible violence, where both mortal and divine blood shall soak the ground, and the World Serpent will mete out destruction with a fanatic's zeal. He will swim through the earth and seas alike with vast snapping jaws and caustic venom, sweeping away any opponent brave enough to stand against him.

One such champion is Thor, the mighty God of Thunder. Long has his destiny been written that he will confront the World Serpent in the nightmarish days of Ragnarok. It is a clash that will shake creation, rivalling any battle in history, and one that neither Thor nor Jormungandr may ever walk away from.

Woe to any who hears the trumpets of Ragnarok, for they herald the coming of Jormungandr, the instrument of their annihilation. The World Serpent exists as the bearer of the end times, the collapse of all creation. Who could hope to oppose such power?
baseInformation.shortRole: Spread Poison and Defeat Enemies with your Toxic Breath

basic.name: Basic Attacks
basic.shortDesc:
Deal 38 (0 +38) Magical Damage to all enemies in front of you. As you attack, your Overheat increases causing your firing rate to decrease. • Increasing Attack Speed decreases the Overheat speed.
5 attacks per sec. decreasing while firing to a minimum of 2 per sec.
• Basic Attacks proc item hit effects for 40% damage and 20% healing.
• Base Overheat Rate - 1.5 | Base Cooling Rate - 2.5 | Tick Rate - 0.1s
Inhand Movement Penalty is 50%.

passive.name: Immovable
passive.shortDesc:
You are immune to Hard Displacement, but are instead Dazed, a debuff that Slows you and increases the damage you take.
Hits from Basic attacks and Venomous Haze cause enemy gods to gain a debuff stack. At max stacks, the next hit deals damage based on your Max Health or Strength, and resets the debuff.
Hard Displacement effects include: Banish, Grab, Knockback, Levitate and Vortex effects. Jormungandr's breath auto attack hits 5 times a second, proccing auto attack items at 40% damage and 20% healing.

A01.name: Venomous Haze
A01.shortDesc:
Fire noxious spit at an area dealing [40% INT] Magical Damage and creating a toxic cloud that persists for 20s.
• The cloud deals [5% INT] Magical Damage, Slows and reduces enemy Healing.
• While within the cloud, you gain extra Cooling for your Basic Attacks.
• Interacting with a toxic cloud with other abilities reduces their duration to 8s.
You can have a maximum of 9 clouds active at once.
Cooling effect is 25% the base rate.

A02.name: Consuming Bellow
A02.shortDesc:
Channel briefly, drawing power from nearby toxic clouds, then roar to deal [45% INT + 30% STR] Magical Damage, Slow, and Tremble enemies around you.
• Each cloud Empowers you and the roar, increasing its size and causing it to deal [10% INT] bonus Magical Damage per cloud.
• While Empowered, your Basic Attacks fire at their maximum rate.
Size increases up to 3 times but the bonus damage is uncapped.
• Each cloud provides 2s of Basic Attack Empowerment, up to 6s max.

A03.name: Submerge
A03.shortDesc:
Submerge into the ground, becoming Stealthed and gaining Movement Speed. Reactivate to emerge, Knocking Up and dealing [50% INT + 40% STR] Magical Damage to enemies.
• While submerged, you pulse out a ring, gaining vision of enemies.
• Emerging through a toxic cloud creates two new clouds.
Taking damage while in Stealth partially reveals you.

A04.name: The World Serpent
A04.shortDesc:
Enter into the world before diving up to three times across the battlefield. Each time you Submerge you deal [35% INT + 30% STR] Magical Damage to enemies hit.
• Emerging out of the ground and your body Crashing down deals [20% INT + 15% STR] Magical Damage to enemies hit. • Each time you Emerge and Submerge, you Knock Up enemies and leave behind a low duration toxic cloud.
• You are CC Immune during this ability.
If you cannot cannot dive to the target location when the time expires, the ability will end.

tips.title: COMING SOON
tips.value: N/A

aspect.description: Toxic clouds do not deal tick damage but allied gods within them gain Damage Reduction. Within them, you do not gain Cooling but lower cooldown for Venomous Haze. Consuming Bellow does not deal bonus damage but allies gain INT and STR. Submerge Knockup height is increased.

baseInformation.loreShort: Khepri, the Dawn Bringer, whose scarab form rolls the sun across the heavens each morning, renewing life from the shadows of night. As the scarab renews itself from a sphere of dust, so too does Khepri safeguard the cycle of rebirth, guiding the sun through the underworld. His worshipers, drawn from those who seek new beginnings or who cherish the mysteries of life's cycle, look to Khepri for protection against darkness and despair. As gods clash and chaos spreads, Khepri's relentless journey across the sky shines as a beacon of hope, each dawn a quiet promise of endurance.
baseInformation.loreLong: At dawn, the sun lifts above the horizon, bathing mankind in color and life. At dusk, it sinks beneath the edge of the world, lost to sight, casting all into darkness. While the sun shines on the realm of mortal kind it is radiant Ra that guides it, but at night we share the sun with the underworld. It is not Ra that moves the sun along its subterranean path but Khepri, the scarab God, he who comes with the dawn. Scarabs form dirt and dung into perfect spheres. Within, they lay eggs from which their young emerge fully formed. Using powerful arms and legs, scarabs can roll these spheres - many times their own size - incredible distances. It is from their father, Khepri, the sacred scarab gained this power. For the Dawn Bringer, the Sun is his sphere and all souls, living and dead, his children. Khepri is a guardian, a protector and a friend. He is a symbol of power and loyalty. Usually content to stay aloof, Khepri has risen for battle to defend existence from this shattering war. He will fight to shield us until the final sunset. Yet, it is he that brings the dawn.
baseInformation.shortRole: Resurrects Allies, Pulls Enemies Into His Grasp

basic.name: Khepri Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal  Magical Damage</> to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Fortitude
passive.shortDesc:
Every 5s apply a 2% Health Shield to yourself and nearby allies.

 • The interval is increased to 10s if you have taken or dealt damage in the last 5s

A01.name: Abduct
A01.shortDesc:
Lunge forward, grabbing and pulling backwards the first enemy god hit.

 • This deals [40% INT] Magical Damage
	• Gain Physical Protection and Magical Protection while pulling a god and for 3 seconds after.
	• The lunge damages enemies you pass through

A02.name: Rising Dawn
A02.shortDesc:
Roll the sun forward, setting ablaze all enemies it touches.

 • Affected enemies repeatedly take [5% INT] Magical Damage over time (once every .3s for 3s, 11 ticks total)
	• Affected enemies have reduced Physical Protection.
	• Allies hit gain Damage Mitigation
	• After reaching max distance the sun sets, reapplying all effects in a larger area.

A03.name: Solar Flare
A03.shortDesc: Call down the sun's ire, dealing [30% INT] Magical Damage and Rooting enemies in an area.

A04.name: Scarab's Blessing
A04.shortDesc:
Bless an allied god for 5s. If the blessed ally would die while blessed, they are instead revived at Khepri's location. 

 • Blessed allies are cleansed of crowd control effects
	• Blessed allies receive Strength, Intelligence, and Movement Speed
	• Blessed allies are Slow Immune
	• Successfully Revive an Ally to gain a permanently stacking buff of 2 Protections

tips.title: Peeling/Setting Up Tips
tips.value: Before grabbing (using Ambush) an enemy god either to peel them off of your teammate or to setup for a teammate, be sure to apply your 2, as it both gives damage mitigation to your ally, while shredding the enemy's physical resistance.

aspect.description: Abduct no longer Pulls enemy gods but Roots them in place with you, dealing constant Basic Attack Damage for the duration.

baseInformation.loreShort: To speak of Kali is to speak of the beginning of time itself.
baseInformation.loreLong:
To speak of Kali is to speak of the beginning of time itself.

Across the majestic lands of Hindustan rose an army of demons that conquered the countryside and slaughtered the people. Yet, when the Gods themselves were defeated, Devi, the supreme Goddess, emerged to throw back this host and protect the faithful.

Three forms she took during these ancient times. The first, Durga, the Mother of Creation, was as radiant as she was fearsome. Astride her tiger, she scattered the demon horde until she encountered the horrendous Raktabija. For each wound she gave the demon, his cursed blood created a copy, quickly resulting in an unstoppable army of Raktabija clones. Rage boiled at the corners of Durga's mind, in the quest to slaughter and destroy evil. From the forehead of Durga sprung her wrathful form, with skin darkened and eyes glowing a menacing red.

Thus was Kali, the Black One, Goddess of Time and Destruction, the second form of Devi born.

Rarely has Kali been seen since, kept in the shadows of Devi's mind, but when the tides of battle shift and evil looms large, there is no solution but unrestrained destruction of evil, and Kali once again, walks the Earth
baseInformation.shortRole: Bleeds Enemies, Bringing Death to Foes with a Storm of Blades.

basic.name: Kali Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal  Physical Damage</> to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Marked for Death
passive.shortDesc:
Mark a god. Gain Physical Penetration against that god. Killing that god restores Health [20% Max HP]. Assists restore less Health [7.5% Max HP]. You also gain bonus gold bounty [15% Bonus Gold]. Additionally, Basic Attacks and most abilities apply Rupture to enemies.
				
• If a marked target dies, the enemy god closest to Kali becomes marked
				
• You can use 'Interact' in the fountain to change your marked target

A01.name: Nimble Strike
A01.shortDesc:
Leap dealing [80% STR] Physical Damage to enemies when landing. Heal [20-60] Health if this ability landed on an enemy.
• If you landed on an enemy god, heal for 6% of their missing health
• If you landed on multiple gods, restore based on the god that would heal you the most
• Applies 3 stacks of Rupture

A02.name: Lash
A02.shortDesc:
Throw 3 blades forward, each dealing [30% STR + 35% INT] Physical Damage and applying a bleed. The bleed deals [5% STR + 10% INT] Physical Damage every .5s for 3s.
				
• Applies 1 stack of Rupture for each blade hit

A03.name: Incense
A03.shortDesc:
Stun enemies with incense, gaining bonus STR and INT. Siphon targets, dealing [15% STR + 40% INT] Physical Damage and healing 8-16 Health per hit. Deal [10% STR + 12.5% INT] Physical Damage and  Heal [4% INT] per Rupture stack, consuming them.

• All heals after the first are reduced by 80%. The strongest heal is resolved first.

• Non-god targets with a stack of Rupture are damaged and restore health as if they had 3 stacks.

A04.name: Destruction
A04.shortDesc:
Become immune to death, displacement, slows, and roots. Deal [4% STR + 6.5% INT] Physical Damage to nearby enemies every 0.25s for its duration.
• If you are below 15% Health when the ultimate ends, you are restored to 15% of your Health
• Each hit applies 1 stack of Rupture
