baseInformation.loreShort: Kukulkan, the Feathered Serpent, whose majestic form rides the winds of the Mayan lands. Known as the bringer of wind and rebirth, his tempests cast shadows that flicker with his likeness, stirring both awe and reverence. Kukulkan's power flows through the air itself, said to be held in a jewel around his neck, inspiring worship from those who revere transformation and the cycles of nature. His followers offer sacrifice and prayers, knowing that his favor can change fortunes or bring ruinous storms. As war brews among the gods, Kukulkan soars with a fury that demands divine blood, not merely mortal devotion.
baseInformation.loreLong: Upon the high winds of Maya lands rides the feathered serpent, Kukulkan. Awesome and terrible to behold, majestic yet sinister, Kukulkan embodies the danger and beauty of a double-edged sword. Time has worn away knowledge of this God like the breeze over stone. Perhaps Kukulkan prefers this. He is known by many names and worn the face of man and beast - and worshipped with the kind of reverence born of fear. Dotted across the vast forests of the Maya lands stand great pyramids bearing his serpentine likeness, architecturally perfect so as to catch the light and bring to life those slithering stone statues. Painted in blood, sacrifices were made upon these sites to appease Kukulkan. He is the lord of death and rebirth. To the world, he brought the four elements, but of wind he keeps control. A great gem, worn about his sinuous neck is said to be the source of all air. To the people, he brought the calendar. That glorious, portentous stone disk, which counts time to a distant future, only to stop abruptly and with horrifying finality. To the battlefield, he brings a legacy of power and domination. Sacrifices made for his favor were measured in blood, but mortal blood is weak. It seems that, for Kukulkan's ultimate appeasement, the blood of a God is required.
baseInformation.shortRole: Summons Tornadoes and Deadly Winds

basic.name: Kukulkan Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals  Magical Damage to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Power Of The Wind Jewel
passive.shortDesc: As you build items that provide Mana, you gain bonus Intelligence.

A01.name: Zephyr
A01.shortDesc:
Blow a breezy projectile that explodes when it hits an enemy, dealing [80% INT] Magical Damage in a small area.

  • Damaged enemies are also Slowed

A02.name: Slipstream
A02.shortDesc:
Summon the wind to become Slow Immune and gain Movement Speed that fades over time. 

  • Activate this within a Whirlwind to instead Dash, dealing [80% INT] Magical Damage and Slowing enemies hit

A03.name: Whirlwind
A03.shortDesc:
Create a Whirlwind, which spreads onto all enemies that enter its area.

  • Affected enemies repeatedly take [20% INT] Magical Damage per tick; 6 times over 2.5 seconds, even if they leave the area

A04.name: Spirit Of The Nine Winds
A04.shortDesc:
Summon the Spirit of the Nine Winds to deal [115% INT] Magical Damage in a large path.

  • Enemies hit are also Knocked Back

tips.title: Ultimate Tips
tips.value: Similar to Ra, try to position behind walls and/or wait for setup to ult. You can also use your 1 to setup for your ult as the slow is MASSIVE and lasts 3 seconds.

tips.title: Securing Tips
tips.value: Your ultimate ability, Spirit of The Nine Winds is THE best securing tool in the game, be sure to use it as an objective is getting low to ensure your team gets the objective. (the definition of 'low' changes throughout the game as you get more power online but normally at around 15-20% health is suffecient)

aspect.description: You gain Mana from Jungle monster kills and god kills and assists. Zephyr has a shorter range but fires 3 projectiles. Slipstream is always a dash and shreds protections. Whirlwind attaches to you and explodes in a blast that knocks back enemies.

passive.name: Power Of The Wind Jewel
passive.shortDesc: As you build items that provide Mana, you gain bonus Intelligence.

A01.name: Zephyr
A01.shortDesc:
Blow a breezy projectile that explodes when it hits an enemy, dealing [80% INT] Magical Damage in a small area.

  • Damaged enemies are also Slowed

A02.name: Slipstream
A02.shortDesc:
Summon the wind to become Slow Immune and gain Movement Speed that fades over time. 

  • Activate this within a Whirlwind to instead Dash, dealing [80% INT] Magical Damage and Slowing enemies hit

A03.name: Whirlwind
A03.shortDesc:
Create a Whirlwind, which spreads onto all enemies that enter its area.

  • Affected enemies repeatedly take [20% INT] Magical Damage per tick; 6 times over 2.5 seconds, even if they leave the area

A04.name: Spirit Of The Nine Winds
A04.shortDesc:
Summon the Spirit of the Nine Winds to deal [115% INT] Magical Damage in a large path.

  • Enemies hit are also Knocked Back

baseInformation.loreShort: Loki, the Trickster God, whose schemes and mischievous laughter twist through the tales of Asgard like a serpent through grass. Loki has aided the gods but betrayed them just as quickly, his chaotic nature both a blessing and a curse. It was he who orchestrated the tragic death of Baldr, securing his legacy in both triumph and infamy. His worshipers, often clever rogues who value cunning over strength, see in him the freedom to defy fate and disrupt order. With Ragnarok looming, Loki's boundless mischief may soon unravel destiny itself, and his followers revel, knowing their god walks a path no one else dares tread.
baseInformation.loreLong: Villain. It's a word used to describe those that break the rules, that take what they want, and care nothing for those hurt along the way. Loki, the trickster God, would say villainy is nothing more than a point of view. The point of view of fools too mindless to seize opportunity. Of course, with Loki now on the loose, the implications are dire. The end of times may be at hand. The final battle, Ragnarok, possibly on the horizon, for it was foretold that Loki would break free of his prison and herald the horrific final battle that would leave the Gods slain, the heavens sundered, and the world in ashes. None are more eager for that time of chaos than Loki. Before his confinement, Loki?s malicious mischief managed to affect every God in ways both beneficial and terrible. Yet Loki?s most heinous act was in the death of the God of light, Baldr. Baldr?s prophetic dreams showcased his own death. Fearful for her son, Frigg forced all objects of the world to swear never to harm him. All save mistletoe. Cruelly amused, Loki forged a spear from the plant and provided it to Baldr?s brother Hodr. The Gods had a new favorite game, hurling objects at Baldr and laughing as they harmlessly ricocheted. So Hodr threw the spear, but, to everyone?s horror Baldr was impaled. Hel agreed to release Baldr from the underworld if all creatures of the world mourned the God of Light. And all did, save one crone who refused. So Baldr died. When it was discovered the crone was Loki in disguise, the furious Gods bound him in entrails and hung a venomous serpent overhead. Loki?s wife, Sigyn, collected the dripping venom in a bowl, but when she was forced to empty it, Loki was struck, causing such anguish his thrashing would shake the world. But now Loki is free and already wreaking havoc. If the prophetic Volva are correct, and they always are, then the end of all things has finally come.
baseInformation.shortRole: Turns Invisible and Backstabs His Foes

basic.name: Loki Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal  Physical Damage to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Behind You
passive.shortDesc: Hitting enemies from behind deals bonus damage. This is also triggered on enemies Blinded by Agonizing Visions.

A01.name: Vanish
A01.shortDesc:
Disappear in a puff of smoke, become Stealth and gain Movement Speed. 

  • Your next basic attack deals additional [20% STR] Physical Damage over time
  • Killing a god resets the cooldown of this ability
  • You are Immune to Slows while Stealthed

A02.name: Agonizing Visions
A02.shortDesc:
Summon a vision of Loki that repeatedly deals [15% STR] Physical Damage to nearby enemies.

  • Damaged enemies have reduced damage
  • Enemies hit four times are Blinded

A03.name: Flurry Strike
A03.shortDesc:
Unleash a flurry of dagger strikes, dealing [25% STR] Physical Damage repeatedly and Slowing enemies in front of you.   
  • The final hit deals [60% STR] Physical Damage and applies a stronger Slow  
  • This is a Channeled ability  
  • You are Displacement Immune  while Channeling

A04.name: Assassinate
A04.shortDesc:
Teleport to the target location, then strike twice to assassinate a foe.

  • The first strike deals [35% STR] Physical Damage and Cripple enemies in the area
  • The second strike deals [80% STR] Physical Damage and Stuns enemies in front of you

tips.title: Ulting Tips
tips.value: Don't ult in first when attempting to gank/kill an enemy, especially if they have a leap/dash. Dump your kit on them first then ult to teleport after they burn their escape.

tips.title: Stealth
tips.value: Jumping while stealthed helps minimize your footstep noise.

aspect.description: Vanish no longer grants Stealth; its attack now inflicts Blind instead of Bleed. Agonizing Visions can be cast on allies, providing damage mitigation while slowing enemies. Behind You now deals bonus damage scaling with your Health and Protections rather than its percentage increase.

baseInformation.loreShort: Medusa, the Gorgon, whose very gaze turns flesh to stone, embodies vengeance born of sorrow. Once beautiful, she was cursed by Athena, transforming her hair to writhing snakes and her beauty to horror. Her worshipers are those who have suffered unjustly, who turn pain into power and find strength in defiance. They embrace her visage as a protective shield, a warning to foes who might cross them. Now, Medusa enters the godly fray, seeking to turn her horror into a weapon, ensuring that all who meet her gaze pay the price for her suffering.
baseInformation.loreLong: All monsters are hideous and fearful, but some are worse than others. There is only one whose hair is made of slithering serpents, only one with skin of scales, and only one whose very gaze can turn man, beast, or God to stone. Medusa, the Gorgon. Stories claim she was beautiful once. A priestess of Athena. Loyal and devout in action, but Poseidon was determined to make her his own. It was the fairness of her features, the golden of her hair, that attracted the Sea God. Medusa, a mortal maiden, could not refuse. When Athena learned of Medusa's betrayal, she punished the priestess. Those fair features were hideously wrought and those golden locks twisted to hissing vipers. Anyone that met her eyes was petrified ? a statue for all eternity. Medusa fled, frightened, ashamed and furious. For years she hid from the world, nursing a festering hatred for all things, craving beauty but having none, seeing none, until she became the monster she appeared to be. She slithers now to the field of battle. Those wretched features concealed behind a cruel mask of porcelain perfection, eyes burning from within. All that she perceives, all that is wonderful in this world, she seeks to destroy. Perhaps then she will be the fairest monster of them all.
baseInformation.shortRole: Slays Enemies with Venom and Turns Them to Stone

basic.name: Medusa Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals <Damage>{Damage_Formula} Physical Damage</> to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Sidewinder
passive.shortDesc: You suffer no movement penalty when strafing and half the movement penalty when backpedaling.

A01.name: Viper Shot
A01.shortDesc:
Gain Attack Speed and augment your next 4 Basic Attacks, shooting vipers that poison the targets hit, dealing [8% STR + 8% INT] Physical Damage every 0.5s for 1.5s.

  • Each successful viper hit on an enemy god reduces the Cooldown of this ability by 0.5s

A02.name: Acid Spray
A02.shortDesc:
Spit acid that deals [65% STR + 80% INT] Physical Damage to the first enemy hit, then spraying out in a cone behind them.

 
	• Enemies hit have their Healing Taken reduced for 5s
	• Acid Spray will explode on statues Medusa has created

A03.name: Lacerate
A03.shortDesc:
Slither forward, dealing [75% STR + 40% INT] Physical Damage to all enemies you pass through.

 • The first enemy god you encounter causes you to stop before damaging and Rooting the god hit

A04.name: Petrify
A04.shortDesc:
Remove your mask, revealing your true face to deliver a powerful blast from your horrifying gorgon gaze.

 • Enemies are dealt [80% STR + 95% INT] Physical Damage and Stunned if they look at you
	• Enemies are dealt [70% STR + 95% INT] Physical Damage and Slowed if they are not looking at you
	• You are CC Immune while using this ability

tips.title: Trading Tips
tips.value: While boxing, or before all-inning, be sure to use your 2, Venomous Spit to apply antiheal, then use your 1 and 3 to quickly barrel-stuff all 4 charges of Viper Shot, instantly killing most squishies mid-late game.

baseInformation.loreShort: Mercurial. A word that evokes volatility, quickness, and cunning. But to compare anything to the speed and wit of the Messenger God Mercury would be to compare a hot day to the searing forge of Vulcan, or a blooming rose to the glory of Venus.
baseInformation.loreLong: Mercurial. A word that evokes volatility, quickness, and cunning. But to compare anything to the speed and wit of the Messenger God Mercury would be to compare a hot day to the searing forge of Vulcan, or a blooming rose to the glory of Venus. It is but a pale comparison. Mercury can leap the space between lightning and thunder, outfox the fox king, and reach tomorrow before the sun.As an infant, Mercury escaped the watchful eye of his half-brother, Apollo, stole his cattle, fashioned a lyre from a tortoise shell, and snuck in a nap, all before Apollo found him missing. His father, Jupiter, found the child's mischief boisterously entertaining, and awarded him the task of delivering messages for the Gods, traveling between the Heavens, the Earth, and even to the depths of the Underworld.Now war looms and Mercury races to keep the lines of communication open, though he is often caught in the thick of battle. No stranger to combat, he once slew the monster Argus, the demon with one hundred watchful eyes, using speed and guile alone. But this war is different. The foes are stronger, the stakes higher, and Mercury a pivotal cog in the machine. Should he fail to deliver even a single order the balance could tip, and the time left for this world would be truly mercurial.
baseInformation.shortRole: Move fast and strike faster. Rush forward and unleash your fists.

passive.name: Fastest God Alive
passive.shortDesc: Gain bonus basic damage on your next basic based on distance travelled. Gain Strength from Movement Speed.

Bonus Basic Damage: 0.04% per 1 cm Travelled, up to a max of 20%. [Automatically calculated at 20% for S2 Calculator]
Strength Gained: 25% of Movement Speed from Items or Abilities

A01.name: Made You Look
A01.shortDesc: Move in the blink of an eye, dealing [70% Strength] Physical Damage to every enemy within a target area. Can critically hit and trigger basic attack AND ability item effects.

A02.name: Maximum Velocity
A02.shortDesc: Passive: Gain Movement Speed. Active: Gain Attack Speed, cleanse slows, and become immune to slows.

A03.name: Special Delivery
A03.shortDesc:
You dash forward, knocking minions aside, dealing [64% STR] Physical Damage to them. Grab the first enemy god encountered, spinning them, dealing [20% STR] Physical damage every 0.25s until the effect ends. They are then thrown in the direction you are facing 
This ability can be cancelled early.

A04.name: Sonic Boom
A04.shortDesc: Gather energy for a powerful Dash. The range increases as you charge. Dash forward, dealing [100% STR] Physical Damage and spinning enemies hit along the dash. Gain Movement Speed after the dash that scales based on the energy gathered.

baseInformation.loreShort: Many know the legend of King Arthur, and yet the man who set that legend into motion remains shrouded in mystery. His origins, and the source of his power, are secrets known only to him. Even his very nature is questioned. Is he simply a man greatly adept in the magical arts? A demon? Or something in between? Whatever the answer, what is beyond doubt to all who encounter him is that Arthur's right hand, the great wizard Merlin, is a being of immense wisdom, and formidable strength.
baseInformation.loreLong:
Many know the legend of King Arthur, and yet the man who set that legend into motion remains shrouded in mystery. His origins, and the source of his power, are secrets known only to him. Even his very nature is questioned. Is he simply a man greatly adept in the magical arts? A demon? Or something in between? Whatever the answer, what is beyond doubt to all who encounter him is that Arthur's right hand, the great wizard Merlin, is a being of immense wisdom, and formidable strength.

In the ages of warring kings, where ruler vied against ruler and only the strongest rose to rule the land, Merlin pledged his skills in service to great sovereigns as both an advisor and a devastating battle mage. A collector of secrets and ancient lore, Merlin is a master of arcane wizardry, able to wield a myriad of elements and harness them to his will. He also devoted his mind to the art of prophesy, one day foreseeing the rise of a ruler destined to stand above all others, and unite the land beneath his banner.

When Uther Pendragon fell in battle against the barbarous hordes of the invading Saxons, Merlin swore an oath of fealty to his successor, Arthur. From that day, the mage never left the wielder of legendary Excalibur's side, a tireless advisor, fellow warrior, and friend. Together they brought about the rise of Camelot, and the unity of a once fractured land. The foul Saxons were driven back across the distant sea, and the people of the kingdom knew peace, justice and prosperity.

To any who think to cross Merlin, beware. Many have tested him, seeking his knowledge, his secrets, or the source of his power for themselves. And few, if any, have lived to tell their tale.
baseInformation.shortRole: Switches Stances, Casting Spells.

basic.name: The Morrigan Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Throw a projectile forward that deals  Magical Damage to enemies hit.

passive.name: Overload
passive.shortDesc: Using an ability provides 1 stack of Overload. Firing a basic attack consumed 1 stack of Overload and deals [20% INT] Magical Damage to enemies hit. This can trigger ability item effects.

arcane.name: Eclipse
arcane.shortDesc:
• Arcane: Conjure a celestial orb that grows in size as it travels, dealing [55% INT] Magical Damage repeatedly to enemies in an area and marking them.
• Marked enemies take additional [10% INT] Magical Damage if they stay within the outer range of Eclipse
• Marked enemies take damage every 0.4s while within the outer range of Eclipse

fire.name: Radiate
fire.shortDesc:
• Fire: Channels a beam of intense fire for 2s that deals [20% INT] Magical Damage every 0.25s.
• Each time Radiate hits an enemy, it also applies a Burn, dealing additional [5% INT] Magical Damage over time
• Burn does damage every 0.5s for 2s.

ice.name: Frostbolt
ice.shortDesc:
• Ice: Fire a Frostbolt that explodes when it hits an enemy, dealing [55% INT] Magical Damage in a small area.
• This ability deals an additional 15% damage if the enemy hit is slowed
• Projectile stops on walls.

arcane.name: Vortex
arcane.shortDesc: • Arcane: Deploy a field of arcane energy. After a delay, the Vortex deals [70% INT] Magical Damage and Pulls enemies towards the center.

fire.name: Dragonfire
fire.shortDesc:
• Fire: You summon forth two ethereal dragons deal [20% INT] Magical Damage to enemies every 0.25s.
• If caught in the center of the area, enemies are affliced by the Radiate Burn and gods have their Protections reduced for 4 seconds.
• The dragons persist for 3s.

ice.name: Blizzard
ice.shortDesc:
• Ice: Create a Blizzard, dealing [15% INT] Magical Damage repeatedly to enemies in an area.
• Enemies hit are debuffed with a stacking Slow
• This ability hits 8 times over 4s.

arcane.name: Flicker (Arcane)
arcane.shortDesc: • Arcane: Heal 30% of the damage taken within the last 4s

fire.name: Flicker (Fire)
fire.shortDesc: • Fire: For a short duration, your basic attacks apply the Radiate debuff effect to enemies hit

arcane.name: Elemental Mastery (Arcane)
arcane.shortDesc:
Tap into your inner potential, exploding with energy in your current stance dealing [15% INT] Magical Damage to enemies hit.
• After the explosion reaches its apex, it collapses in on itself dealing [15% INT] Magical Damage again
• During the explosion time, you can choose which stance to enter into next 
• Arcane - Enemies hit are Knocked Up

fire.name: Elemental Mastery (Fire)
fire.shortDesc:
Tap into your inner potential, exploding with energy in your current stance dealing [15% INT] Magical Damage to enemies hit.
• After the explosion reaches its apex, it collapses in on itself dealing [15% INT] Magical Damage again
• During the explosion time, you can choose which stance to enter into next 
• Fire - Enemies hit are afflicted the Radiate Burn

ice.name: Elemental Mastery (Ice)
ice.shortDesc:
Tap into your inner potential, exploding with energy in your current stance dealing [15% INT] Magical Damage to enemies hit.
• After the explosion reaches its apex, it collapses in on itself dealing [15% INT] Magical Damage again
• During the explosion time, you can choose which stance to enter into next 
• Ice - Enemies hit are Slowed

builds.author: Mytharria
builds.notes: INT-oriented burst mage build. (CLEAR: Start in fire stance, then switch to ice.)

tips.title: Starting Stance Tips
tips.value: When starting a match, start off in Fire Stance. This way you can use your 2 which shreds the protections of the wave, then switch to Ice Stance using your Blizzard to quickly clear wave.

tips.title: Stance Switching Tips
tips.value: Each stance has a dedicated role. Ice is for poking/clear, Fire is for objective burning/1v1 shred, and Arcane is for zoning/self-peel. Late game you can look to walk around in Arcane stance, that way if you get dove, you can use your Arcane flicker to heal 30% of any recently taken damage, then self-peel with 2 knockups, before switching into Fire stance to quickly shred your opponent.

aspect.description: Elemental Mastery has a reduced cooldown [20 Seconds] and each time it is used, a random Stance is selected with 2 random primary abilities, Flicker will always persist. Using Elemental Mastery provides an Intelligence buff for a short duration.

baseInformation.loreShort: Tales are spun and the soft murmurs of the name Morgan Le Fay are whispered in groves, villages, and kingdoms alike. The dark enchantress of ill repute was not always so—she studied under Merlin in Camelot's courts, grew close to King Arthur, and was driven away by Guinevere's schemes. With Tiamat's arrival and the gods unprepared, the time has finally come for her to take the throne.
baseInformation.loreLong:
Tales are spun and the soft murmurs of the name Morgan Le Fay are whispered in groves, villages, and kingdoms alike. The dark enchantress of ill repute was not always so, and her story matured in the great kingdom of Camelot itself.

Morgan Le Fay roamed the courts of Camelot inspiring awe and drawing many a knight's gaze in her direction. In the libraries, she was found tutoring under the master wizard, Merlin. It was in those dusty halls, filled with arcane grimoires, tomes and parchments littered with enchantments, that she learned to hone in on her talents as a sorceress, revealing new avenues of power that were previously hidden.

King Arthur and Morgan Le Fay grew closer over their time together in Camelot, frequently seen hand-in-hand at the royal dances, side-by-side in the war room discussing strategies and plans of attack. There was another, however, that did not enjoy how close the two were getting.

Queen Guinevere lurked those same hallways, quiet and frustrated to be seemingly left behind and forgotten. In those moments alone that she still maintained with the King, she sowed seeds of distrust in Arthur's mind and from Arthur in turn to Merlin.

The schemes were brought to fruition after a heated exchange took place between Morgan Le Fay, King Arthur and Merlin. Angered at being shamed for trivial reasons in full view of his loyal subjects, Arthur used the arsenal of thoughts provided by the Queen and brandished them as a weapon against Morgan Le Fay. She was accused of insubordination and conspiracy to usurp the throne. Abashed at the groundless allegations and bitter that not even her mentor would come to her aid, Morgan Le Fay's sorrow transformed into utter fury as she condemned the royal pair. Turning away, she blasted open the doors of the throne room, leaving them broken and charred as she walked away from Camelot, making a decision at that moment only to return when she was prepared to truly take the throne.

With Tiamat's arrival, the gods on edge and unprepared, that time has finally come.
baseInformation.shortRole: Marks Enemies, Consumes Power, Devastating Ult

basic.name: Morgan Le Fay Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Throw a projectile forward that deals Magical Damage to enemies hit.

passive.name: Empowered Blade
passive.shortDesc: Damaging enemy gods with abilities Marks them and activates a shared Symbol on your sword, providing Mana Regen. After gaining all 5 Symbols you become empowered temporarily, causing your Cooldowns to be decreased and providing Intelligence and Mana Regen. Additionally you gain 1 stack of permanent Intelligence and bonus healing towards Consuming Power.

A01.name: Sigil Mastery
A01.shortDesc:
Summon a crashing sword, imbued by your chosen sigil, dealing [50% INT] Magical Damage before exploding dealing [30% INT] Magical Damage.

• Mark of Mind: Fear enemy gods away from the center of the sigil
• Mark of Body: Create a Slowing field
• Mark of Soul: Spawn a clone at the enemy god location
• During the targeting of this ability, you can choose which sigil to imbue.
• Clones last 4s, inherit 30% of your Health and 75% of your Attack damage.

A02.name: Dragonflight
A02.shortDesc:
Summon a dragon that deals [35% INT] Magical Damage and Knocks Up enemies. The dragon then flies forward, dealing [50% INT] Magical Damage and Knocking Back enemies hit.

• Applies Mark of Spirit to enemy gods hit.

A03.name: Shroud of Wildfire
A03.shortDesc:
Send out Wildfire that explodes at max range or when it hits an enemy god, dealing [35% INT] Magical Damage in a small area and afflicts enemies with a Debuff. Enemies debuffed take [5% INT] Magical Damage. Enemy gods debuffed explode for [10% INT] Magical Damage when hit by your Attacks. If an enemy uses a Movement ability while debuffed, they take [20% INT] Magical Damage and the debuff is removed.

• Using this ability increases your Movement Speed for a short duration
• Your Attacks extend the debuff on enemies
• Applies Mark of Matter to enemy gods hit
• Burn does damage every 1s for 4s and can be extended 1s per Attack, up to an additional 4s.

A04.name: Consuming Power
A04.shortDesc:
Consume Marks from enemy gods dealing [10% INT] Magical Damage and empowering you. For a short duration, fire out 3 strikes of dark energy dealing [40% INT] Magical Damage and Healing you for [50] and a percentage of your Missing Health.

• The center of the final strike deals bonus damage based on the enemy's Max Health (5%)
• You are CC Immune during this ability
• Unique Marks consumed increase the width of the energy projectiles
• Stacks of Empowered Blade provide bonus Healing (4 per stack)
• You ignore all movement penalties for the duration.

aspect.description: Your Basic Attacks are now melee. You gain Wrath over time and when you deal damage. Consuming Power has two levels that can be used at 40 and 80 Wrath. Sigil Mastery fires all 3 Sigils simultaneously. Shroud of Wildfire Attack explosions deal a percentage of your Max Health as damage. Empowered Blade provides bonus maximum health instead of Intelligence. Consuming Power at 40 Wrath causes you to deal [10% INT] Magical Damage to enemies in front of you, healing you for each god hit. This occurs multiple times for each mark consumed.Consuming Power at 80 Wrath causes you to become CC Immune and re-fire to dash up to three times, dealing damage and slowing enemies hit. This dash also heals you and provides protections for each god hit. (Max 3)
aspect.name: Aspect of the Cursed Crown

basic.name: Morgan Le Fay Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Throw a projectile forward that deals Magical Damage to enemies hit.

passive.name: Empowered Blade
passive.shortDesc: Damaging enemy gods with abilities Marks them and activates a shared Symbol on your sword, providing Mana Regen. After gaining all 5 Symbols you become empowered temporarily, causing your Cooldowns to be decreased and providing Intelligence and Mana Regen. Additionally you gain 1 stack of permanent Intelligence and bonus healing towards Consuming Power.

A01.name: Sigil Mastery
A01.shortDesc:
Summon a crashing sword, imbued by your chosen sigil, dealing [50% INT] Magical Damage before exploding dealing [30% INT] Magical Damage.

• Mark of Mind: Fear enemy gods away from the center of the sigil
• Mark of Body: Create a Slowing field
• Mark of Soul: Spawn a clone at the enemy god location
• During the targeting of this ability, you can choose which sigil to imbue.
• Clones last 4s, inherit 30% of your Health and 75% of your Attack damage.

A02.name: Dragonflight
A02.shortDesc:
Summon a dragon that deals [35% INT] Magical Damage and Knocks Up enemies. The dragon then flies forward, dealing [50% INT] Magical Damage and Knocking Back enemies hit.

• Applies Mark of Spirit to enemy gods hit.

A03.name: Shroud of Wildfire
A03.shortDesc:
Send out Wildfire that explodes at max range or when it hits an enemy god, dealing [35% INT] Magical Damage in a small area and afflicts enemies with a Debuff. Enemies debuffed take [5% INT] Magical Damage. Enemy gods debuffed explode for [10% INT] Magical Damage when hit by your Attacks. If an enemy uses a Movement ability while debuffed, they take [20% INT] Magical Damage and the debuff is removed.

• Using this ability increases your Movement Speed for a short duration
• Your Attacks extend the debuff on enemies
• Applies Mark of Matter to enemy gods hit
• Burn does damage every 1s for 4s and can be extended 1s per Attack, up to an additional 4s.

A04.name: Consuming Power
A04.shortDesc:
Consume Marks from enemy gods dealing [10% INT] Magical Damage and empowering you. For a short duration, fire out 3 strikes of dark energy dealing [40% INT] Magical Damage and Healing you for [50] and a percentage of your Missing Health.

• The center of the final strike deals bonus damage based on the enemy's Max Health (5%)
• You are CC Immune during this ability
• Unique Marks consumed increase the width of the energy projectiles
• Stacks of Empowered Blade provide bonus Healing (4 per stack)
• You ignore all movement penalties for the duration.

baseInformation.loreShort: Mordred, the shadow of Camelot, whose hatred for King Arthur, his uncle and king, kindles a flame of vengeance that will not be snuffed. Cursed from birth, Mordred grew in the shadow of a prophecy foretelling Arthur's demise by his hand, a destiny that fueled his wrath and desire for retribution. His followers are those wronged by family or bound to dark fates, those who seek justice by any means necessary. In him, they find the strength to defy legacy and rewrite destiny. As he takes the field, the end of Camelot echoes within him—a necessary betrayal that could yet reshape the world.
baseInformation.loreLong: Throughout many legends and tales of King Arthur, details around the man that leads to his demise can differ dramatically. One thing is constant, however: his Wrath. Mordred, Breaker of the Round Table, seeks nothing more than to destroy the man who led his family to ruin long ago. His father King Lot, brother of Arthur, ruled prior to the young king - first as an adversary and then as allies. Prophecy foretells that Arthur will fall to a child born on the first of May, and in a desperate move Arthur has all children of that year rounded up and shipped away. This ship would find itself to sink, and while many are presumed dead, Mordred survives and grows up with an unbridled hatred of his uncle. Lot would take up arms against Arthur once more, fighting alongside foes seeking to depose him. One such battle would lead to Lot?s death at the hands of King Pellinore, spurred on by Merlin. This act would cause a schism that never would heal, pushing Mordred to seek revenge at every turn. Over decades, he plotted and fought in many battles that saw him rise to a position of influence and fear. He took on his foes with a ferocity that spread to those he led in battle, causing a group of Knights to dedicate themselves to his cause. As his forces grew and his own legend spread, he finally found himself in position to launch his betrayal against the Crown. The two sides would struggle for control of the Kingdom many times, leaving Mordred more and more corrupted by magical afflictions he sustained each mutinous rule. Every time, his Wrath only grows and his thirst for revenge pushes him to reclaim it forever.
baseInformation.shortRole: Charges Into Battle and Bleeds His Foes

basic.name: Mordred Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal  Physical Damage to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Wrath of the Forsaken
passive.shortDesc:
Hit enemies to gain Wrath.
   • Your Ultimate Ability Heart Slash can only be used after reaching 40 Wrath
 • Your Ultimate Ability Pursue the Weak can only be used after reaching 80 Wrath
After using an ability or attacking, your Interact key becomes a Dash forward.
  • This deals  Physical Damage.

A01.name: Cruel Strikes
A01.shortDesc:
Strike twice with your swords.

  • First Slash, dealing [45% STR] Physical Damage to enemies in front of you

   • Then Slam, dealing [65% STR] Physical Damage to enemies in a line and either dealing [45% Protections] bonus damage or [25% Protections] Healing, based on your Basic Attack Chain

A02.name: Bloodrage
A02.shortDesc:
Deal [60% STR + 40% INT] Physical Damage to enemies around you, then gain increased Attack Speed and Movement Speed.

   • While buffed, basic attack hits on enemies reduce their Physical Protections and increase your own.  This can stack up to 5 times, and the Bonus Protections are refreshed while the Attack Speed buff ends when the Movement Speed Buff ends.

A03.name: Severing Slice
A03.shortDesc:
Fire a projectile that deals [50% INT] Physical Damage and Slows enemies.

  • Direct hits with the center of the projectile cause targets to Bleed, taking [12.5% STR] Bonus Physical Damage as they move.

A04.name: Heart Slash
A04.shortDesc:
Deal [75% STR] Physical Damage to enemies in an area in front of you. If you hit an enemy god, they are Rooted. You then Channel to siphon their lifeforce, gaining a Health Shield and Healing over time for [12.5% INT] Health while dealing [25% INT] Physical Damage repeatedly (Every 0.3s for 1.5s) to that enemy


 	Become CC Immune and charge forward, travelling through walls and stopping on the first enemy god hit, dealing a burst of [75% STR + 75% INT], followed by a barrage of 4 attacks of [15% STR + 15% INT]

A05.name: Shoulder Charge
A05.shortDesc:
After using an ability or attacking, your Interact key becomes a Dash forward.
 • This deals 12 + 3 per Level Physical Damage. Hitting an enemy god with a damaging ability resets this ability's cooldown.

tips.title: Early Game Brawling
tips.value: Your 2nd ability is a steroid that gives your autos a protection-shred effect, so using that in tandem with your shoulder charge and abusing the fact that it procs your Bluestone Pendant will help you win most early-game trades.

aspect.description: Basic attacks while Bloodrage is active no longer provide bonus Protections or Attack Speed but instead your non-ultimate and non-passive ability cooldowns are lowered by .5s. Additionally Shoulder Charge deals more damage if it has hit an enemy god recently. Stacks decrease one at a time on expiration of buff timer.
aspect.name: **__Aspect of Rage__**

baseInformation.loreShort: Neith, the ancient Huntress and Weaver of Destiny, born of the primordial waters and the first of the Egyptian pantheon. Hers is the hand that nurtured humankind, providing food, shelter, and guidance through life's trials. Her followers are weavers, hunters, and healers, those who cherish the strength of community and the interconnected threads of fate. Now, as the gods clash and the fabric of existence frays, Neith picks up her bow and readies herself once more, determined to preserve the delicate weave she has guarded for eons, her arrows a testament to both protection and precision.
baseInformation.loreLong: When time began, there was only endless black waters. Yet, from water comes all life, and from this primordial force was born the first of Goddesses, Neith. As the waters continued to form the world and everything within it, Neith?s natural instinct to protect and nurture the people led her to champion the hunt. With meat she fed them and with hides she clothed them, but as the people she sought to protect grew old, or sick, or died on the battlefield, Neith?s compassion forced her to change her calling. With nimble fingers, the Goddess wove shrouds to shield the lost spirits of the dead on their journey to the underworld, leaving a loose thread she could trace as the ghosts ventured down the river. As time passed, more and more threads spread across the world, weaving an intricate pattern of fate that only Neith could see. Now, the war between Gods grows ever more fevered, and Neith can no longer sit idle. The threads of fate are fraying at the edges and she must once again pick up her bow and hunt, or watch the world return to the waters from whence it came.
baseInformation.shortRole: Fires Arrows, Explodes Weaves, and Snipes Globally

basic.name: Neith Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals  Physical Damage to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Broken Weave
passive.shortDesc:
Your abilities either create or detonate Weaves.

  • Detonating Weaves deals [20% STR + 20% INT] Physical Damage to nearby enemies and Roots them
  • You gain a stacking Attack Speed and Intelligence increase per Weave detonated

A01.name: Spirit Arrow
A01.shortDesc:
Fire a Spirit Arrow projectile that deals [95% STR + 75% INT] Physical Damage and Roots enemies.

  • This ability detonates Weaves that it hits
  • This arrow passes through everything

A02.name: Unravel
A02.shortDesc:
Unravel the world weaves, dealing [90% INT + 50% STR] Damage and slowing enemies enemies in an area.

  • This ability creates a Weave
  • You Heal [5% INT] Health for each enemy  hit
  • Hitting a god Heals for [5% INT] Health instead

A03.name: Back Flip
A03.shortDesc:
Deal [60% INT] Physical Damage to enemies in front of you, then Leap backwards.

  • This ability detonates Weaves that it hits

A04.name: World Weaver
A04.shortDesc:
Charge up and fire an arrow across the world, locked on to an enemy god and ignoring obstacles.

  • The arrow deals [105% STR + 90% INT] Physical Damage and Stuns 
  • You can fire the arrow before it is fully charged, but it will deal less damage

tips.title: Ultimate Tips
tips.value: Try to keep an eye on fights happening around the map and gauge whether or not an ultimate would be helpful; not just looking for last hits, but even just to help stun an enemy your teammate is ganking.

aspect.description: Broken Weave explosions no longer Root enemies and the Buff has a reduced duration however the Attack Speed gained is much higher. Additionally using World Weaver provides max stacks of Broken Weave.
aspect.name: **__Aspect of Wind__**

baseInformation.loreShort: Nemesis, the balancer of fate, who punishes arrogance and pride wherever it grows unchecked. Known for humbling even the mighty Narcissus, she ensures that no deed skirts by without judgement. Her worshipers are often those wronged by betrayal or those who pursue justice beyond the reach of mortal law. In Nemesis, they find a goddess who hears their pleas and wields her power without mercy, promising retribution for those who escape mortal consequence. Now, amid the gods' vanity-fueled warfare, she strides forth, delivering divine balance to all who defy the scales.
baseInformation.loreLong: Of arrogance and pride, the Greeks have but one word: hubris. It is a crime against Gods and Men, but a crime many commit with impunity. Even mighty Zeus is guilty. But when punishment is due, there is one from whom even the God of Thunder shrinks. She is Nemesis, the red-washed blade of Divine Retribution. Recall the tale of Narcissus, so taken by his own beauty. Proudly, his visage was flaunted, to the detriment of all. Until, at last, he was led to a pond of crystal hue where his own reflection enraptured him. So long did he remain to gaze upon this self-image that hunger claimed his life. Thus does Nemesis respond to hubris. It is consequence equal to the crime. Now, behold the war torn land, beset by the wrath of wanton Gods filling the air with blood and boasts. This battlefield is stained with hubris. Unwittingly, these foolish warriors have summoned their greatest adversary. Nemesis has come!No longer will foul deeds engender success, no hoard of wealth held in undeserving hands, no legends told when falsely earned. Nemesis will unleash a tide of retribution. Punishment for the crime of hubris is death.
baseInformation.shortRole: Dashes in, Steals Power, and Slices Through Armor

basic.name: Nemesis Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal <Damage>{Damage_Formula} Physical Damage</> to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Scales of Fate
passive.shortDesc: Hitting enemies with Basic Attacks reduces the target's Basic Attack Damage, Strength, and Intelligence while increasing yours.

A01.name: Swift Vengeance
A01.shortDesc:
Dash in a line, passing through all enemies.
    
    • Deals [35% STR] Physical Damage to all enemies hit.
    • May dash again within 2s.
    • Calculated as both dashes in SMITE 2 Calculator.

A02.name: Slice and Dice
A02.shortDesc:
Slash in a cone, dealing [40% STR + 25% INT] Physical Damage to all enemies hit. 
• Enemies in the center of the slash take [90% STR + 75% INT] Physical Damage and are Slowed.

A03.name: Retribution
A03.shortDesc:
Gain a [7.5% Max Health] Health Shield for a duration.
• Heal [40% + 5% INT] of the damage taken during the effect.
 • Reflect [40% + 20% STR] of the damage taken from gods as Physical Damage. 
 Hard Crowd Control removes the shield.

A04.name: Divine Judgement
A04.shortDesc:
Choose a single enemy god, dealing [15%-25%] of their Current Health + [65% INT + 45% STR] as Physical Damage to them and all enemies in an area around them.
    
    • Gain Movement Speed and a percentage of the enemy's Protections.
    • Reduce enemy's Movement Speed and Protections by the same amount.

tips.title: Ganking Tips (JUNGLE)
tips.value: Start fights/ganks with your Ultimate: Divine Judgement as this heavily slows the target and reduces their protections greatly, which when combined with Oathsworn-Spear's protection shred allows you to do near true-damage.

tips.title: Aspect Tips
tips.value: If you are using Nemesis' Aspect, remember that it ***AVERAGES*** the HP between you and your target. This means if you are 1% and they are 100%, you will both be set to around 50% HP. This can be really good to know if you're fighting a target that is specifically hard to kill or you get in a situation where you're being ganked by the full HP jungler.

aspect.description: Divine Judgement no longer does damage in an area but instead moves  you and the target 75% of the way to the averages of your combined health pool as long as you have less than the target. This healing is not affected by Healing Reduction.
aspect.name: **__Aspect of Justice__**

baseInformation.loreShort: Ne Zha, the Third Lotus Prince, was born not of flesh, but of divine magic and rebellion. Gifted with power even in childhood, he defied both gods and demons, wielding his flaming spear and fiery wheels with unmatched speed and grace.
baseInformation.loreLong: Ne Zha, the Third Lotus Prince, was born not of flesh, but of divine magic and rebellion. Gifted with power even in childhood, he defied both gods and demons, wielding his flaming spear and fiery wheels with unmatched speed and grace. When his strength brought calamity to those he loved, Ne Zha sacrificed himself to restore balance—only to be reborn from a lotus blossom, purified and renewed. Now a symbol of redemption and youthful courage, Ne Zha fights with playful confidence and relentless energy. Players drawn to Ne Zha thrive on precision, agility, and the thrill of striking fast and true, dancing between life and death with every spin of his flaming rings.
baseInformation.shortRole: Harass with bouncing rings, engage with Sash and Spear.

basic.name: Ne Zha Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal <Damage>{Damage_Formula} Physical Damage</> to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Righteous Spirit
passive.shortDesc: Build Righteous Spirit stacks by hitting enemies to gain Movement Speed. Stacks reset if you have not taken or dealt damage recently. Critical Strikes grant 2 stacks. Gaining a stack permanently increases Critical Strike Damage.

A01.name: Universe Ring Toss
A01.shortDesc: Throw your ring to deal [50% STR] Physical Damage, then bounce between enemies dealing [20% STR] Physical Damage, applying a Physical Protection Debuff and Slowing. The number of bounces is based on this ability's rank. After the final hit, the ring bounces into the air, granting Ne Zha an extra throw if he catches it. Subsequent hits deal 35% damage and strengthen the Slow. Each hit grants a stacking Movement Speed buff. The extra throw from catching the ring can Critically Strike. This ability stops on first target hit, and does not pass through walls.

A02.name: Flaming Spear
A02.shortDesc: Ignite your spear to gain Attack Speed and Critical Strike Chance. Heal based on your Righteous Spirit stacks.

A03.name: Armillary Sash
A03.shortDesc: Throw your Armillary Sash, Stunning the first enemy god hit and Leaping to them. Deal [80% STR] Physical Damage on arrival. This ability can Critically Strike. Ne Zha is CC Immune and cannot be targeted while Leaping to his target. The Stun is not affected by diminishing returns. This ability passes through non-God enemies, but stops on walls and enemy gods.

A04.name: Wind Fire Wheels
A04.shortDesc: Dash forward, gaining protections and becoming CC Immune. Hitting an enemy god deals [65% STR] Physical Damage and Launches them and yourself into the sky, triggering a 3-hit combo that deals [45% STR] Physical Damage. Successful timing can allow these to Critically Strike. After the combo, slam the target down dealing [65% STR] Physical Damage and Knocking Up nearby enemies. After landing, Dash back to the cast location. This ability ends early if the target dies during the combo. The Protections Buff refreshes when the combo ends.

aspect.description: Righteous Spirit no longer stacks on hitting enemies. Taking damage charges Righteous Spirit to provide a Heal over Time. Ring Toss can bounce to allies for a Movement and Attack Speed boost. Flaming Spear is now a damaging AoE attack that buffs Ne Zha's Attacks and consumes Righteous Spirit to burst heal.
aspect.name: Aspect of Righteous Flame

Flaming Spear Damage Scaling (Aspect): 6% of target Base Health + 4% of their Item Health
Enflamed Percent Health Damage Scaling: 4% of their Base Health + 4% of their Item Health

builds.notes: Bruiser Solo Lane with **Aspect of Righteous Flame**

baseInformation.loreShort: Nu Wa, guardian of heaven and earth, who sculpted humankind from clay and saved creation from collapse with her elemental stones. When the world pillar shattered, she restored balance, sacrificing her peace to protect her people. Her followers are those who revere life's harmony and the delicate balance between nature and civilization. In times of crisis, they call upon her strength to hold their world together. As the gods' chaos threatens devastation once more, Nu Wa raises her elemental stones, determined to defend her creation from the ruinous path of divine strife.
baseInformation.loreLong: When two warring Gods shattered the World Pillar, Heaven and Earth dangerously shifted, and Armageddon was unleashed. Firestorms, floods, and bloodthirsty monsters ravaged the land and stole the lives of more than can be counted. In ages past, Nu Wa, Guardian of Heaven and Earth, hand-crafted all people from clay. They were her creations, her children, her responsibility. As the Earth shuddered and broke, and the skies turned black with ash, Nu Wa forged five mystic stones, one for each of the elemental forces of nature: Earth, Water, Fire, Wood, and Metal. With the power of these stones, she slew a gargantuan tortoise, severed its legs, and raced to the broken World Pillar. One leg she used to prop the Heavens aloft and with the elemental stones, she sealed it in place. Balance between Heaven and Earth was restored, but the world was never the same. Skies cleared, but the stars had shifted. Seas retreated, but rivers drained westward. Everything leaned, but the End was averted. In the years that followed, Nu Wa and her brother, Fu Xi, took the throne of China. They ruled with wisdom and ushered in an age of recovery and prosperity, for which she is adored and worshipped to this day. Yet, the skies grow dark once more and chaos threatens to engulf the land. Gods from all Pantheons have chosen Earth as a battleground, trading blows with the same thoughtless fervor that destroyed the World Pillar so long ago. Determined to prevent such calamity from repeating, Nu Wa has summoned the elemental stones once more. For she is the Guardian of Heaven and Earth; all mankind are her creations, her children, her responsibility.
baseInformation.shortRole: Conjures Clay Soldiers, Then Shatters Them

basic.name: Nu Wa Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals  Magical Damage</> to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Strength of Wood
passive.shortDesc: Every 15s your next Basic Attack will Root all targets hit. Damaging an enemy with a Basic Attack reduces this cooldown by 1s.

A01.name: Mysterious Fog
A01.shortDesc:
Send a dense fog slowly forward that deals [50% INT] Magical Damage when it touches an enemy. Enemies that remain in the fog take additional damage every second. You and Allied Gods are Stealthed when inside the fog.

   • Gain Strength of Water, which gives you Movement Speed. Allied gods that enter the fog gain Strength of Water.

A02.name: Clay Soldiers
A02.shortDesc:
Summon Clay Soldiers to attack the chosen target. Clay Soldiers will dash at enemes within 4.8m, dealing [7.5% INT] Magical Damage, then begin to basic attack. Clay Soldiers last for 6/7/8/9/10s.

   • Gain Strength of Earth, which gives you Magical Penetration.

A03.name: Shining Metal
A03.shortDesc:
Launch a projectile that pierces all enemies and walls, dealing [75% INT] Magical Damage to enemies it passes through.

   • If the projectile hits a Clay Soldier it explodes, dealing [40% INT] damage in an area and Stunnin enemies and towers.

  • Gain Strength of Metal, causing your Basic Attacks to deal Magical Damage in an area when you hit an enemy. (Calculated as explosion + initial projectile hit)

A04.name: Fire Shards
A04.shortDesc:
Become CC Immune and ascend into the air. You and your team gain visibility on all enemy gods. Summon Fire Shards that deal [30% INT] Magical Damage to each enemy god.
   • Gain Strength of Fire, which causes your Basic Attacks to go farther and faster.
 • This ability procs items for only 50% of the item's effect.

tips.title: Tips
tips.value: Be sure to press Clay Soldiers BEFORE ulting as this gives a boost to flat penetration, making your ult hit a LOT harder. Also, BUILD ARCHMAGE'S GEM. This is over DOUBLING your ultimate's scaling. ty c:

Shining Metal has reduced Scaling while Fire Shards now scales with Basic Attack Power, can Critically Strike  at 40% reduced value and procs Basic Attack Item Effects instead of Ability Item Effects. Additionally Strength of Fire also provides Attack Speed.

passive.shortDesc: Every 15s your next Basic Attack will Root all targets hit. Damaging an enemy with a Basic Attack reduces this cooldown by 1s.

A01.name: Mysterious Fog
A01.shortDesc:
Send a dense fog slowly forward that deals [50% INT] Magical Damage when it touches an enemy. Enemies that remain in the fog take additional damage every second. You and Allied Gods are Stealthed when inside the fog.

   • Gain Strength of Water, which gives you Movement Speed. Allied gods that enter the fog gain Strength of Water.

A02.name: Clay Soldiers
A02.shortDesc:
Summon Clay Soldiers to attack the chosen target. Clay Soldiers will dash at enemes within 4.8m, dealing [7.5% INT] Magical Damage, then begin to basic attack. Clay Soldiers last for 6/7/8/9/10s.

   • Gain Strength of Earth, which gives you Magical Penetration.

A03.name: Shining Metal
A03.shortDesc:
Launch a projectile that pierces all enemies and walls, dealing [75% INT] Magical Damage to enemies it passes through.

   • If the projectile hits a Clay Soldier it explodes, dealing [40% INT] damage in an area and Stunnin enemies and towers.

  • Gain Strength of Metal, causing your Basic Attacks to deal Magical Damage in an area when you hit an enemy. (Calculated as explosion + initial projectile hit)

A04.name: Fire Shards
A04.shortDesc:
Become CC Immune and ascend into the air. You and your team gain visibility on all enemy gods. Summon Fire Shards that deal [30% INT] Magical Damage to each enemy god.
   • Gain Strength of Fire, which causes your Basic Attacks to go farther and faster.
 • This ability procs items for only 50% of the item's effect.

baseInformation.loreShort: Nut, Mistress of the Sky, stretches across the heavens, her body a canopy of stars guarding all beneath her.
baseInformation.loreLong: Nut, Mistress of the Sky, stretches across the heavens, her body a canopy of stars guarding all beneath her. She is the mother of gods, including Osiris, Set, Eset, and Nephthys, and the eternal protector of Ra as he journeys through the night. To mortals, she is both comfort and mystery, offering safe passage to souls traveling to the afterlife. Though separated from her husband Geb by the decree of Ra, her love bridges even the vast distance between earth and sky. Worshippers call upon Nut for peace, renewal, and the promise of dawn after darkness. On the battlefield, she is serene yet unyielding, her celestial power shielding allies and dispelling the chaos that dares to threaten the cosmic order.
baseInformation.shortRole: Descends from the Stars, Blasting Foes with Converging Cosmic Power

basic.name: Nut Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals Magical Damage to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Flux
passive.shortDesc:
While Strength exceeds Intelligence, Ability hits grant Stellar Flux which provides Attack Speed. Stacks decay every 3s.

Otherwise, Basic Attacks grant Astral Flux. Abilities consume this for Bonus Magical Damage and effects.

Both stack up to 10.

A01.name: Convergence
A01.shortDesc:
Your Attacks fire two additional projectiles that deal [17.5% STR + 20% INT] Magical Damage. These and your Attack pass through enemies.

Astral Flux: Consumes 4 stacks to cause targets to lose Protections.

  • This ability gains stacks if Nut dies while firing.
  • Start the match with 2 full shots of this ability.
  • If you cancel this ability without firing a shot, it goes on a 1s cooldown.

A02.name: Crashing Comet
A02.shortDesc:
Call down a comet that deals [50% STR + 85% INT] Magical Damage where it lands and [25% STR + 50% INT] Magical Damage in an area around it.

  • Enemies in the center are Rooted
  • Enemies on the edge are Trembled

Astral Flux: Consumes 4 stacks to cause the comet to echo, damaging and trembling again.

A03.name: Warp
A03.shortDesc:
Teleport in the direction you are moving and fire homing projectiles at nearby enemies that deal [30% STR + 40% INT] Magical Damage and applies a stacking slow. Subsequent hits deal 33% damage.

Stellar Flux: Resets the Cooldown of Convergence and grants 8 Ammo.
Astral Flux: Consumes 4 stacks to fire 2 additional projectiles.

  • Projectiles deal increased damage to the first target hit, and reduced damage to subsequent hits.

A04.name: Skyfall
A04.shortDesc:
Become CC Immune and ascend into the sky. Fire a massive black hole that deals [90% STR + 80% INT] Magical Damage before detonating for [45% STR + 40% INT] Magical Damage and Pulling enemies toward the center.

  • Grants 2 stacks of Flux for each God hit
  • Projectile lands after 1s, and detonates 0.5s later.

aspect.description:
Flux becomes Cosmic Flux (Mitigation, decays). Decaying past 10 stacks Heals [2% of Ally's Max Health] the lowest health ally nearby.
Scaling -25%. Convergence grants a mini version of itself to allies on God hit.
Crashing Comet Stuns and Slows. Warp grants CC Immunity to self and 1 nearby ally.

baseInformation.loreShort: Odin, All-Father of Asgard, wielder of knowledge and destiny, forever prepares for Ragnarok, the fated end of gods and men alike. It was he who sacrificed an eye for wisdom, who hung from Yggdrasil's branches to learn the secrets of runes, and who forged the world from the bones of giants. His worshipers are warriors, poets, and seekers of truth, those who respect wisdom as much as they do strength. With his ravens Huginn and Muninn by his side, Odin's watchful eye is ever upon the world, seeking any path to delay the prophesied end, knowing that even his mighty throne may one day fall.
baseInformation.loreLong: Mightiest of the Aesir, warrior and king, the All-Father is a doomed God. It seems strange to imagine a God concerned with his fate, but Odin regularly consults the volva, the seers in the North. They speak of a final war, Ragnarok, when the mightiest Gods are slain and the world is consumed by the sea. For this day, Odin constantly prepares. The All-Father can trace his lineage to the evil Frost Giant, Ymir, whom he slew with the aid of his two brothers, Vili and Ve, and with his remains forged the world. Atop Asgard, where Odin presides, he is known as the bringer of war, a sage of wisdom, and the keeper of souls slain in battle. As a warrior, Odin rides his eight legged steed, Sleipnir, and wields the mighty spear Gungnir. Accompanying him are two wolves and his two trusted ravens, Huginn (thought) and Muninn (memory), who fly around Midgard and return to Odin with all they see and hear. As a sage, Odin travels disguised as a simple wanderer, seeking wisdom. His thirst for knowledge has come at great personal cost, having been hanged from the branches of Yggdrasil, the world tree, for nine days and nights and sacrificed one of his eyes to the Well of Wisdom. As keeper of souls, Odin welcomes slain warriors to his mighty hall of Valhalla on the agreement they will aid him at Ragnarok. Favored female warriors become his elite Valkyrie that usher those worthy of entry to Valhalla. It is in preparation for the final days of Ragnarok he does all this. For the seers claim his death will come at the fangs of the great wolf Fenrir, and like anyone, Odin seeks to change his fate. Yet, never once have the volva been mistaken.
baseInformation.shortRole: Cages Foes and Shields Himself

passive.name: Path to Valhalla
passive.shortDesc: Killing a god grants a brief increase to Movement Speed, Strength, and Intelligence.

A01.name: Lunge
A01.shortDesc:
Leap forward, dealing [75% STR] Physical Damage to nearby enemies as you land.

A02.name: Raven Shout
A02.shortDesc:
Gain a [40% STR + 50% INT] Health Shield for a duration.

  • After the duration, the Shield explodes dealing Physical Damage equal to the remaining Shield Health to nearby enemies
  • If the Shield is at full HP when it explodes, it deals 15% Bonus Physical Damage instead

A03.name: Gungnir's Might
A03.shortDesc:
Charge up, pulsing damage around you twice, then throw your spear. This spear Pierces gods, but can only stun the first god hit. 

  • The pulses deal [60% INT] Physical Damage and Slow.
  • The spear deals [75% STR] Physical Damage and Stuns if fired after two pulses
  • If you refire the spear early, nearby allies get Attack Speed and this ability has a 40% reduced cooldown

A04.name: Ring Of Spears
A04.shortDesc:
Create a spear ring of walls that impedes enemies and gain a stack of your passive.

  • Enemies in the area cannot Heal, and have reduced Strength and Intelligence
  • Enemies who leave the area take [75% STR] Physical Damage and are Slowed, unless exiting through segment of the ring destroyed by basic attacks. Enemy gods who die in the ring grant a permanent stacking 5 STR and 8 INT buff.

tips.title: Ultimate Tips
tips.value: Use your ultimate to either seperate fights or cage vulnerable targets. If the support or the player with Phantom Shell isn't present or close enough to an enemy, try to take advantage and cage them to setup an easy kill.

baseInformation.loreShort: Osiris, God of the Afterlife, once ruled Egypt with unmatched wisdom and benevolence
baseInformation.loreLong: Osiris, God of the Afterlife, once ruled Egypt with unmatched wisdom and benevolence. Betrayed by his jealous brother Set, he was murdered and torn apart, his remains scattered across the land. Through the devotion of his wife, Eset, Osiris was reassembled and given new life, though incomplete. No longer king of the living, he took his throne in the underworld, guiding souls with the same fairness he once gave his people. Worshiped by those seeking justice beyond death, Osiris is steadfast and unyielding. Even broken, he rises again to challenge the darkness, a guardian of life, death, and balanve. For those who value persistence, resilience, and unwavering justice, Osiris stands eternal.

Throw your Sickle, dealing [45% STR + 35% Item Protections] Physical Damage and Slowing the first God hit.

Tether yourself to nearby enemy Gods. Tethered enemies have reduced Attack Speed and reduced Damage for 4 seconds. If still tethered when the duration expires, enemies are Stunned.

Leap to a target location, dealing [80% STR] Physical Damage and Rooting enemies. Applies Healing Prevention and Cooldown Reduction reduction to enemies hit.

Lord of the Afterlife summons a slowing, damage-dealing specter.

baseInformation.loreShort: Pele, the goddess of Fire, Volcanoes, and Dance, is both creator and destroyer, her fiery spirit as untamed as the molten earth she commands. Exiled from her homeland, Pele shaped the Hawaiian Islands with her volcanic power, her passion scorching and renewing all in its path. Her worshipers are those who honor life's fierce energy, those who respect the land and recognize its unforgiving beauty. They know Pele as both protector and vengeful force, her love fierce but her wrath formidable. As chaos brews among the gods, Pele's lava flows hot and unrelenting, defending the land she created and all who call it home.
baseInformation.loreLong: Pele is the goddess of Fire, of Dance and Wind, of Volcanoes and Violence. Those who worship Pele speak of her as Madame Pele or Tutu Pele, while others know her as Ka wahine ?ai honua, ?the woman who devours the land?. Though she is capable of great acts of destruction, and possesses the temper to provoke such acts, Pele is both destroyer and creator. It was through her mastery over the world?s molten core that she wrought the Hawaiian Islands into being, forging them from the lava that ruptured forth from the mouths of her volcanoes. They are places of immense beauty, lush and brimming with life, and by looking upon and revelling in such beauty, you offer Pele worship. Born of Kane Milohai, who wrought the earth, sky and heavens, and the Earth goddess Haumea, Pele is one of fourteen children, seven sisters and seven brothers. It is from the enmity between these siblings that Pele arrived in Hawai?i, after her exile at the hands of Kane Milohai for her fiery temper and conflict with her sister Namakaokahai, goddess of the Sea. Pursued across the oceans by Namakaikahai, Pele wrought Hawai?i into a paradise, making her home within the great calderas of the volcanoes that stand sentinel across the island chain. To worship Pele is to grant one?s devotion to a goddess of terrible power, one who can shape and render a grand, vibrant world into being at her slightest whim, and burn it to ashes just as quickly. But just as her worshipers are devoted to Pele, she devotes herself to them as a force of divine protection and care. She is a tireless defender of life and the people that dwell upon her islands, and any that seek to threaten them shall surely taste Pele?s fire.
baseInformation.shortRole: Fuels Herself, Unleashes Magma Blasts and Lightning

passive.shortDesc: When you drop below 50% health, gain increased Strength and Lifesteal. This effect has a cooldown.

A01.name: Pyroclast
A01.shortDesc:
Fires a Magma projectile that deals [60% STR] Physical Damage and grants fuel per enemy hit. At max range the Magma persists and fires additional shard projectiles back to you, dealing [15% STR] Physical Damage to enemies. 

   • The number of shards returning is dependent on the rank of this ability. 

  • Pele has 2 charges of this ability.

A02.name: Eruption
A02.shortDesc:
Deal [65% STR] Physical Damage and Knock Up enemies around you.
	At ranks 3 and 5 this area gains an additional explosion areas, which deal [45% STR] Physical Damage and a smaller Knock Up.

   • Activating this ability while using Magma Rush grants a large Movement Speed boost during the pre-fire of Eruption.

A03.name: Magma Rush
A03.shortDesc:
Repeatedly deal [5% STR] Physical Damage to nearby enemies and gain movement speed while this ability is active. Magma Rush uses Fuel for its duration and has no cooldown. 

   • Reactivate this ability to amplify the effect, expending more fuel but dealing increased [15% STR] Physical Damage and gaining additional movement speed.

A04.name: Volcanic Lightning
A04.shortDesc:
Become CC Immune then Dash forward dealing [60% STR] Physical Damage and Slowing the first enemy God hit, and firing a cone attack that deals [40% STR] Physical Damage to other enemies. 

   • For the next 10 seconds or 4 attacks, every time you basic attack an enemy, you deal reduced damage and also fire a cone attack.

tips.value: When trying to gank, if they have a movement ability, hold your ultimate until after they burn it to ensure you can chase easily to finish off the kill. Just engage with your 2-1 combo while chasing with your 3 up.

Additionally Everlasting Flame now provides Protections instead of Strength and Lifesteal, and Volcanic Lightning damage no longer has Strength Scaling.

baseInformation.loreShort: Poseidon, god of the oceans and storms, stands ever in the shadow of his brother, Zeus. Though he aided in the defeat of the Titans, his ambitions were curbed by Zeus, who took the throne for himself. Poseidon's power commands the seas and the Kraken, his rage mirrored by the tempestuous waves. His followers are seafarers, those who respect and fear the sea's vast, untamable depths. They know Poseidon's favor is fleeting and his wrath inevitable. Now, as the gods' battle threatens to engulf all realms, Poseidon's storm surges, threatening to drown even Olympus, along with its ill-gotten throne, under the weight of his unleashed tides.
baseInformation.loreLong: Deep beneath the turbulent tides of the ocean lingers a God of idle rage. What must it be like to live forever in the shadow of your brother? To be denied again and again that which you desire, despite the power of the wrathful seas at your command? So Poseidon broods on his throne at the ocean floor. As a child, Poseidon and his brother, Hades, were consumed by their father, Cronus. Powerless to escape, it was left to mighty Zeus to free them and slay their patron. Together, they defeated the last of the Titans and ushered in a new age of dominance for Olympian Gods, but their freedom, their very lives, Zeus would never let either brother forget were owed to him. Even as the heavens, the underworld, and the seas were divided among them, Zeus positioned himself as king of all, and what was Poseidon to do but be thankful he had been spared a fate in his father?s belly? So he retired to his new realm and began the pursuit of gathering worshipers. Soon rose a great ocean front city of mankind, and Poseidon presented himself as their God, but another had already come, Athena, Zeus? daughter. A competition was held ? each God would offer a gift and the people would select their sponsor. Confident, Poseidon drove his Trident, a trophy from the battle against the Titans, into the ground and a limitless spring emerged, but the people found the water salty and undrinkable. Athena extended her hand and a graceful olive tree rose from the earth. Wood, food, and oil, all from one gift. The people decided and the city was called Athens. It may be that Poseidon never desired greatness as Zeus did. Had his father never consumed him, had he never been made God of the Sea, had his brother never crowned himself King of the Gods, Poseidon might have lived contentedly, but that chance is gone. Now, with the very tides at his command, an army of sea beasts at his back, and the formidable Kraken at his call, the glowering God of the deep seeks to quench his thirsty rage and plunge all the world in endless, wrathful seas.
baseInformation.shortRole: Commands Tidal Waves and Unleashes the Kraken

passive.name: Mystic Surge
passive.shortDesc:
Accumulate Spirit by dealing damage to enemies. Upon reaching 100 Spirit you enter Mystic Surge for 5s and can use any of your first 3 abilities with no cooldown. 
Abilities used this way have a bonus effect. 
• Gain bonus Strength, Intelligence, and Attack Speed while in your Mystic Surge based on the rank of Spirit's Cadance

A01.name: Sacred Bell
A01.shortDesc:
Lob a bell, dealing [60% STR and 75% INT] Magical Damage to enemies where it lands. Warding Gusts or Reverent Steps push the Bell, dealing [60% STR and 75% INT] Magical Damage and Slowing enemies hit. 
• Mystic Surge: +20% Lob Damage and now applies a Slow. +20% Rolling Damage and now applies a Stun. The Bell itself is 1.67x the size.

A02.name: Warding Gust
A02.shortDesc:
Swipe your fan dealing [50% STR + 80% INT] Magical Damage to enemies and Knockback enemies who are close. 
• -30% Damage to enemies further away 
• Mystic Surge: +20% Damage and Knockback. Bells pushed are treated as Mystic Surge Bell

A03.name: Reverent Steps
A03.shortDesc:
Dance for a short burst of speed in any direction, then fire a projectile dealing [45% STR + 70% INT + 100% Basic Damage] Magical Damage when it ends. 
• Hitting a god grants 50 Spirit and reduces this ability's Cooldown by 25% 
• Mystic Surge: +20% Damage, dealt instantly in a line. Bells pushed are treated as Mystic Surge Bell

A04.name: Spirit's Cadence
A04.shortDesc:
Conjure a spiritual dance that pulses three times. The first two pulses deal [20% STR + 25% INT] Magical Damage. The last pulse deals [40% STR and 70% INT] Magical Damage.
• You and allies in the area gain Guarding Spirits for 4s. When taking lethal damage become Immune, Silenced, and Disarmed for 1.5s 
• Casting this ability immediately places you in Mystic Surge for 7s
• Slow stacks three times
• Gods can receive Guarding Spirits only once per Spirit Cadence use

builds.author: Mytharria
builds.notes: INT-oriented cooldown burst mage mid build

tips.title: Tips
tips.value: Using your Mystical Surge (free ability cast) to double cast your Sacred Bell into your Warding Gust is extremely strong. Also be sure to use your ultimate to save allies (or yourself) from big burst damage ultimates, like Vulcan or Kukuklan ult.

baseInformation.loreShort: Ra, the sun god and creator of life, sails daily across the heavens, guarding Ma'at—truth and order—and bringing warmth to the living and the dead. Yet his age has weakened him, and his descendants, like Eset, vie for power. Betrayed by his granddaughter, who sought his True Name, Ra's fury rekindles, and his gaze turns once more toward judgment. His worshipers—farmers, healers, and scholars—honor his light as the source of life and knowledge, fearing his wrathful stare. In this time of war, Ra's searing light may once again cleanse, purging any who would upset the cosmic order.
baseInformation.loreLong: Without the sun, there would be no light, no warmth, no life. Without Ra, there would be no sun. Ra is a guardian, the keeper of Ma?at - order and truth, and master of the sun. Each day he sails across the sky in a golden ship, and each night traverses the underworld, bringing light and warmth to the dead beneath the horizon. For those in the scorched South, Ra was the first God, creator of earth and sky and father to all other Gods. But Ra?s time is fading like a twilight sky. His descendants have grown in power and prominence and seek to replace him as the figure of authority. His own granddaughter, Eset, cleverly poisoned him by secretly summoning a viper under his feet. When he stepped on it, the serpent?s venomous bite poisoned him. Eset promised to heal Ra if he revealed to her his True Name. At first, he refused, for knowledge of his True Name would give Eset unparalleled power over him, but as the pain mounted, Ra was forced to relent. Eset kept her word and Ra was healed, though the cost may have been too high. Light can heal, but light can also burn, and the great protector is showing signs of wrath not seen since mankind?s insolence. In that rare moment of anger, Ra sent his daughter, Hathor, in the form of a lion, to devour the people. However, he ended her bloody feeding before she consumed all mankind. This time, his burning gaze is set on the other Gods. When his wrath finally cools, nothing may be left but ash.
baseInformation.shortRole: Blinds Foes, Snipes, and Heals With Solar Beams

basic.name: Ra Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals Magical Damage to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Speed of Light
passive.shortDesc:
After using an ability gain Movement Speed.
			
• This buff stacks up to 3 times

A01.name: Celestial Beam
A01.shortDesc: Summon a moving beam of intense light, dealing [105% INT] Magical Damage to enemies.

A02.name: Divine Light
A02.shortDesc:
Emanate light in the area around you that repeatedly Slows enemies before exploding dealing [70% INT] Magical Damage.

   • For a period after activation, basic attacking fires solar rays, dealing [85% INT] Magical Damage

  • The solar rays count as abilities, trigger ability effects, and pierce targets

  • Enemies looking at you gain additional Slow stacks and are Blinded by the explosion

A03.name: Solar Blessing
A03.shortDesc:
Summon a pillar of blessed light repeatedly dealing [35% INT] Magical Damage and Healing allies for  Health.

  • The light empowers you and allied gods in the area, granting Strength, Intelligence, Physical Protection, and Magical Protection

A04.name: Searing Pain
A04.shortDesc:
Blast enemies in front of you with a wave of extreme heat, dealing [110% INT] Magical Damage.

 • Deals bonus damage to gods based on their maximum Health

tips.title: Tips
tips.value: Try to hide behind cover while having vision when ulting. This will allow you to catch your targets off-guard, often giving free snipes.

aspect.description: Solar Blessing and Searing Pain only deal base damage with no scaling. In return, Solar Blessing can be placed on allied gods, attaching to them and Searing Pain Heals allies hit.
