baseInformation.loreShort: Rama, Prince of Ayodhya, is the mortal avatar of Vishnu, embodying strength, patience, and devotion. When the demon-king Ravana abducted his beloved Sita, Rama took up arms to confront Ravana, defying even Shiva's protection over the tyrant. Worshipers of Rama are seekers of justice, the honorable, and those loyal to family. They look to him as the ideal hero, one who fights for humanity's peace. With his unbreakable resolve and boundless compassion, Rama's journey is more than revenge—it is the salvation of humankind from an era of darkness.
baseInformation.loreLong: Ravana's immortal reign subjugates the people. Shiva's boon grants the demon-king invulnerability against gods and beasts. Only mortal men stand a chance of defeating him, but Ravana's might is too potent for a mere man to overcome. All seems lost. To save us, Vishnu took human form. Rama, prince of Ayodhya. At age 16 he was already the perfect embodiment of man. Strong, humble, handsome, patient, devout; he is what mankind strives to be, yet, for all our flaws, can never reach. When a neighboring kingdom, cursed by Ravana's magic, asked for aid, Rama answered, despite the misgivings of his father. With his brother Laksharma, Rama set out and slew a horde of demons. At the palace, Rama cleansed the curse by freeing the queen from stone. The city celebrated and Rama was invited to string Shiva's bow ? with the prize princess Sita's hand in marriage. Shiva's bow could not be lifted by any man, let alone strung, but Rama broke the bow in half. He and Sita wed immediately. Returning home, Rama was to be crown prince, but the jealousy of queen Kaikeyi forced Rama into exile, where he's remained these past fourteen years. Yet Rama was recently seen on the field of battle. Word is, Ravana stole Rama's wife, and Rama left exile to embrace his destiny. He must defeat Ravana, free his wife, and set mankind on a path for peace, else darkness and evil will conquer all.
baseInformation.shortRole: Cripples Foes, Snipes from the Sky with His Arrows

basic.name: Rama Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals <Damage>{Damage_Formula} Physical Damage</> to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Astral Quiver
passive.shortDesc:
Your Astral Quiver generates an Astral Arrow every 12s. 
    
    • Each Basic Attack hit reduces the time to generate an Astral Arrow by 3s. The first hit of Astral Arrow will also trigger this effect.
    • The first time a fired Astral Arrow hits an enemy, it has a chance to drop to the ground.
    • Dropped Astral Arrows last for 10s, and picking one up restores 10 Mana.
passive.shortDescAspect:
Your Basic Attacks cost Astral Arrows.
    
    • When you run out of Astral Arrows, you automatically reload.
    • You can reload at any time by using the cancel input.
    • Reload time is scaled down with Attack Speed.
    • The first time a fired Astral Arrow hits an enemy, it has a chance to drop to the ground.
    • Dropped Astral Arrows last for 10s, and picking one up restores 10 Mana.

A01.name: Astral Strike
A01.shortDesc:
Switch to a more powerful arrow that deals an additional [20% STR] Physical Damage, pierces, and Slows enemies. 
• While this ability is active, each Basic Attack consumes 1 Astral Arrow and Mana.

A02.name: Infinite Arrows
A02.shortDesc:
Focus your mind and gain increased Attack Speed.
    
    • When activating this ability, if there are any dropped Astral Arrows within 8.8m of you, automatically collect them.
    • While this buff is active, every third successful Basic Attack on an enemy god will immediately generate an Astral Arrow.

A03.name: Rolling Assault
A03.shortDesc:
Roll in the direction you are currently traveling.
 • For the next 5s after rolling, your next Basic Attack will have Haste and consume an Astral Arrow, Crippling all enemies hit and dealing an additional [55% STR] Physical Damage.

A04.name: Astral Barrage
A04.shortDesc:
Launch yourself into the air for up to 5s and shoot 3 powerful arrows at the ground that deal [75% STR] each, with each successive shot dealing increasing Physical Damage while decreasing in size.

  • Each Astral Arrow consumed reduces the cooldown of this ability by 0.4s.

tips.title: Tips
tips.value: Be sure to activate your 1st ability before attempting to clear wave, especially in the early as this greatly increases your clear and wave pressure. You can combine this with your 2nd ability to dish out insane amounts of DPS.

aspect.description: Astral Strike is always active, but you no longer regen Astral Arrows and your Attack Speed is significantly decreased. When out of Astral Arrows, reload. While Infinite Arrows is active, Astral Arrows are not consumed. Astral Arrow also has increased [15% STR] Scaling.
aspect.name: **__Aspect of Precision__**

baseInformation.loreShort: Sobek, the Crocodile God of the Nile, is hunter, guardian, and terror of the waters. Some say he shaped the Nile from his sweat; others claim he guides the worthy dead to the afterlife. Sobek's worshipers are riverfolk and warriors, those who respect the crocodile's power and the river's life-giving and deadly nature. They fear his wrath but honor his protection, knowing that their loyalty will grant them safe passage. As chaos touches his sacred Nile, Sobek rises, a primordial force bound only by his ancient river and his animalistic hunger.
baseInformation.loreLong: Hunter, guardian, destroyer, menace; the Crocodile God, Sobek, swims where the Nile takes him and owes allegiance to no one. Some say that Sobek was there when only the Dark Waters existed, so he helped create the world. Some say that Sobek made the Nile with his sweat and protects those that travel it if they pay him tribute, otherwise he destroys them. Some say that Sobek ushers the worthy dead down the Nile to the afterlife and aids in their rebirth, restoring sight and strength. Some say he is evil and hateful, ravaging those along the shores and terrorizing villages. Sobek, in truth, is all of these things and none. Sobek is not a God of good or evil, of benevolence or malice, but a God of solitary neutrality, unless disturbed; a God that tends to his mighty river and performs his responsibilities to passing souls. The rest of the time, he does what he wishes. For his protection to the living and the dead, he is to be worshipped and respected. For his power, ferocity, and ruthlessness, he is to be rightfully feared.
baseInformation.shortRole: Flings Backs Enemies, Lurks Underwater to Execute

basic.name: Sobek Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal Magical Damage to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Blessing of the Nile
passive.shortDesc:
When Sobek deals damage he gains a stack of Protections.

   • This effect stacks 3 times and lasts for 10s

  • An ability can only provide one stack per cast

A01.name: Charge Prey
A01.shortDesc:
Dash forward. The first enemy hit takes [50% INT] Magical Damage and stops the dash. Sobek displaces the enemy behind him.

   • Damaged enemies are Stunned before being thrown

  • You are Displacement Immune while dashing and CC Immune while throwing

A02.name: Tail Whip
A02.shortDesc:
Spin your tail, dealing [40% INT] Magical Damage in an area around Sobek.

   • Damaged enemies are also Displaced away from Sobek

A03.name: Sickening Strike
A03.shortDesc:
Strike in front of you. This strike deals [30% INT] Magical Damage in a cone and applies Disease, reducing healing received for 5s.

   • Enemies diseased take Bonus Magical Damage, take Reduced Healing, and can spread the disease for 2.5s

  • Gods take 5-7% of their Maximum Health over the duration of the disease. 

  • Each enemy hit by the cone reduces this ability's cooldown by 1s and Heals you, up to 4

A04.name: Lurking in the Waters
A04.shortDesc:
Submerge, Slowing enemies nearby and granting you CC Immunity, Protections, and Mana Regen. After 4 seconds, emerge dealing [80% INT] Magical Damage to enemies in range.

   • You may cancel this ability early, dealing less damage

  • Executes enemies below 10% to 25% HP, scaling with time submerged. Executed or killed enemies Heal you and grants 25 permanent bonus Health

tips.title: Tips
tips.value: Try to position in a way to tactically pluck the enemy into your teammates to setup an easy kill. Or to pluck an enemy AWAY from your teammates. You should also be ready to ult immediately after plucking if the target is around allies to avoid being bursted quickly.

aspect.description: Charge Prey now passes through all targets and knocks them forward. Sickening Strike's cooldown reduction now additionally triggers off Jungle Monsters. Charge Prey now gains an extra [35% INT] scaling and extra base damage. Sickening Strike also deals bonus damage to jungle monsters.
aspect.name: Aspect of Prey

baseInformation.loreShort: Sol, radiant Goddess of the Sun, races across the sky, pursued eternally by Skoll, the wolf son of Fenrir. Cast to the heavens with her brother Mani as punishment for their father's pride, Sol defies her fate, her light blazing defiantly across the heavens. Her worshipers are warriors of resilience, farmers of the sunlit fields, and travelers who seek her guiding warmth. With war drawing even the celestial into its wake, Sol joins the fray, her essence burning with liberation's desire. For beyond her light lies a resolve as fierce as the flames that carry her across the sky.
baseInformation.loreLong: Sol and her brother came into this world radiant, glorious beings. So enamored with them, their father arrogantly named each after a sacred heavenly body, he for the moon and she, the sun. As they grew, each child proved their namesake. Mani, of the moon, was contemplative, obedient, calm. Sol, conversely, became willful, spirited, passionate. His children were everything a father could dream, but Odin and his ilk were not pleased. For a father's hubris, Mani and Sol were punished. To the sky, they were sent, each to lead the chariot that dragged the sun or the moon. At their heels were loosed the bloodthirsty sons of Fenrir. Hungry wolves, relentless in pursuit of their prey. Resigned, Mani guided his steeds through the night, ever ahead of his hunter, but Sol did not accept her fate. Furious at the Gods, she taunts and challenges the wolf Skoll as she blazes across the sky, unleashing her spite on the only opponent she can reach. Odin has never shown signs of regret for his decision, but now war is upon the land. His attentions are turned. Sol, normally confined to her chariot has found a way to send her flaming essence to the battlefield. To the casual observer, the combat, the bloodshed, may all seem just a game to Sol, but she is driven by an age old grudge and a desire to be free.
baseInformation.shortRole: Throws Fire, Builds Heat, Unleashes a Supernova

basic.name: Sol Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals  Magical Damage to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Unstable Manifestation
passive.shortDesc:
Hit enemies with basic attacks or use abilities to gain Heat.

  • Heat grants increased Strength, Intelligence, and Attack Speed
  • At Max Heat, increase your basic attack damage. 
(Calculated here fully max heat)

A01.name: Radiance
A01.shortDesc:
Create a pool of flames and Heal yourself over time for [2.5% + 1.25% INT] of your missing Health. (6 times over 5 seconds)

  • Enemies in the pool take [30% INT + 15% STR] Magical Damage repeatedly
  • The size of the pool increases if you have more Heat when using this ability
  • You gain Heat when using this ability

A02.name: Stellar Burst
A02.shortDesc:
Your next basic attack fires a projectile that explodes on hit or upon reaching max range, then retracts, and generates Heat.

  • Both the explosion and the retraction deal [45% INT + 25% STR] Magical Damage and Slow

A03.name: Disapparate
A03.shortDesc:
Gain Movement Speed and  Slow Immunity, gaining Heat and leaving a trail of fire behind you.

  • The Trail deals [30% INT] Magical Damage
  • After 3 seconds, you become Immune and unable to attack, and the trail detonates dealing [75% INT + 40% STR] Magical Damage

A04.name: Supernova
A04.shortDesc:
Unleash flame and fury, gaining Heat and striking 8 times along a moveable ground target location.

  • Each strike deals [40% INT] Magical Damage; subsequent hits deal AFTER the 2nd hit deal 70% reduced damage, with the 2nd hit dealing 35%  reduced damage. 
  • The first time an enemy is hit, they are Knocked Back
  • You are CC Immune while using this ability

tips.title: Heat Management
tips.value: Try to use your first and second ability before boxing to maximize Heat gain, since increased Heat multplies your Basic Damage, Strength, AND Intelligence.

aspect.description: Sol's Heat provides Intelligence and Ability Damage, but no longer provides Strength, Attack Speed, and Basic Attack Damage. Sol erupts and deals [50% INT] Magical Damage around her when becomes immune in Disapparate.
aspect.name: **__Aspect of Conflagration__**

baseInformation.loreShort: Susano, storm-laden brother of Amaterasu, was cast out for his destruction and wrath. Yet, in his wanderings, he found redemption, saving mortals from the dreaded serpent Orochi and earning the blade Kusanagi. His worshipers—those who seek to master their tempests, the fierce but loyal—know Susano as both a symbol of rage and redemption. Guided by his stormy nature, he turns his destructive power against only the most deserving foes. As gods collide, Susano storms forward, his tempestuous spirit a force seeking honor, even within chaos.
baseInformation.loreLong: Like his sister, Amaterasu, Susano was born to rule, but he was tempestuous, with a flaring anger, wicked tongue, and a penchant for destruction. Their father, Izanagi, would have none of it and decreed that Susano live in the Underworld. His mood dark at this news, Susano committed heinous acts of vandalism and murder. Acts that sent Amaterasu into shameful hiding. Cast low, Susano descended to earth on his way to the Underworld. He carried only his Ten-Span Sword, and regret for his actions. What had his defilements achieved but to envelop the world in darkness? This was his nature, to broil like a storm, to frighten and destroy what stood in his path. But a storm could also herald life. At a river side, Susano ventured upon an elderly couple, weeping and cradling their young daughter. Eight children, they?d had once, but now only one remained; seven devoured by the eight-headed serpent. Susano was resolved. To atone for his crimes, to prove his father wrong, to better himself, Susano would slay this beast. Eight traps he laid, one for each head of the creature, with eight tubs of aromatic sak?as bait. Slithering forward, the Orochi spread its gruesome heads to drink from the trapped tubs. Susano cut the rope. Gates dropped. The heads were imprisoned. One by one, he chopped the eight necks, until only the wriggling tail remained. Sweeping his blade split the reptilian hide, leaving notches in his blade but revealing a brilliant sword inside: the Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi. To his sister, he gave the blade. An act of atonement, but not an apology. His true nature would never change, but his intention could. Though seen on the battlefield of the Gods, Susano?s fight is not truly against those foes, but within himself. He is a raging summer storm, struggling to focus that destructive power at evil, and atoning when the innocent are harmed.
baseInformation.shortRole: Pulls Enemies, and Slices Through Like a Storm

basic.name: Susano Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal  Physical Damage to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Storm's Edge
passive.shortDesc:
After using an ability, your next basic attack will add a Storm's Edge debuff stack to an enemy for 5 seconds.

  • At 1 stack, enemies have reduced Strength
  • At 2 stacks, enemies are also Slowed
  • At 3 stacks, enemies also take 10% increased Damage

A01.name: Storm Kata
A01.shortDesc:
Strike three times in quick succession.

  • The first attack deals [55% STR] Physical Damage to enemies in front of you.
  • The second attack spins, dealing [55% STR] Physical Damage to enemies around you
  • The third attack Dashes forward dealing [25% STR] Physical Damage

A02.name: Wind Siphon
A02.shortDesc:
Command the winds to blow, dealing [70% STR] Physical Damage to enemies in front of you.

  • Enemies in the inner area are also Pulled towards you

A03.name: Jet Stream
A03.shortDesc:
Throw a gust of wind projectile that stops on walls or the first enemy hit, creating a whirlwind. 

  • The whirlwind deals [25% STR] Physical Damage repeatedly
  • You can reactivate this ability to Teleport to the whirlwind

A04.name: Typhoon
A04.shortDesc:
Summon a typhoon which repeatedly deals [12% STR] Physical Damage as it grows in size.

  • After reaching full size, the typhoon is launched in the direction you are facing, dealing [115% STR] Physical Damage and Knocking Up enemies.
  • Reactivate this ability to fire the typhoon at its current size, dealing 1-20% less damage and losing the Knockup.

tips.title: Chasing tips
tips.value: If you're attempting you gank/fight a character with a leap or dash, apply your 3 to them and wait it out while dumping the rest of your kit to force them to jump away from you. (if you can, wait until you teleport to cast your ult)

baseInformation.loreShort: Warrior, rebel, trickster, monk, Sun Wukong is known by many titles in the East, yet it was a Journey to the West that made him a hero.
baseInformation.loreLong:
Warrior, rebel, trickster, monk, Sun Wukong is known by many titles in the East, yet it was a Journey to the West that made him a hero.

In his youth, Sun Wukong gained leadership of the monkey tribes, learned secret arts and spells from a Taoist monk, and acquired the legendary Ruyi Jingu Bang from Ao Kuang, a staff that could change size according to his whim. He defied death itself by wiping his name from the Book of Life and Death, and united many of the other monster tribes who sought to ally with him, an act that garnered the attention of the Jade Emperor.

In an effort to better control the Monkey-King, the Jade Emperor awarded him a title, but when Sun Wukong discovered his new position was little more than stable boy he grew angry and declared himself Great Sage, Equal of Heaven, and waged war on the heavens. For this treasonous act, he was finally captured and imprisoned beneath a mountain for five centuries.

At long last, the humble monk Xuanzang was seeking disciples to protect him while on his legendary Journey to the West to collect the Indian Sutras. Sun Wukong gladly agreed to serve him, but the Buddha wrapped a golden circlet around the Monkey King's head so that Xuanzang could control him. With three other disciples, they faced eighty one trials during their quest, and collected the sutras. For his service and strength, Sun Wukong was awarded Buddhahood, finally ascending to heaven and gaining immortality.

With an epic battle between Gods taking place, Sun Wukong's presence is all but assured.
baseInformation.shortRole: The Monkey King
baseInformation.subText: Smashes Foes with his Cudgel and a Trickster's Transformations

Transform into one of three animals based on aim distance. 
• Ox (Close): Knockback enemies, dealing [60% STR] Physical Damage and is immune to Knockups. 
• Tiger (Mid): Mauls and Stuns first enemy hit, dealing [60% STR] Physical Damage. 
• Eagle (Far): Fastest form.

• If all clones are killed during Stealth, the stealth ends early. 
• You are CC Immune during this ability.

baseInformation.loreShort: Thanatos, the personification of Death, harvests mortal souls with a grim satisfaction. Yet it is not mortals he craves, but the immortal. Thanatos despises the gods' defiance of death, their timeless existence a mockery to his purpose. His followers are those who accept death's certainty, the mourners, and those who find peace in life's final release. Now, as divine blood stains the earth, Thanatos stalks the battlefield, eager to test whether even gods can escape the inescapable—a fate that may render him victor over all existence.
baseInformation.loreLong: Inevitably, all mortals must die. They cling to life with fevered desperation, for death is a cold unknown, filled with uncertainty and bleak, everlasting finality. Some accept fate with grace, others are dragged, kicking and screaming, though in truth, no mortal would go to Hades willingly. They are reaped by the nightmarish God of Death, whose grim visage strikes strange against the gleeful satisfaction he feels at performing his work. Thanatos is the very face of death. Yet, to Thanatos, Mortals are easy prey. Their end is preordained, harvesting them but a game to pass the eons, but a God, a being beyond the mortal coil, now there is a creature Thanatos eyes with greedy longing. He despises the immortality other Gods covet. To him, it is a perversion and defiance of his very purpose. But can a God truly die? As the Heavens clash and immortal blood spills on the Earth, Thanatos hungers to find out. War is but the preamble to death, a battlefield of souls rent from their bodies, listlessly waiting to be taken by the God of Death. A war between Gods, a war of this magnitude can mean only one thing. Thanatos is about to be very, very busy.

baseInformation.loreShort: It is no coincidence the fields and forests surrounding the Mediterranean are haven to shepherds, farmers, and wildlife. They're guarded by aged Sylvanus, a benevolent spirit of nature, whose glittering eyes and easy grin match the vibrant life that grows in these lands.
baseInformation.loreLong:
It is no coincidence the fields and forests surrounding the Mediterranean are haven to shepherds, farmers, and wildlife. They're guarded by aged Sylvanus, a benevolent spirit of nature, whose glittering eyes and easy grin match the vibrant life that grows in these lands.
Companion to all woodland creatures, both mundane and fantastic, Sylvanus has been seen with fawn and wolf, nymph and satyr, and even trees come to life. He protects and nurtures those that respect his domain.
When the boy Cyparissus went traveling with his pet deer, Sylvanus accidently allowed wolves to hunt the peaceful animal. So stricken with grief at the loss of his pet, the child succumbed to despair and died. Wracked with guilt, Sylvanus transformed the child into the Cypress tree, whose sap weeps like the saddened child.
Old and wise, Sylvanus views the passing of time with ease and understanding. His laughter is the wind in the leaves, his touch the sprout of flowers from the earth, and his voice the call to calm the most savage beast. He is slow to anger and slower to act, which is why his presence on the battlefield is so strange. As the war between Gods spills into the idyllic realms Sylvanus keeps, he is left with no choice but fight back.
baseInformation.shortRole: Heal Friends and Pull Foes. Tend to your Flowers.

basic.name: Sylvanus Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deals Magical Damage to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Nature's Bounty
passive.shortDesc:
When your abilities hit or are deployed, a seed pod appears. Picking it up restores Mana and reduces your cooldowns. Picking up 8 spawns a pod near Sylvanus.

• Reduces cooldowns by 0.5s; at level 15 the reduction increases to 1s.

A01.name: Verdant Growth
A01.shortDesc:
Throw a pod that deals [50% INT] Magical Damage, applies Protection Reduction, and Roots enemies hit.

• If it lands without hitting, it becomes a Flower after 6s that grants nearby allies Mana Regeneration.
• The pod can be destroyed by Basic Attacks, applying Protection Reduction to the destroyer.
• Grown Flowers wilt for 6s when destroyed, still interacting with Wisps and Nature's Grasp.

A02.name: Wisps
A02.shortDesc:
Release wisps that restore [3% INT] Health to allies or deal [10% INT] Magical Damage to enemies over time. Allies gain Protections while healed.

• The first tick deals twice the normal amount of healing/damage.
• Wisps can revive Wilted Flowers or cause Living Flowers to grant Protections in an aura.
• Damage and Healing reduced by 65% vs Minions. Seeds are not spawned when hitting Minions.

A03.name: Nature's Grasp
A03.shortDesc:
Extend a vine that Stuns and Pulls the first enemy god hit.

• Sylvanus can pull Pods to him, granting a Mana aura for 30s. If Sylvanus drops below 40% Health, he consumes the Aura to gain Wisps.

A04.name: Wrath of Terra
A04.shortDesc: Slam the ground, Knocking Up enemies in an area and dealing [25% INT] Magical Damage repeatedly over time. If a Pod has been grabbed, consume it to apply Verdant Growth's Protection Reduction.

aspect.description: Attacks: +15% damage to targets debuffed by Sylvanus. Wisp's Heal, Protections, and Ultimate Knockup reduced by 40%. Nature's Grasp tethers, dealing [33% STR + 10% INT] Magical Damage, slowing, and granting Sylvanus Haste. Gain Anger (+ 6.5% Attack Speed) when Verdant Growth/Ally dies; +2 on Ultimate cast. At 4 stacks, Enrage: + 35% Attack Speed and a free Wisps or Nature's Grasp cast.

baseInformation.loreShort: The Morrigan, Goddess of Fate and War, is both weaver and warrior. With three faces—Anu, Macha, and Badb—she embodies prophecy, sovereignty, and death. Her worshipers are warriors, seers, and those drawn to the mysteries of life's end. To them, she offers strength in battle and visions of what is yet to come, but at a price. In her many forms, she incites conflict, ensuring fate fulfills its course. As gods themselves wage war, The Morrigan steps forward, guiding them to their predestined ends, a silent conductor to the symphony of their demise.
baseInformation.loreLong: Fate is an unusual thing. An unseen force predicting every action of every being. Some deities can see it, recognizing the flow of the plan. There are none, save one, that claims mastery over it, that manipulates it with such cold intent, that steers it ever toward war and the red demise of heroes. In truth, she is many Goddesses, with many names and many forms. But there is only one title. The MorriganAnu, Macha, Badb. Individually, these three Goddesses were sisters, progenitors of sovereignty, war, and death. They were caretakers of their lush green ?re, champions for armored soldiers, guides for the deceased. It is unknown what bound them together. Some whispered curse, some lover?s scorn. Some dark power or darker purposes. But now they are one. She?s been seen in many shapes. The lurid seductress, the wrinkled hag, the screaming crow, and worst of all, the washer in the river. For those who witness her cleaning their bloody armor prophesies a violent end. Subtle and manipulative, there is no war The Morrigan did not incite. No dying breath she did not collect. Even this war, a war between Gods, must secretly be of her making. So, beware those that behold her, for The Morrigan controls your fate.
baseInformation.shortRole: Copies Gods, Confuses Enemies, Causes Chaos

baseInformation.loreShort: Tsukuyomi, God of the Moon, rules the night with cold pride and ruthless precision. Where others plead for harmony, he imposes order. Wielding twin tonfa with deadly grace, he cuts down disrespect as swiftly as his enemies. Once cast from the heavens for his merciless justice, he now walks the battlefield with purpose. Worshiped by those who seek discipline, control, and fearsome strength, Tsukuyomi is the answer to chaos. For players who crave calculated power and punishing aggression, the moon has never shone brighter. The long night has come, and Tsukuyomi stands at its peak.

A01.name: Dark Moon Shuriken
A01.shortDesc:
Throw a shuriken that deals [80% STR + 70% INT] Physical Damage to enemies and sticks into the first enemy hit. If the enemy dies, it will fall to the ground.

• Retrieving the shuriken reduces this ability's cooldown.
• After casting, your next Shingetsu Attack becomes a ranged attack that steals Movement Speed.
• This ability passed through and damages non-god enemies but stops on walls and enemy gods.

A02.name: Kusarigama
A02.shortDesc:
Perform a three-swing combo with a Kusarigama.

• First two swings deal [40% STR + 25% INT] Physical Damage and disarms enemies.
• Final swing deals [90% STR + 50% INT] Physical Damage. 
• If Strength > Intelligence: Shingetsu Infused — Gain Movement Speed and final swing Stuns.
• If Intelligence > Strength: Mangetsu Infused — Final swing gains [30% INT] scaling and [3.2m] Range.
• You are Knockup Immune while casting.

A03.name: Silver Moon Caltrops
A03.shortDesc:
Scatter caltrops in an area, dealing [12.5% STR + 15% INT] Physical Damage to enemies.

• Enemies moving within the caltrops take additional damage (same as the initial hit) and are Slowed
• After casting, your next Mangetsu Attack becomes ranged and deals [30% STR + 65% INT] bonus damage. (still a basic attack)
• Checks for movement every .25 seconds for 6 seconds.

A04.name: Piercing Moonlight
A04.shortDesc:
Fire 4 piercing beams of moonlight, marking enemies, dealing [15% STR + 40% INT] Physical Damage.

• You are CC Immune and gain Damage Reduction while firing
• After firing, dash through all marked gods, dealing [55% STR + 20% INT] Physical Damage with each strike
• Each strike applies Shingetsu & Mangetsu effects and item on-hit item effects (basic attack item procs).
• Killing a god empowers both weapons, granting both ranged attacks
• This ability passes through walls and pierces all enemies.

aspect.description: When gaining Shingetsu Ranged Attack, they become Mangetsu Ranged Attacks instead. Dark Moon Shuriken no longer sticks to gods. Piercing Moonlight hits each target only once for major damage in an AoE, before returning to his casting location.
aspect.name: Aspect of Mangestu

basic.name: Tsuku Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal Physical Damage to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Shingetsu & Mangetsu
passive.shortDesc: Shingetsu & Mangetsu Damaging enemies empowers your twin tonfas, Shingetsu and Mangetsu. Empowered Shingetsu cleaves for 50% damage and restores mana. Empowered Mangetsu deals bonus Physical Damage and restores health.

A01.name: Dark Moon Shuriken
A01.shortDesc:
Throw a shuriken that deals [80% STR + 70% INT] Physical Damage to enemies and sticks into the first enemy hit. If the enemy dies, it will fall to the ground.

• Retrieving the shuriken reduces this ability's cooldown.
• After casting, your next Shingetsu Attack becomes a ranged attack that steals Movement Speed.
• This ability passed through and damages non-god enemies but stops on walls and enemy gods.

A02.name: Kusarigama
A02.shortDesc:
Perform a three-swing combo with a Kusarigama.

• First two swings deal [40% STR + 25% INT] Physical Damage and disarms enemies.
• Final swing deals [90% STR + 50% INT] Physical Damage. 
• If Strength > Intelligence: Shingetsu Infused — Gain Movement Speed and final swing Stuns.
• If Intelligence > Strength: Mangetsu Infused — Final swing gains [30% INT] scaling and [3.2m] Range.
• You are Knockup Immune while casting.

A03.name: Silver Moon Caltrops
A03.shortDesc:
Scatter caltrops in an area, dealing [12.5% STR + 15% INT] Physical Damage to enemies.

• Enemies moving within the caltrops take additional damage (same as the initial hit) and are Slowed
• After casting, your next Mangetsu Attack becomes ranged and deals [30% STR + 65% INT] bonus damage. (still a basic attack)
• Checks for movement every .25 seconds for 6 seconds.

• You are CC Immune and gain Damage Reduction while firing
• After firing, dash through all marked gods, dealing [55% STR + 20% INT] Physical Damage with each strike
• Each strike applies Shingetsu & Mangetsu effects and item on-hit item effects (basic attack item procs).
• Killing a god empowers both weapons, granting both ranged attacks
• This ability passes through walls and pierces all enemies.

baseInformation.loreShort: Thor, thunderous protector of Asgard, bears Mjolnir, the hammer forged by dwarves and unmatched in its might. Known for his boundless strength and courage, Thor is revered by warriors and defenders, those who seek to protect hearth and home. His followers look to him for protection and swift justice, trusting that Thor's storms will shield them as surely as they smite his foes. With Asgard itself threatened, Thor charges forth, lightning blazing, his hammer's fury an unwavering shield against any who would dare breach the Aesir's defenses.
baseInformation.loreLong: Thor, as famous as he is bold, is God of the thundering sky. Yet his hammer, Mjolnir is nearly as famous as he is, and Thor will not be parted from it. Odin, the All-Father, bore many children, but none as well known nor as mighty as Thor, who had the strength to move mountains and the power to call lightning from the sky. Despite these incredible gifts, Thor?s enemies were cunning, bloodthirsty, and numerous. The Frost Giants constantly threatened to invade Asgard requiring every ounce of Thor?s near limitless strength just to keep them at bay. Had the tide truly turned, Asgard might have been destroyed, but a simple prank played by the treacherous Loki ended with the creation of Mjolnir, the legendary hammer, and with it, Thor utterly crushed the Frost Giants. Sif, Thor?s wife, had hair like rays of sun through a cloudy sky, and Loki, being covetous, secretly sheared it off while she slept. Mad with fury, Thor demanded Loki fix this. So, Loki went to the dwarves of Ivaldi who forged a golden crown that would grow equally golden hair, a spear that never missed its mark, and a ship that would never sink. Proud of his treasures, Loki returned to Thor and the other Gods, gave the crown to Sif, then bragged that no greater items could be forged. The dwarf Brokk took offense to this and claimed that he and his brother could do better. Loki bet his head they could not. So, Brokk and his brother forged three items, a ring that made a duplicate every nine days, a golden pig that could fly and swim, and the hammer, Mjolnir, with the power to shatter mountains. As each item was forged, Brokk was bitten fiercely by a fly, yet only on the third did he flinch, causing the short handle on the hammer. When they were done, the dwarves took their artifacts before the Gods and decried Loki?s fly disguise. All the Gods agreed that Brokk and his brother had forged greater items. Loki, ever cunning, declared his neck was not part of the bargain, denying them his head. Mjolnir went to Thor to battle the Frost Giants. Proud and mighty, Thor beat back the evil horde and continues to defend Asgard to this day. With Mjolnir in hand, Thor is an unstoppable force that even other Gods would do well to avoid.
baseInformation.shortRole: Soars Skyward, Smashes Down With Mjolnir's Might

basic.name: Thor Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal Physical Damage to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Warrior's Madness
passive.shortDesc:
You gain Strength for each enemy god near you, up to 3. After using Berserker's Barrage or Anvil of Dawn, you gain Thunderstruck.

   • While Thunderstruck, Mjolnir deals [10% STR] bonus Physical Damage

   • Thunderstruck's damage can arc to up to 2 nearby enemies

A01.name: Mjolnir's Attunement
A01.shortDesc:
Throw Mjolnir forward, dealing [45% STR] Physical Damage to enemies as it travels. When it reaches max range it flies back to you.

   • Gods hit by Mjolnir returning to Thor take 200% damage. Minions take 100% damage

  • Reactivate while Mjolnir is traveling to teleport to and catch Mjolnir

A02.name: Tectonic Rift
A02.shortDesc:
Slam Mjolnir down creating a fissure, dealing [30% STR] Physical Damage to enemies as it erupts. Enemies hit are also Stunned.

   • The fissure blocks movement, but allows projectiles to pass through

  • Reactivate to bring the fissure down early

A03.name: Berserker Barrage
A03.shortDesc:
Spin, dealing [40% STR] Physical Damage to enemies around you. You spin twice before unleashing a final cone attack that deals [85% STR] Physical Damage to enemies infront of you.

   • You are Displacement Immune while Channeling

   • Gain 3s of Thunderstruck, causing Mjolnir hits to arc and damage nearby enemies

A04.name: Anvil of Dawn
A04.shortDesc:
Fly into the sky. Choose a location to land dealing [85% STR] Physical Damage to enemies upon landing. Enemies hit are Stunned.

  • You are CC Immune during this ability

   • You are Thunderstruck for 6s after landing, causing Mjolnir hits to arc and damage nearby enemies

tips.title: One-Shot Combo
tips.value: When ulting onto an enemy, use your hammer first while they're stunned, THEN apply your 2nd stun to confirm the returning hit of your hammer. Then finish the enemy off with your 3rd ability. This is an instant kill on almost every squishy mid-late game if they don't have a form of immunity up.

aspect.description: Thunderstruck is always active; triggering it 3 times on a god heals Thor, slowing and trembling them. Warrior's Madness grants protections instead of Strength. Mjolnir's Attunement no longer deals bonus return damage, and Tectonic Rift has no damage scaling.
aspect.name: **__Aspect of Thunderstruck__**

basic.name: Thor Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal Physical Damage to an enemy in front of you.

baseInformation.loreShort: Ullr, the silent Norse God of Glory and the Hunt, finds honor not in loud proclamations but in the perfect shot and solemn vows. While others boast, Ullr reveres the quiet triumph of single combat and the thrill of the hunt. His followers are hunters, archers, and those bound by unbreakable oaths, finding in Ullr a god who prizes skill over spectacle. Now, Ullr joins the fray not for fame, but to preserve honor itself. His arrows fly true, his purpose unswayed as he fights to defend Asgard’s very foundations.
baseInformation.loreLong: Amongst the snow-capped forests of the frigid north resides the hunter-God of Glory, Ullr. Reserved, secretive, Ullr does not represent the tankard downing, boast bellowing contemporaries of the Norse Pantheon, but pays respectful tribute to glory in all its forms. Glory is in the silence of snowfall, in the honorable victory earned from single combat, in the perfect curve of a yew bow firing an arrow true. Dotted across the lands of the Faithful, shrines to Ullr can be found in remote, secluded hideaways. It is there that glory is found in the sanctity of a vow, once sworn, never broken, and bound by a ring, no matter how crude, then buried in the earth to weather the ages of time. Though rarely spoken of, he is highly regarded, even among the Gods, for it is Ullr, not one of Odin?s sons that tends the throne of Asgard while the All Father is away. Other Gods might use the opportunity to abuse the power, but Ullr?s rule is merely an extension thereof. So much so that only the innermost circle even know that Odin is gone. All the more noticeable is it, then, that Ullr has abandoned this post to join the fray. Early on did Odin take the field of battle, leaving Ullr to manage the kingdom. So great a threat must this war be as to endanger the very survival of Asgard. Whatever the reason, Ullr comes to preserve glory, which, for him, may be the whole world.
baseInformation.shortRole: Swaps Between Melee and Ranged Attacks

basic.name: Ullr Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc:
Ullr's Basic attacks change depending on his current stance. 

 Bow: Fire a projectile that deals Physical Damage</> to the first enemy hit. 

 Axe: Deal  Physical Damage to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Weapon Master
passive.shortDesc: When you use an ability in either stance your Cooldowns in the opposite stance are reduced.

bow.name: Bladed Arrow
bow.shortDesc: Fire an arrow that passes through enemies and deals [75% STR] Physical Damage to all targets hit. No longer goes through walls.

axe.name: Thrown Axe
axe.shortDesc: Throw an axe that Stuns and deals [70% STR] Physical Damage to the first enemy it hits.

bow.name: Hail of Arrows
bow.shortDesc:
Fire a volley, dealing [85% STR] Physical Damage.

• The arrow remains in the ground for 3s

• If you activate Wield Axes while the arrow is deployed, the area around it becomes snowy for 3s, Slowing enemies.

axe.name: Glory Bound
axe.shortDesc:
Leap to deal [55% STR] Physical Damage.

• The axes remain in the ground for 3s

• If you activate Wield Bow while the axes are deployed, the axes tear open the ground, reducing Physical Protections of enemies hit.

bow.name: Wield Axes
bow.shortDesc:
Change stance, remove Disarms, and gain benefits.

Bow: Ranged Basic Attacks and additional Strength. You also gain half of the axe stance's stats.

axe.name: Wield Bow
axe.shortDesc:
Change stance, remove Disarms, and gain benefits.

Axe: Melee Basic Attacks and additional Lifesteal. You also gain half of the bow stance's stats.

tips.title: Cooldown Management Tips
tips.value: Be sure to spam your abilities in your opposite stance if you're trying to get your abilities to come back up in your original stance. For example, if you miss your axe, just use your bow stance's abilities to quickly reset it's cooldown using your passive.

baseInformation.loreShort: Vulcan, master smith of the Roman gods, shapes divine weapons and chariots in the heat of his forge. Cast from Olympus by his own mother, Juno, Vulcan's craft embodies the fire of resilience and the steel of revenge. His followers—blacksmiths, warriors, and those shaped by hardship—see in him the power to rise from ruin. Despite his skill, bitterness drives him, his forge's flame fueled as much by resentment as by artistry. Now, as war beckons, Vulcan's hammer falls, each blow a testament to strength born from scorn and tempered by the fires of his past.
baseInformation.loreLong: When the Gods of Rome ride chariots across the sky, it is Vulcan that made them. When they reach for weapons to wield in battle, it is Vulcan that forged them. When the very earth rumbles and mountains erupt in searing flame, it is Vulcan?s rage that burns. Smith of the Gods, Vulcan crafts with fire and forge as an artist would with paint and brush. As an infant, so hideous was Vulcan that his disgusted mother, Juno, cast him into the sea from the heights of Mount Olympus. So great was the fall, it smashed one of Vulcan?s legs, a wound from which he would never recover. Beneath the waves, a sea nymph, Thetis, rescued the child-god and raised him as her own. As Vulcan grew, he ventured to the surface and discovered fire and the art of smithing. His natural talents emerged and he forged wondrous jewels for his adopted mother. Summoned to a gala on Mount Olympus, Thetis wore Vulcan?s glittering gifts, but when Juno discovered Vulcan had made them, she jealously demanded he return to Mount Olympus. Angrily, Vulcan refused. In his stead, he sent a magnificent throne of gold and gems that imprisoned Juno when she sat upon it. For three days Juno was so entombed, and would have remained so had not Jupiter, Vulcan?s father, interjected. To Vulcan he offered an exchange, Juno?s freedom for the hand of Venus, the most beautiful Goddess of Love. Vulcan agreed. Though his vengeance was served cold, the fire of spite still burns in Vulcan?s chest. Unable to truly forgive, he loses himself at the forge. None of the wondrous chariots, jewelry or weapons he crafts can ever fill the hurt in his heart, but each one is worthy of a God.
baseInformation.shortRole: Hurls Magma Bombs, Bursts Enemies With Explosions

basic.name: Vulcan Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals <DamageMagical>{Damage_Formula} Magical Damage</> to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Master Craftsman
passive.shortDesc:
Vulcan has access to a variety of Mods in the item shop that affect his abilities. There are 3 sets of mods available at different levels. You can only have one Mod from each set.
    
    Hitting an enemy god with an ability grants you increased Movement Speed and Mana Regen for 5s.
passive.shortDescAspect:
Vulcan has access to a variety of Mods in the item shop that affect his abilities. There are 3 sets of mods available at different levels. You can only have one Mod from each set.
    
    Hitting an enemy god with an ability grants you increased Movement Speed and Mana Regen for 5s.
    
    Vulcan and Turrets within 8.8m gain Protections and Health Regen.

Alternator Mod (Set One - Requires Level:
Gain access to the Thumper Turret by alternating between an Inferno Cannon and Thumper each ability activation.
Thumper pulses out an attack around it every 1s that deals 20/30/40/50/60 + 8% INT Magical Damage to all enemies in the area. 
Thumper's Rank will match Inferno Cannon's. Cooldown 6s.
Dual Mod (Set One - Requires Level 1):
Gain access to the Thumper Turret in your first consumable slot. This will occupy this slot for the entire game. 
Thumper pulses out an attack around it every 1s that deals 20/30/40/50/60 + 8% INT Magical Damage to all enemies in the area. 
Thumper's Rank will match Inferno Cannon's. Cooldown 20s.
Effeciency Mod (Set One - Requires Level: Do not gain access to the Thumper ability, instead your passive grants 20 CDR and 5 + 1 per level INT in addition to the other effects when triggered.
Resonator Mod (Set Two - Requires Level: Thumper gains 150 increased HP and applies an 10/15/20/25/30% Attack Speed debuff for 3s on hit.
Thermal Mod (Set Two - Requires Level 7): Backfire and Magma Bomb deal 20 + 15% INT Bonus Magical Damage.
Shrapnel Mod (Set Two - Requires Level 7: Inferno Cannon applies additional damage over time on hit. Hits 6 times over 3s for 2/4/6/8/10 + 2% INT per hit.
Masterwork Mod (Set Three - Requires Le: Thumper gains 300 increased HP and applies a 10/20/30/40/50 Physical and Magical Protection buff to allied gods hit for 3s.
Surplus Mod (Set Three - Requires Level: You can have 2 Inferno Cannons deployed at the same time.
Seismic Mod (Set Three - Requires Level: Earthshaker Trembles and Knocks Up enemies it hits for 2s.

A01.name: Backfire
A01.shortDesc:
Fire a projectile that causes you to Dash back and deal [80% INT or 30% INT with Aspect] Magical Damage to all enemies it hits, marking the first god that is hit.
    
    • Inferno Cannons will prioritize hitting the marked god and deal 15% more damage to them for 4s.
A01.shortDescAspect:
Fire a projectile that causes you to Dash back and deal [30% INT] Magical Damage to all enemies it hits, marking the first god that is hit.
    
    • Inferno Cannons will prioritize hitting the marked god and deal 15% more damage to them for 4s.

A02.name: Inferno Cannon
A02.shortDesc:
Create an Inferno Cannon that shoots projectiles every 1s at an enemy in front of it, dealing [40% INT] Magical Damage.
    
    • It lasts until destroyed or another is placed.
    • Inherits Vulcan's penetrations but in addition to having 25% base Penetration.
    • Does not trigger item effects.

A03.name: Magma Bomb
A03.shortDesc:
Lob a projectile that explodes on impact with the ground, dealing [90% INT or 40% INT with Aspect] Magical Damage and Displacing enemies in the area.
    
    • Enemies are displaced up and away from the explosion
A03.shortDescAspect:
Lob a projectile that explodes on impact with the ground, dealing [40% INT] Magical Damage and Displacing enemies in the area.
    
    • Enemies are displaced up and away from the explosion

A04.name: Earthshaker
A04.shortDesc:
Launch a projectile that explodes on impact with the ground, dealing [110% INT] Magical Damage enemies in the area.
    
    • Deals more damage the further the projectile travels, 
    • Deals 1.65x the total damage at max travel time.

A05.name: Thumper XXV
A05.shortDesc: Create a Thumper that pulses out an attack around it every 1s that deals [10% INT] Magical Damage to all enemies in the area.

tips.title: Tips
tips.value:
Vulcan Mod Setup: Non-Aspect/Normal Vulcan: Effeciency - Thermal - Seismic 
 Aspect/Dual Turret Setup: Dual - Shrapnel - Surplus.

aspect.description:
Vulcan's Passive gains: Being near one of your turrets grants you and the turret increased Protections and Health Regeneration. 
    
    Backfire and Magma Bomb have lower Damage Scaling.

baseInformation.loreShort: Xbalanque, the Moon Hero, was born to avenge his father's death at the hands of the Lords of the Underworld.
baseInformation.loreLong:
Xbalanque, the Moon Hero, was born to avenge his father's death at the hands of the Lords of the
Underworld. Alongside his twin Hunahpu, he honed his skills in hunting and the sacred ball game before daring to challenge gods and monsters. With wit and courage, the Hero Twins braved every trial, until at last they overthrew the Lords of Death themselves. For his triumph, Xbalanque ascended to the heavens, shining as the moon, a guide to hunters and warriors who thrive through cunning as much as strength. For those who outsmart their foes and strike when least expected, Xbalanque lights the way.
baseInformation.shortRole: Hidden Jaguar Sun
baseInformation.subText: Poison Enemies and Enshroud Them in Darkness

basic.name: Xbalanque Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals Physical Damage to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Dead of Night
passive.shortDesc: Deal damage to enemy Gods to gain stacks up to 6 stacks. Each stack provides Strength or Intelligence, whichever is higher. At 3 and 6 stacks you gain an additional buff of Strength and Intelligence

Stats at max Stacks: 44 STR + 30 INT OR 20 STR + 66 INT (depending on which is higher)

A01.name: Branching Bola
A01.shortDesc:
Empower Basic Attacks to split and deal extra damage. Split projectiles deal reduced damage.
 • While this ability is active, each Basic Attack consumes Mana
• Splitting projectiles home on enemy Gods in range
• Hitting an enemy affected by Poison Darts causes the split projectiles to afflict enemies with a diminished debuff
• Split attacks proc item hit effects for 40% damage and 20% healing

A02.name: Poison Darts
A02.shortDesc:
Fire 15 dart projectiles in a cone, dealing [25% STR + 35% INT] Physical Damage and poisoning enemies hit, dealing [7.5% STR + 15% INT] per tick. 
 • Poisoned enemies repeatedly take Physical Damage and are Slowed
• Poison ticks every 0.5 seconds for 3 seconds (6 ticks total)
• Enemies can only be hit by 7 darts, with each after the first doing reduced damage
• Each dart stops on first target hit, and does not pass through walls

A03.name: Rising Jaguar
A03.shortDesc:
Dash forward then rise into the air, firing darts at nearby enemies, dealing [50% STR + 35% INT] Physical Damage. CC Immune while rising.
• While in the air, you fire darts at all nearby enemies, dealing Physical Damage
• Enemies affected by Poison Darts take additional damage
• Enemies around you when the Dash starts will still be hit
• Darts from this ability can pass through walls

A04.name: Darkest of Nights
A04.shortDesc:
Shroud enemies in darkness, limiting their vision while gaining increased attack stats and mobility.
• This ability hits every enemy God on the map
• If an enemy God leaves the area, they are affected by a decaying Slow

aspect.description: Branching Bola is no longer a toggle but provides 3 fires of ability-based projectiles. These projectiles no longer proc basic item effects, but ability ones.  Poison Darts requires only 3 hits to deal full damage. Darkest of Nights loses existing buffs but provides increased ability damage.
aspect.name: Aspect of the Nightstalker

basic.name: Xbalanque Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals Physical Damage to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Dead of Night
passive.shortDesc: Deal damage to enemy Gods to gain stacks up to 6 stacks. Each stack provides Strength or Intelligence, whichever is higher. At 3 and 6 stacks you gain an additional buff of Strength and Intelligence

Stats at max Stacks: 44 STR + 30 INT OR 20 STR + 66 INT (depending on which is higher)

A01.name: Branching Bola
A01.shortDesc:
Empower Basic Attacks to split and deal extra damage. Split projectiles deal reduced damage.
 • While this ability is active, each Basic Attack consumes Mana
• Splitting projectiles home on enemy Gods in range
• Hitting an enemy affected by Poison Darts causes the split projectiles to afflict enemies with a diminished debuff
• Split attacks proc item hit effects for 40% damage and 20% healing

A02.name: Poison Darts
A02.shortDesc:
Fire 15 dart projectiles in a cone, dealing [25% STR + 35% INT] Physical Damage and poisoning enemies hit, dealing [7.5% STR + 15% INT] per tick. 
 • Poisoned enemies repeatedly take Physical Damage and are Slowed
• Poison ticks every 0.5 seconds for 3 seconds (6 ticks total)
• Enemies can only be hit by 7 darts, with each after the first doing reduced damage
• Each dart stops on first target hit, and does not pass through walls

A03.name: Rising Jaguar
A03.shortDesc:
Dash forward then rise into the air, firing darts at nearby enemies, dealing [50% STR + 35% INT] Physical Damage. CC Immune while rising.
• While in the air, you fire darts at all nearby enemies, dealing Physical Damage
• Enemies affected by Poison Darts take additional damage
• Enemies around you when the Dash starts will still be hit
• Darts from this ability can pass through walls

A04.name: Darkest of Nights
A04.shortDesc:
Shroud enemies in darkness, limiting their vision while gaining increased attack stats and mobility.
• This ability hits every enemy God on the map
• If an enemy God leaves the area, they are affected by a decaying Slow

builds.author: Mytharria
builds.notes: Full Damage INT Build with **__Aspect of the Nightstalker__**

baseInformation.loreShort: Yemoja, goddess of rivers and mother of the Yoruba pantheon, is a healer and protector, her waters cleansing sorrow and washing away sickness. Her worshipers are those who seek solace and strength, travelers of water, and the brokenhearted. She watches over women and children, her nurturing embrace as fierce as it is gentle. But when provoked, her wrath surges as fiercely as a flood. Now, as her children cry out amidst the gods' chaos, Yemoja rises from her waters, an unstoppable current, shielding all who turn to her from the coming storm.
baseInformation.loreLong: Every river must have its beginning, and that beginning is Yemoja. The Goddess of the Ogun River is mother to the Yoruba pantheon, and from her floodwaters sprang the first mortals. She is the mistress of all rivers and the eternal guardian of the ancient mysteries of life itself. Fishermen and sailors seek her blessings, for she is the protector of all who travel on water. Mothers-to-be ask for her aid, for she is the patron spirit of women and governs everything pertaining to the feminine ? especially childbirth. After all, who better than the mother of all life to oversee its continuation?As such, she considers herself the caretaker of all that lives. She cares deeply for all of her children, be they mortal or god, comforting them and cleansing them of their sorrows. At Yemoja?s blessed touch, wounds are healed and sickness is cured. But even her kindness has its limits and should she be roused to anger, she becomes as violent and as destructive as the most turbulent of floodwaters. All who would dare stand against her are swept aside by her roaring fury. To challenge Yemoja is to challenge the river itself. But like the rivers, Yemoja has ever followed her own course. She keeps to her spheres of influence and attends only those who seek out her blessings. Content in her solitude, she has maintained her distance from the affairs of the gods, for war does not interest her and she has power enough to suit her. But the advent of Olorun has changed things and even rivers must turn with the will of the heavens. And now, as her children cry out for aid, the river rises and Yemoja rises with it.
baseInformation.shortRole: Heals, Shields, and Walls With Omi Mana

basic.name: Yemoja Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals Magical Damage</> to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Omi
passive.shortDesc:
Yemoja uses Omi to cast spells instead of Mana.

   • Starting with 7, earning up to 10. These level breakpoints are at levels 5, 10, and 15. 
 Omi Regeneration is calculated as 1 + 0.01 per Level Omi per 5s. Total rate including level increases is increased by 1% per Cooldown Rate. Reductions to Ability Cooldowns are also applied to her Omi. (Chronos' Pendant, Spear of Desolation, etc.)
	Yemoja's water based attacks and abilities can hit allies, providing them a Heal for 1 + 0.2 per Level every 0.5s for 6s.

A01.name: Bouncing Bubble (Moonstrike)
A01.shortDesc:
Toss a bubble that bounces twice, dealing [30% INT] Magical Damage and Slows enemies before exploding into smaller bubbles.
 Small bubbles deal [15% INT] Magical Damage
  • This is being calculated as both big bubble bounce hits, with the 50% DR on the 2nd hit.

When transformed into Moonstrike: The Moon strikes multiple times at a location dealing [20% INT] Magical Damage before a final strike dealing [40% INT] Magical Damage and Stunning enemies hit for 1s. This may also stun structures. 
Allies in the final strike gain increased Attack Speed and Basic Attack Damage.

A02.name: Mending Waters
A02.shortDesc:
Fire a water wave, dealing [50% INT] Magical Damage to enemies. This wave stops and bounces between allied gods, Healing them for Health and providing them a [4% Max Health] Health Shield.

  • This ability also does bonus damage to enemy Health Shields.

A03.name: Riptide
A03.shortDesc:
Create a water ring that Knock Backs anyone entering it.

  • Allies travel further and gain Movement Speed and Protections for 3s. 

  • Enemies are Slowed for 2s.

  • Placing the ring at the maximum distance reverses it's direction.

A04.name: River's Rebuke
A04.shortDesc:
Conjure two large walls of water that block enemy movement and projectiles. After a delay they crash down dealing [70% INT] Magical Damage, Trembling and Slowing enemies hit.

  • Allies impacted by the crashing waves gain Omi Healing and Moonstrike's buff

  • You gain a buff of increased Omi over time on using this ability

tips.title: Tips
tips.value: Learn the locations where you could place your ultimate to create a perfect box, this seperates a lot of good Yemoja players from bad ones. You should also look to manage your omi well enough to not run out when peeling, zoning, or setting up.

aspect.description: Mending Waters no longer affects allies, but stops and bounces on enemy gods, dealing damage [50% INT], shield damage, and providing Yemoja a stacking [6-10] Intelligence per hit. This bounce deals 65% damage after the initial hit. Additionally, Moonstrike no longer stuns gods but has increased Intelligence scaling.
aspect.name: **__Aspect of Downpour__**

baseInformation.loreShort: Ymir, the frost-giant progenitor, from whose body the very earth was forged, is said to be long dead, yet still he persists. In the icy realm of Niflheim, where frost and fire first met, Ymir once loomed, birthing giants and gods alike. His followers are those who respect the primordial, the ancient power bound in ice and stone. They see in Ymir the promise of retribution and rebirth. With the world at war, Ymir stirs, his cold breath rumbling from beneath the earth, eager to reclaim his fractured body and all that was stolen from him.
baseInformation.loreLong: As far as it is known, Ymir has long been dead. In Midgard, in the North, it is believed that Ymir?s skull is the sky, his flesh the earth, and his blood the sea. Though, these claims could merely be the boasting of proud Odin and his brothers, who take credit for slaying the first jotunn and fashioning the earth from his body parts. At the beginning of time, the Realm of Fire, Muspell, and the Realm of Ice, Niflheim met at the place called Ginnungagap; the void of space. Steam rose and froze again, and from that primordial mixture, Ymir, King of the Frost Giants took shape. From his body dripped other beings that would become the ancestors of Gods, Men, Dwarves, and of course, Giants. Even the mighty Odin must claim Ymir as his grandfather. Yet Ymir, like all giants, was an evil creature and led his Giant brethren against the Gods. After ages of warfare, Odin and his brothers slew the Giant-King and forged the world from his remains. Yet there are other whispers. Rumors that Ymir, though not a God, was still a Primeval Being, and as such cannot truly be destroyed. Perhaps he only lies dormant in Niflheim, the realm of ice, eager to return and wreak vengeance upon Odin in his kin.
baseInformation.shortRole: Freezes Foes, Then Smashes Them With His Club

basic.name: Ymir Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Deal  Magical Damage to an enemy in front of you.

passive.name: Frostbite
passive.shortDesc:
Your abilities apply Frostbite when they damage or CC enemies.

  • Frostbitten enemies take more basic attack damage [175% STR + 35% INT] from you and deal less damage to you. They also have their Attack Speed reduced by 10%.

A01.name: Ice Wall
A01.shortDesc:
Create a Wall that Knocks Back enemies on creation.

  • Reactivate to destroy the Wall early

A02.name: Glacial Strike
A02.shortDesc: Smash your club into the ground, creating a field of ice that deals [70% INT + 55% STR] Magical Damage and Slows.

A03.name: Frost Breath
A03.shortDesc: Unleash your freezing breath, Stunning enemies in front of you and dealing [55% INT] Magical Damage.

A04.name: Shards Of Ice
A04.shortDesc:
Freeze the air around you, Slowing enemies as you Channel.

  • After 3 seconds, erupt with a burst of [160% INT + 45% Protections] Magical Damage to all enemies in range
  • You may cancel this ability early, dealing 50% damage
  • You are CC Immune while using this ability

tips.title: Wall Tips
tips.value: Try to place your will to seperate fights or to zone enemies off from objectives. This can be incredibly useful if you need to block something like a Fenrir Ultimate from ulting a teammate into his team/tower.

baseInformation.loreShort: Zeus, King of Olympus, wields justice and thunder, upholding the divine order with an iron hand. He defeated Cronus, taking his rightful throne and wielding lightning against any who challenge his rule. Worshiped by kings, judges, and those who revere power, Zeus's followers know he is both a generous protector and a fearsome punisher. With Olympus threatened, his fury awakens, darkening skies and shaking mountains. Zeus will not yield to fate or foe—he is the lord of storms, and his wrath is as boundless as the sky itself.
baseInformation.loreLong: King of Gods, Zeus strives for fairness and justice, but will not hesitate to call down the thunder when there?s smiting to be done!Zeus? father, the Titan Cronus, immediately swallowed his children after birth so that none could overthrow him as he had his own father. When Zeus was born, Rhea, Zeus? mother, hid the child from Cronus and gave the Titan a rock in swaddling clothes, which he swallowed instead. In secret, Zeus grew up on the isle of Crete until he was old and strong enough to challenge his father. During their battle, Zeus slit open Cronus? stomach, allowing his swallowed brothers and sisters to escape, slaying the Titan. Then, Zeus released the Cyclops and other beings Cronus had kept imprisoned. In gratitude, the Cyclops awarded Zeus control over lightning. With his two brothers, Poseidon and Hades, the three Gods drew lots to decide command of creation. Poseidon became king of the sea, Hades the underworld, and Zeus the sky. From his golden throne atop Mount Olympus, Zeus now rules Gods and Men with his wife Hera, upholding justice, hospitality, oaths, and honesty to all under his domain. Yet, Zeus is a restless God, and while he holds high expectations for others, he does not keep those standards for himself. Chiefly, Zeus is nearly infamous for several infidelities to his wife which have resulted in dozens of children beget by other women, most famous of which are Hercules, Artemis, and Athena. Such wanton unfaithfulness is the source of great jealousy from Hera, though as King, Zeus can ultimately do as he wishes. Weather is dictated by the Thunder God?s mood. When in a fair temperament, the sky is clear, bright and warm. When Zeus? anger flares, black clouds roll in, lightning streaks the sky and thunder shakes the very ground. Lately, Zeus has been furious.
baseInformation.shortRole: Hurls Lightning Bolts and Detonates Charges

basic.name: Zeus Basic Attack
basic.shortDesc: Fire a projectile that deals  Magical Damage to the first enemy hit.

passive.name: Overcharge
passive.shortDesc:
Deal damage to apply Charges.

  • Enemies take increased basic attack damage from you for each Charge they have

A01.name: Chain Lightning
A01.shortDesc:
Hurl a bolt of lightning that arcs between enemies.

  • Deals [60% INT] Magical Damage and Slows. (Calculated as 3 hits)

A02.name: Thunderclap
A02.shortDesc:
Call down lightning, dealing [75% INT] Magical Damage to nearby enemies.

  • You also gain increased Attack Speed and Movement Speed

A03.name: Detonate Charge
A03.shortDesc:
Detonate the Charges on all enemies, dealing [30% INT] Magical Damage for each Charge.

  • Enemies at Max Charges are Stunned

A04.name: Lightning Storm
A04.shortDesc:
Conjure a lightning storm that deals [75% INT] Magical Damage repeatedly in an area. 
(Calculated as 5 hits)

tips.title: Detonate Tips
tips.value: Try to only use your detonate on the target if they have 3 charges as this will stun them and do a lot more damage than 1 or 2 charges being detonated. You can easily reach 3 charges instantly if you ult them, 2, then autoing them. This is especially useful when getting dove.
